title: Sterlite Power Strengthens North-East Grid With New Transmission Project
content:
Sterlite Power Boosts North-East Grid With 1000 MW Transmission Project.

Expert Analysis:

  • Project Commissioning: Sterlite Power commissioned the 400kV D/C Nangalbibra-Bongaigaon transmission line, an ISTS project, via SPV execution, enhancing power reliability in Meghalaya.

  • Project Scope: Spans 300 ckt km of transmission lines, including a 300 MW greenfield substation across the challenging terrains of Assam and Meghalaya.

  • Key Transmission Links: A 123km, 400kV D/C transmission line connects Bongaigaon (Assam) to Nangalbibra (Meghalaya), initially operating at 220kV, crossing the Brahmaputra River.

  • Expansion & Connectivity: The 220/132kV Nangalbibra substation (2x160MVA) allows future 400kV expansion; a 132kV D/C line (19km) links Hatsinghmari (Assam) to Ampati (Meghalaya).

  • Strengthening North-East India's Power Infrastructure: The project enables seamless transfer of 1000+ MW, improving power reliability, and accessibility, and supporting industrial, commercial, and socio-economic growth in Meghalaya and Assam.

  • Future Expansion & Economic Impact: Bridges existing power gaps while paving the way for future expansion, attracting investments, creating jobs, and reinforcing India's national grid for sustainable energy growth.

  • Enhanced Power Reliability & Timely Completion: Ensures 1000+ MW reliable power transmission from Assam to Meghalaya, decongesting networks and improving supply quality; completed ahead of schedule (Sept–Nov 2024) after Dec 2021 tariff-based bidding.
content_snippet: Sterlite Power Boosts North-East Grid With 1000 MW Transmission Project.Expert Analysis:Project Commissioning: Sterlite Power commissioned the 400kV D/C Nangalbibra-Bongaigaon transmission line, an IS
takeaways: Sterlite Power’s 1000 MW transmission project strengthens North-East India’s power grid, ensuring reliability, industrial growth, and expansion ahead of schedule.
takeaways_snippet: Sterlite Power’s 1000 MW transmission project strengthens North-East India’s power grid, ensuring reliability, industrial growth, and expansion ahead of schedule.

title: Tata Capital to issue green bonds, NCDs to raise Rs 15,000 crore
content:
Tata Capital Plans ₹15,000 Crore Fundraise through Debt and Green Bonds Ahead of IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • Fundraising Plan: Tata Capital plans to raise ₹15,000 crore ($1.72 billion) through debt, including green bonds and non-convertible debentures, with board approval.

  • Use of Funds: Tata Capital will use the ₹15,000 crore proceeds for on-lending purposes and business operations, ahead of RBI’s September listing deadline.

  • Green Bond Purpose: Funds raised by green bonds will be lent for projects in renewable energy, energy efficiency, and clean transportation.

  • Fundraising Details: On February 6th, the board approved raising ₹10,000 crore through green bonds and market-linked NCDs, and ₹5,000 crore via secured redeemable NCDs with a liquidity window facility. Tata Capital did not comment on its fundraising plans.

  • Merger Approval: In June last year, Tata Capital and Tata Motors Finance approved the merger, which has now received approval from the RBI and respective lenders.

  • RBI Designation and IPO Plans: Fitch Ratings designates Tata Capital as an 'Upper Layer' NBFC, subject to higher regulatory requirements and a public listing by September. Tata Sons, holding a 93% stake, is unlikely to dilute below 75% in the IPO.

  • Post-Merger Stakeholding: After the merger with Tata Motors Finance, TMF Holdings will hold a 4.7% stake in the merged entity.

takeaways: Tata Capital plans to raise ₹15,000 crore through debt to support operations, and green projects, and strengthen post-merger capital.
takeaways_snippet: Tata Capital plans to raise ₹15,000 crore through debt to support operations, and green projects, and strengthen post-merger capital.

title: NCDEX diversifies beyond agri, enters equity & derivatives with Rs 400-600 crore investment
content:
NCDEX Enters Equity Market with Rs 400-600 Cr Investment, Expanding Beyond Agri Trade.

Expert Analysis:

  • Market Diversification – NCDEX enters the equity and equity derivatives segment, expanding beyond its traditional agri-focused operations.

  • Board Approval – NCDEX's board approved the launch of the equity and equity derivatives segment on February 12, 2025.

  • Strategic Planning – The decision was based on a feasibility study and business plan prepared for the expansion.

  • Investment Plan – NCDEX plans to invest Rs 400-600 crore in expanding into the equity and equity derivatives segment.

  • Growth & Synergies – This move will help NCDEX diversify beyond agri-trade while also creating synergies to strengthen the agri segment.

  • Financial Alignment – The announcement coincided with NCDEX’s unaudited standalone and consolidated financial results for Q3 FY25 and the nine months ending December 31, 2024.

title: Sterlite Power Secures Record Orders, Eyes Global Expansion
content:
Sterlite Power Secures ₹2,250 Cr Orders.

Expert Analysis:

  • Milestone Order Win: Sterlite Power secured Rs 2,250 crore in new orders through its Global Products and Services (GPS) business, strengthening India's green energy transmission and global expansion.

  • Record L1 Positions: Sterlite Power achieved Rs 650 crore in L1 positions, marking a record high for the current financial year.

  • Diverse Offerings: The GPS segment includes high-performance conductors, OPGW, power cables, and specialized EPC services, reinforcing its strong presence in power transmission.

  • Strong Market Interest: CEO Reshu Madan highlights significant domestic and global demand, reflecting a thriving transmission sector.

  • Reliability & Repeat Orders: Repeated orders for 144-fiber-count OPGW cable and key EPC contracts reinforce Sterlite Power’s reputation as a trusted industry leader.

title: OYO to invest $10 million in US-based G6 Hospitality's digital assets
content:
OYO Invests $10M in G6 Hospitality to Boost Digital Growth.

Expert Analysis:

  • Digital Expansion: OYO will invest $10 million to enhance G6 Hospitality’s website and app for improved digital presence.

  • Strategic Acquisition: OYO acquires American budget hotel chain Motel 6 and Studio 6 from Blackstone Real Estate for $525 million in an all-cash deal to expand in the U.S.

  • Hotel Expansion: OYO plans to add over 150 hotels under Motel 6 and Studio 6 brands in 2025 to accelerate G6 Hospitality’s growth.

  • Digital Growth Strategy: OYO aims to grow the Motel 6 website and My6 app, targeting a 4X increase in installs before summer via Google and Microsoft partnerships.

  • Targeted Digital Campaigns: OYO’s investment will drive direct bookings through digital marketing, reducing reliance on third-party platforms and supporting G6 Hospitality’s franchise network.

  • Tech Enhancement: G6 Hospitality partners with HotelKey to upgrade technological capabilities across its properties in the U.S. and Canada.

title: SBI Funds Management invests Rs 212 cr in Ajax Engineering ahead of IPO
content:
SBI Funds Invests Rs 212 Crore in Ajax Engineering Ahead of IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investment Details: SBI Funds Management Ltd invested Rs 212 crore in Ajax Engineering via SBI Magnum Children's Benefit Fund and SBI Infrastructure Fund.

  • Share Purchase Agreement: On February 5, Jacob Hansen Family Trust transferred 27.02 lakh shares to SBI Magnum Children's Benefit Fund and SBI Infrastructure Fund at Rs 629 per share, totaling Rs 170 crore.

  • Stake Distribution: SBI Magnum Children's Benefit Fund received 19.07 lakh shares, and SBI Infrastructure Fund got 7.95 lakh shares; post-IPO, SBI Funds will hold a 2.94% stake.

  • Anchor Investor Allocation: On January 7, SBI Funds received 11.03% of anchor investment, with 3.17 lakh shares to SBI Magnum Children's Benefit Fund and 3.47 lakh shares to SBI Infrastructure Fund at Rs 629 per share, totaling Rs 42 crore.

  • Total Investment: Combined with the share purchase agreement, SBI Funds' total investment in Ajax Engineering amounts to Rs 212 crore.

  • IPO Details: Ajax Engineering's Rs 1,269-crore IPO will be open from February 10 to 12, with a price band of Rs 599 to Rs 629 per share and a market cap of Rs 7,200 crore at the upper band.

  • Offer-for-Sale (OFS): The IPO is a complete OFS of 2.01 crore shares, with Kedaara Capital offloading 74.37 lakh shares.
content_snippet: SBI Funds Invests Rs 212 Crore in Ajax Engineering Ahead of IPO.Expert Analysis:Investment Details: SBI Funds Management Ltd invested Rs 212 crore in Ajax Engineering via SBI Magnum Children's Benefit
takeaways: SBI Funds invested Rs 212 crore, securing a 2.94% stake ahead of Ajax Engineering’s Rs 1,269 crore IPO opening on February 10.
takeaways_snippet: SBI Funds invested Rs 212 crore, securing a 2.94% stake ahead of Ajax Engineering’s Rs 1,269 crore IPO opening on February 10.

title: Hexaware Technologies sets price band of Rs674-708 a share for IPO
content:
Hexaware Sets IPO Price Band at Rs 674-708 per Share.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Price Band: Hexaware Technologies Ltd has set its IPO price band at Rs 674-708 per share, with subscriptions opening on February 12.

  • Anchor Bidding & Subscription Dates: The anchor bidding process starts on February 11, with the issue closing on February 14.

  • Allotment & Stock Debut: The basis of allotment will be decided on February 17, with refunds and credit of equity shares on February 18. The stock will debut on February 19.

  • Offer-for-Sale (OFS) Details: The IPO will be an entirely offer-for-sale (OFS) of shares worth Rs 8,750 crore by promoter CA Magnum Holdings, a subsidiary of Carlyle Group Inc.

  • Revised OFS Size & Stakeholder Info: The OFS size has been reduced from the earlier proposed Rs 9,950 crore. CA Magnum currently holds a 95.03% stake in Hexaware.

  • FY24 Financial Performance: Profit and revenue grew by 12.8% in FY24 to Rs 997.6 crore and Rs 10,380.3 crore, respectively, compared to the previous fiscal.

  • FY25 Half-Year Performance: For H1 FY25, profit rose by 6% to Rs 853.3 crore, with revenue increasing by 13.6% to Rs 8,820 crore.

  • IPO Lead Managers: The IPO is managed by Kotak Mahindra, Citigroup, JP Morgan, HSBC, and IIFL Capital Services as book-running lead managers.

title: IPO-bound Hero Fincorp sees 80% rise in losses from bad loans, profit tumbles
content:
Hero Fincorp’s Bad Loan Losses Up 80%, Profit Drops Ahead of IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • Rising Loan Write-Offs: Hero Fincorp’s loan write-offs surged 80% in 9M FY25 to ₹2,180.9 crore from ₹1,214.7 crore in 9M FY24

  • Sale of Written-Off Loans: Hero Fincorp sold over 2.95 lakh written-off loans worth ₹1,874.73 crore for just ₹49.85 crore; it had written off ₹1,214.7 crore in 9M FY24, as reported by Moneycontrol on April 8, 2024.

  • Assets Under Management (AUM): Hero Fincorp’s AUM reached ₹53,642 crore as of September 30, 2024, with key segments including personal loans (30.7%), 2-wheeler finance (16.9%), MSME loans (24.1% total), corporate loans (9.7%), pre-owned car finance (7.3%), home loans (6.7%), and LAP & others (4.7%), according to ICRA.

  • Rising Gross NPA: Hero Fincorp's gross NPA rose to 5.36% as of December 31, 2024, despite efforts to reduce bad loans.

  • NPA Trend: After peaking at 7.9% in FY22 due to COVID-19, Hero Fincorp reduced NPAs to 5.38% in FY23 and 4.26% in FY24, but fresh slippages are now impacting the portfolio.

  • Profit Decline: Hero Fincorp’s profit after tax dropped 57% to ₹299.35 crore for 9M FY25 due to slippages.

  • IPO Approval Pending: The company awaits SEBI approval for its ₹3,668 crore IPO, which includes a ₹2,100 crore primary fundraising for Tier-I capital to support future lending.

takeaways: Hero Fincorp’s ₹2,180.9 crore write-offs, 5.36% NPAs, and 57% profit drop raise concerns as it awaits SEBI’s nod.

title: Boat partners with Antara senior care to enhance senior living through technology
content:
boAt Partners with Antara Senior Care to Develop Tech Solutions for Seniors.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Partnership: boAt collaborates with Antara Senior Care to develop technology solutions for senior citizens under the Max Group.

  • Specialized Devices for Seniors: boAt will create devices with fall detection, emergency alerts, location tracking, health monitoring (heart rate, SpO2, sleep), and accessibility features for hearing challenges, with remote monitoring for families and caregivers.

  • Integration with AGEasy Platform: Antara's AGEasy platform, dedicated to tackling health challenges such as joint pain, respiratory rehabilitation, and fall-risk management, will incorporate boAt's technology to create intuitive and reliable senior care solutions.

  • Leadership Statements: Sameer Mehta (boAt CEO) highlighted the partnership’s focus on technology for meaningful impact. At the same time, Ishaan Khanna (Antara CEO) emphasized using technology to promote a secure and independent senior lifestyle.

  • Addressing Growing Demand: The partnership comes as India’s aging population increases, with both companies aiming to meet seniors' unique needs, enhancing safety, health, and connectivity.

  • Company Backgrounds: Antara Senior Care (est. 2013) offers senior living and assisted care services, while boAt, under Imagine Marketing Limited, is known for its audio and wearable products with a strong Indian market presence.

  • Impact on Senior Care: The collaboration aims to redefine senior care in India, enhancing the quality of life for older adults and providing peace of mind to their families.

takeaways: boAt and Antara’s partnership targets the growing senior care market, providing tech-driven solutions to enhance safety, health, and connectivity.
takeaways_snippet: boAt and Antara’s partnership targets the growing senior care market, providing tech-driven solutions to enhance safety, health, and connectivity.

title: NSE Q3 profit surges 94% to Rs 3834 crore
content:
NSE Q3 Profit Jumps 94% to ₹3,834 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Profit Growth: NSE's consolidated PAT surged 94% YoY to Rs 3,834 crore for Q3 FY25 (October-December 2024).

  • Previous Year’s PAT: NSE posted a PAT of Rs 1,975 crore in the year-ago period (Q3 FY24).

  • Income Growth: NSE witnessed a 21% YoY growth in total income, reaching Rs 4,807 crore in Q3 FY25 (vs. Rs 3,974 crore in Q3 FY24).

  • Nine-Month Performance: For the nine months ended December 31, 2024, NSE's total income grew 30% YoY to Rs 14,780 crore, while net profit jumped 64% YoY to Rs 9,538 crore.

  • Exchequer Contribution: During the nine months of FY25, NSE contributed Rs 45,499 crore to the exchequer, comprising STT/CTT of Rs 37,271 crore, stamp duty of Rs 2,976 crore, Sebi fees of Rs 1,613 crore, income tax of Rs 2,173 crore, and GST of Rs 1,466 crore.

  • STT/CTT Breakdown: Out of the Rs 37,271 crore from STT/CTT, 61% was generated from the cash market segment, and the remaining 39% from the equity derivatives segment.

takeaways: NSE's strong growth in PAT, income, and tax contributions reaffirms its market leadership and trading volume expansion in FY25.

title: Oyo to invest £50 million in UK to expand premium hotel portfolio
content:
Oyo Invests £50 Million in UK to Expand Premium Hotel Portfolio.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investment Plan: Oyo to invest £50 million in the UK over three years, creating 1,000 jobs and expanding its premium hotel portfolio.

  • Strategic Shift: Oyo is focusing on premium inventory by securing long-term leasehold and management contracts, with talks underway for asset management deals.

  • Economic & Market Growth: Oyo's premium investment supports the UK's "Showcase Britain" initiative and builds on its successful market entry in 2018.

  • Diversification Strategy: Oyo expands premium offerings via leasehold agreements, introduces European brands to the UK, and announces plans at the Nasscom delegation.

  • Strengthening Indo-UK Trade: Oyo contributes to increasing bilateral trade from $42 billion to $50 billion, highlighting India's tech startups' global impact.

  • Current Presence: Oyo operates 200+ budget hotels across 65 UK cities, with major clusters in London, Manchester, Birmingham, Cardiff, and Brighton.

  • Premium Expansion: Oyo plans 40+ premium self-operated hotels via leasehold contracts, adding 22 more in key cities, following its 2024 SUNDAY Lansbury Heritage launch.

title: Veeda Clinical Research Refiles IPO Documents With SEBI
content:
Veeda Clinical Research Refiles ₹185 Crore IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Refiled: Veeda Clinical Research refiles its DRHP with SEBI for its IPO with a ₹2 face value shares,₹185 crore fresh issue, and 13 million shares for sale, initially submitted on September 27, 2021.

  • Strong Financial Growth: Veeda's revenue grew at a 16.18% CAGR (FY22-FY24), with TAM projected to expand from USD 65.4B to USD 110.7B by 2028.

  • Revenue & Client Growth: Veeda reported ₹305.30 crore revenue (H1 FY25) with 436 active clients, completing 580 studies in FY22, showcasing global market strength.

  • Fund Allocation: Funds to be used for ₹50 crore for equipment, ₹35 crore for Bioneeds' capital expenditure, and ₹10.89 crore for Bioneeds' debt repayment to drive growth.

  • Organic Growth Focus: ₹33 crore allocated for marketing, tech upgrades, and digital modernization to enhance efficiency and quality assurance.

  • Regulatory Strength & IPO Management: 119 successful global inspections highlight Veeda’s compliance, with Axis Capital, CLSA, IIFL, and SBI Capital managing the IPO.

  • Strategic Positioning: Veeda’s IPO aims to fund growth, strengthen its clinical research portfolio, and advance global healthcare R&D efforts.

title: Tata Capital sets stage for IPO with articles of association update
content:
Tata Capital Updates AoA Ahead of IPO.

Expert Analysis :

  • IPO Preparation: Tata Capital updates its memorandum and articles of association to align with the Companies Act, 2013, ahead of its IPO by September 2024.

  • Capital Adequacy Compliance: As an NBFC, Tata Capital must maintain minimum capital levels based on risk-weighted assets and off-balance sheet items.

  • Capital Expansion: Tata Capital may raise additional capital through rights issues to support loan growth and maintain capital adequacy ratios.

  • Regulatory Framework: Section 25 defines a “deemed prospectus” for public securities offers, while Section 42 governs private placements for restricted investors.

  • Current Shareholding: Tata Sons hold 92.83%, Tata group companies 2.46%, IFC 1.91%, Employee Welfare Trust 1.16%, and others 1.64%.

  • Merger with TMFL: Following the merger, TMF Holdings will hold a 4.7% stake in the merged entity, pending regulatory approval.

  • Board Strengthening: Tata Capital proposes Punita Kumar Sinha as an independent director, effective January 20, as part of IPO preparations.
content_snippet: Tata Capital Updates AoA Ahead of IPO.Expert Analysis :IPO Preparation: Tata Capital updates its memorandum and articles of association to align with the Companies Act, 2013, ahead of its IPO by Septe
takeaways: Tata Capital prepares for IPO, updates AoA, ensures capital compliance, merges with TMFL, strengthens governance, and awaits regulatory approvals.
takeaways_snippet: Tata Capital prepares for IPO, updates AoA, ensures capital compliance, merges with TMFL, strengthens governance, and awaits regulatory approvals.

title: OYO-owned Innov8 raises Rs 110 crore from investors at Rs 1,000 crore valuation to expand coworking business
content:
OYO-Owned Innov8 Secures Rs 110 Crore at Rs 1,000 Crore Valuation to Expand Coworking Spaces.

Expert Analysis:

  • Funding Details: OYO-owned Innov8 raised Rs 110 crore at a Rs 1,000 crore valuation by diluting 10%, with 2.7x oversubscription. Prominent family offices, including Mankind Pharma and Gauri Khan, contributed 55% of the total funds.

  • Growth Utilization: The Rs 110 crore capital will fund acquisitions, technology upgrades, partnerships, and expansion into niche segments.

  • Investor Confidence: The funds will accelerate Innov8's expansion, leveraging strong growth and highlighting investor trust in its vision and business model.

  • New Launches: Innov8 opened two centers in Navi Mumbai and Andheri, each spanning 1 lakh sq. ft. with 3,000-seat capacity.

  • Expansion Plans: Innov8 plans to double its coworking centers to 100 by 2025, expand in Tier 1 cities, and add 40 lakh sq. ft. of managed office space in the next three years.

  • Occupancy & Financial Performance: Innov8, founded in 2015, boasts over 90% occupancy and reported a PAT of Rs 62 crore in FY24, up from Rs 2.5 crore in FY23.

  • Expansion & Presence: Innov8 operates in 10 cities with over 30 centers, driven by rising demand for flexible office spaces.

title: Vikram Solar Eyes Bigger Pie In India’s Solar Manufacturing
content:
Vikram Solar Eyes Bigger Pie in India’s Solar Manufacturing.

Expert Analysis:

  • Leading Manufacturer: Vikram Solar ranks among India’s top five solar module manufacturers, with a 3.5 GW capacity (March 2024) and a 7 GW target, behind Waaree Energies, Adani Solar, and ReNew.

  • Wide Reach: Vikram Solar has a pan-India presence with 41 distributors, 64 dealers, 67 integrators, and exports to 39 countries.

  • Domestic Expansion: Vikram Solar plans a 3 GW facility in Tamil Nadu, with the eventual goal of scaling to 6 GW, a 1 GW expansion in West Bengal, and a 3 GW solar cell manufacturing.

  • Industry Growth: India’s module capacity reached 63 GW and cell capacity 13 GW in FY24; it is expected to grow to 125 GW by FY29, driven by PLI.

  • Revenue Growth: Vikram Solar achieved ₹2,524 crore in FY24, a 20.7% YoY growth from ₹2,092 crore in FY23.

  • Profitability Surge: After a ₹62.9 crore loss in FY22, the company returned to profitability with ₹14.5 crore in FY23 and ₹79.7 crore in FY24.

  • Risk Mitigation and Innovation: To reduce raw material dependency, Vikram Solar is developing a Vendor Park in Tamil Nadu and focusing on R&D for bifacial modules, HJT, battery storage, and green energy corridors.

  • Vision for the Future: Vikram Solar aims to support India’s 500 GW renewable energy target by 2030, focusing on leadership, empowerment, and inspiring future generations.

title: Apollo Green Energy set to launch IPO by Q4 2025
content:
Apollo Green Energy Plans IPO in Q4 2025.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Launch: Apollo Green Energy, the renewable energy arm of Apollo International Group, plans to launch its IPO by the last quarter (Q4) of CY2025.

  • Fund Allocation: The funds raised will primarily support solar power projects, with remaining funds allocated to battery storage, green hydrogen, and electric vehicle chargers.

  • Transition to Solar Developer: Apollo Green Energy, an EPC company with 400 MW of solar projects under construction, plans to transition into a solar developer while continuing small-scale conventional EPCs.

  • Manufacturing Expansion: By FY27, the company plans to enter solar product manufacturing, targeting a 500 MW manufacturing line.

  • Revenue Target: The company expects to achieve a Rs 11,000 crore topline by FY25, with 57% from solar, 23% from conventional EPC, and 18-19% from new ventures.

  • Green Hydrogen Partnership: Apollo Green Energy is also exploring green hydrogen, with ongoing discussions for an MoU with two major green hydrogen technology providers.

  • Conventional EPC Revenue: The firm will continue generating Rs 200-250 crore from conventional EPC projects, with a major share coming from renewable energy.

title: HDB Financial Services faces regulatory hurdles amid $1.5 billion IPO plans
content:
HDB Financial's $1.5 Billion IPO Faces SEBI Scrutiny Over Regulatory Breach and Rising NPAs.

Expert Analysis:

  • Regulatory Scrutiny: SEBI flagged HDB for issuing shares to over 50 HDFC Bank employees via private placement, potentially breaching the Companies Act, 2008.

  • IPO Implications: The $1.5 billion IPO may face delays as the issue could be classified as public, requiring SEBI clearance.

  •  Potential Penalties: If SEBI refers the issue to MCA and confirms a violation, HDB could face penalties or be required to amend its IPO filing.

  • IPO Filing and Fundraising: HDB filed its DRHP in November 2024, planning to raise Rs 2,500 crore via fresh shares, with HDFC Bank selling Rs 10,000 crore to comply with RBI rules and reduce its stake from 94.36% to below 20%.

  • ESOP vs Public Issue: In January 2008, HDB allocated 12 million shares to 410 HDFC Bank employees, including CEO Aditya Puri; the classification as an ESOP or public issue will determine whether SEBI approval is needed, potentially leading to delays.

  • Financial Performance Challenges: For Q3 FY2024, HDB reported a 20% drop in net profit to Rs 472.3 crore due to increased credit costs and a rise in NPAs to 2.25% from 2.1%, driven by stress in unsecured loans, commercial vehicle financing, and construction equipment portfolios.

  • IPO Outlook: Despite financial concerns, experts believe HDB’s IPO won’t face major roadblocks due to HDFC Bank’s strong reputation, with SEBI expected to resolve the matter through penalties or settlements after receiving feedback from MCA.

takeaways: HDB’s $1.5 billion IPO faces potential delays due to SEBI scrutiny, regulatory compliance, and financial challenges impacting investor confidence.

title: OYO to Expand Footprint with 500 Hotels in Religious Hubs.
content:
OYO will expand its footprint with 500 hotels in religious hubs. 

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Expansion into Pilgrimage Hubs: OYO plans to launch 500 hotels in key Indian pilgrimage destinations: Ayodhya, Varanasi, Prayagraj, Haridwar, and Puri.

  • Ayodhya as a Key Focus Area: Ayodhya leads OYO’s expansion with 150+ hotels, fueled by rising pilgrim demand and a 39% surge in searches after the Ram Temple inauguration.

  • Regional Hotel Allocation: Varanasi will have 100 new hotels, while Prayagraj, Haridwar, and Puri will each get 50 hotels.

  • Enhanced Guest Experiences: All hotels will operate under the ‘company-serviced’ category to ensure a consistent and superior guest experience.

  • Leveraging Spiritual Tourism Growth: India’s spiritual tourism market is projected to reach INR 59 billion by 2028, creating 140 million jobs by 2030.

title: NSE's investor base grows at an even faster pace to cross the 11 crore mark
content:
NSE's Investor Base Surges to 11 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investor Growth: NSE crossed 11 crore unique registered investors with over 21 crore accounts as of January 22, 2025.

  • Rapid Expansion: The last one crore investors were added in just over five months (roughly 6-7 months), with a 3.6-fold growth in registrations over five years.

  • Surge in Investor Confidence: Over one crore new investors added in five months, reflecting growing faith in the stock market as a wealth creation avenue, says Sriram Krishnan, Chief Business Development Officer, NSE.

  • Significant Growth in Investor Participation and Market Capitalization: The investor base grew nearly seven-fold from 1.65 crore in May 2014 to 11 crore in January 2025, while NSE-listed companies' market capitalization rose from ₹73.5 lakh crore to ₹425 lakh crore.

  • Exponential Growth in Investor Registrations: It took NSE 14 years to reach the one crore investor mark, but in the last five months, new unique investor registrations ranged between 47,000 and 73,000 daily.

  • Regional Investor Distribution: Maharashtra, Uttar Pradesh, and Gujarat account for one-third of the investor base, according to NSE's 2024 report of the past year.

  • Factors Driving Equity Culture: Increased investor awareness and strong market performance, with Nifty 50 and Nifty 500 delivering annualized returns of 14.2% and 17.8% over the last five years.

  • Rise in Equity Participation Among Younger Demographics: 20% of India's households now invest in equity markets directly, with the median age of investors at 32 and 40% under 30, reflecting a shift toward younger, tech-savvy investors.

  • Dominance of Discount Brokers in Investor Growth: NSE added 1.52 crore active investors in 2024, with over 65% (around 1 crore) coming from leading discount brokers Groww, Angel One, and Zerodha, solidifying their market dominance.

takeaways: NSE's growth to 11 crore investors reflects a rising equity culture driven by youth, brokers, and strong market confidence.
takeaways_snippet: NSE's growth to 11 crore investors reflects a rising equity culture driven by youth, brokers, and strong market confidence.

title: PharmEasy sets stage for IPO return with new strategy, boosted cash flow
content:
PharmEasy Prepares IPO Comeback with Revamped Strategy and Improved Cash Flow.

Expert Analysis :

  • IPO Revival Strategy: PharmEasy plans to relaunch its IPO this year and will present its strategy at the February board meeting, potentially including a reverse merger with Thyrocare, leveraging improved cash flow.

  • Leadership Transition:Co-founders Dhaval Shah, Dharmil Sheth, and Hardik Dedhia have stepped down from their operational roles to embark on a new venture in the consumer space, while continuing to serve on the boards of API Holdings and Thyrocare.

  • Industry Trend: PharmEasy’s IPO plans align with a broader startup trend, as companies like Boat, Ola Consumer, and Oyo renew IPO discussions after restructuring.

  • Revenue Decline: PharmEasy’s revenue in FY24 dropped by 14.7%, from ₹6,644 crore in FY23 to ₹5,664 crore.

  • Net Loss Reduction: The company halved its net loss in FY24 to ₹2,533 crore, down from ₹5,212 crore in FY23, due to reduced goodwill impairment charges from ₹2,826 crore to ₹582 crore.

  • Revenue Breakdown: PharmEasy’s medicine sales contributed ₹5,008 crore to total revenue, while lab tests and other services accounted for ₹652 crore.

  • Cost-Cutting Measures: Total expenses decreased to ₹7,255 crore from ₹8,974 crore in FY23, with employee expenses dropping sharply from ₹1,283 crore to ₹699 crore.

  • Recapitalisation and Valuation: PharmEasy's 2023 recapitalisation saw its valuation reduced by 90%, from $5.6 billion in 2021, raising ₹3,500 crore through a rights issue for debt servicing and restructuring.

takeaways: PharmEasy’s IPO revival hinges on cutting losses, leveraging recapitalisation, and navigating competition for sustainable growth.

title: Hexaware gets Sebi nod for Rs 9,950 crore IPO
content:
Hexaware Technologies Secures SEBI Approval for Rs 9,950 Crore IPO, Largest in Infotech Sector.

Expert Analysis:

  • SEBI Approval for OFS: Hexaware Technologies Ltd receives SEBI approval to raise Rs 9,950 crore through an offer for sale (OFS) of shares. This IPO will be the largest-ever in the infotech sector.

  • Employee Subscription Reservation: The offer includes a subscription reservation for eligible employees.

  • Equity Sale by CA Magnum Holdings: The OFS involves equity sale by CA Magnum Holdings, the promoter of Carlyle Group-backed Hexaware.

  • Delisting of Shares: Hexaware Technologies shares were delisted from the bourses in November 2020 after the company’s then promoters accepted the discovered delisting price of Rs 475 per share.

  • Carlyle Group Acquisition: In October 2021, the Carlyle Group, a US-based Private Equity Firm, bought Baring Private Equity Asia’s 62% stake in Hexaware for nearly $3 billion.

  • SEBI Approval Statistics: 34 companies have secured SEBI approval for IPOs, aiming to raise over Rs 41,000 crore, with 55 firms awaiting clearance to raise around Rs 1 lakh crore. Companies have a one-year window from SEBI approval to launch their IPOs.

takeaways_snippet: Hexaware Technologies' Rs 9,950 crore IPO is the largest in the infotech sector, marking a significant liquidity boost.

title: Oyo set to revive IPO plans amid record profits and controversial new policy
content:
OYO Revives IPO Plans with Strong Profits, Introduces New Policy for Unmarried Couples in Meerut.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Filing Plans: OYO plans to file its DRHP by Q1 FY25, marking its third IPO attempt, as it looks to cash in on a profitable year and restructuring efforts.

  • Ownership Reshuffle: Founder Ritesh Agarwal strengthens control, while early investors like Lightspeed Venture Partners are expected to exit.

  • Financial Performance: OYO reported a profit of ₹229 crore (US$26.49 million) for FY24 and ₹132 crore (US$15.27 million) for Q1 FY25, boosting IPO prospects.

  • Expansion Speculation: The policy is currently limited to Meerut, but there’s speculation it may expand to other cities. as OYO balances financial goals with social responsiveness.

title: Cochin International Airport rolls out fast-track Immigration; complete process in 20 seconds
content:
CIAL Launches Fast-Track Immigration with Biometric Technology for Seamless Travel.

Expert Analysis:

  • Fast-Track Immigration Program Launch: Cochin International Airport (CIAL) launched the Fast-Track Immigration (FTI) program in collaboration with the Ministry of Home Affairs.

  • Biometric Technology for Quick Processing: The program uses biometric technology to complete immigration in 20 seconds for Indian citizens and Overseas Citizen of India (OCI) cardholders .

  • Free Service for Indian Citizens and OCI Holders: Travellers can avail the service for free initially after one-time registration via the Ministry's portal.

  • Biometric Enrolment Setup: Biometrics (facial and fingerprint) enrolment is set up at CIAL’s FRRO office and immigration counters.

  • Biometric E-Gates for Efficient Processing: Eight biometric e-gates (four each in arrival and departure areas) have been installed for fast processing.

  • Implementation Across Major Airports: The system is also operational at six other major airports, including Mumbai, Chennai, Kolkata, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Ahmedabad.

  • Digi-Yatra for Seamless Boarding: Digi-Yatra, a system for seamless boarding of domestic passengers, is already in operation at CIAL.

title: Oyo’s Early Investor Lightspeed Venture Partners Cash Out Ahead of IPO
content:
OYO's Early Investor Lightspeed Venture Partners Sells Stake Ahead of IPO Plans.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investor Stake Sale and Company Valuation: Early OYO investors, including Lightspeed Venture Partners, are discussing partial stake sales, valuing the company at USD 3.9 billion 60% higher than its INR 1,457 crore funding in August 2024 but far below its USD 9 billion peak in 2021.

  • Partial Stake Sales and Returns: Peak XV Partners sold its remaining 3% stake for USD 80-90 million, adding to the USD 1.4 billion earned in 2019 from selling shares to Ritesh Agarwal.

  • Strong Financial Performance: OYO posted its first profit in FY24 (INR 229 crore) and continued profitability in Q1 FY25 (INR 132 crore), driven by strategic expansion in the US, India, and Southeast Asia, with revenue from operations at INR 5,388.7 crore in FY24.

  • Strategic Acquisition: OYO acquired G6 Hospitality for USD 525 million, expecting to boost EBITDA over INR 2,000 crore by FY26, with Motel 6 contributing INR 630 crore annually, expanding OYO’s North American footprint.

  • Fundraising Support: OYO raised INR 1,457 crore in its Series G round (August 2024) with participation from InCred Wealth, Mankind Pharma family offices, and Ritesh Agarwal contributing INR 550 crore, while celebrities like Gauri Khan, Madhuri Dixit, and Amrita Rao backed the company.

  • Family Office Investments and Stake Management: Nuvama Wealth assisted family offices in purchasing shares worth INR 100 crore (USD 4.6 billion), while investors like Lightspeed balanced liquidity with confidence in OYO’s future by partially cashing out and retaining stakes.

  • IPO Prospects:  OYO withdrew its draft red herring prospectus to focus on private fundraising but may reapply for an IPO between INR 3,450 – 5,200 crore, down from the initial INR 10,300 crore target, reflecting a pragmatic approach to market conditions. Analysts are optimistic about OYO’s profitability and strategic growth.

title: Onix Renewable Invites EOI for 1.9 GW Solar Projects in Maharashtra.
content:
Onix Renewable Launches EOI for 1,959 MW Solar Projects in Maharashtra.

Expert Analysis:

  • Project Scale and Scope: Onix Renewable has announced an Expression of Interest (EOI) for 1,959 MW of ground-mounted solar projects across 252 locations in Maharashtra.

  • Equipment Supply: The company will provide solar modules and inverters for the projects.

  • Proposal Submission Requirements: Proposals must be submitted by January 17, 2025, and an EMD of INR 50,000 per MW.

  • Execution Responsibilities: Bidders will handle EPC tasks, including design, engineering, procurement, supply, erection, testing, commissioning, and 3-year O&M services.

  • Operational Standards: Bidders must ensure a minimum Capacity Utilization Factor (CUF) of 19% over 25 years and manage interconnection with the 11kV grid network, including building transmission lines and substations.

  • Eligibility for Projects up to 50 MW: Bidders must have completed at least one 5 MW ground-mounted solar project at a single location or a total of 20 MW across multiple locations under an EPC contract.

  • Eligibility for Projects above 50 MW: Bidders must have developed 1 MW of ground-mounted solar for every additional 5 MW quoted or 3 MW for every additional 5 MW bid above 50 MW.

  • Financial Requirements: For projects up to 50 MW, bidders must have an annual turnover of INR 25 crores. For projects above 50 MW, an additional turnover of INR 2.5 crores per 5 MW is required. Bidders must also maintain a positive net worth over the last three years.

takeaways: Onix Renewable’s 1,959 MW solar EOI combines ambitious capacity, high operational standards, and rigorous financial and eligibility requirements for long-term success.
takeaways_snippet: Onix Renewable’s 1,959 MW solar EOI combines ambitious capacity, high operational standards, and rigorous financial and eligibility requirements for long-term success.

title: Tata Capital Raises $400M via First USD Bond Issue
content:
Tata Capital Debuts with USD 400M Bond Issue.

Expert Analysis:

  • Successful Fundraising: Tata Capital Limited has raised USD 400 million through its first-ever USD Fixed Rate Senior Unsecured Reg S Bond, with a 3.5-year tenor and a 5.389% interest rate.

  • Pricing Achievement: The initial price guidance of UST + 125 bps was tightened to UST + 92 bps, reflecting strong investor demand.

  • Global Investor Interest: The bond was oversubscribed over 4 times, with participation from Asia and EMEA investors, including asset managers, banks, and insurers.

  • Notable Achievements:

    • First Investment Grade-rated issuance by a Tata Group company and a private sector NBFC from India.

    • The tightest spread over US Treasuries was achieved for a 3/3.5-year USD-denominated, public, fixed-rate bond by a South and Southeast Asian BBB-issuer.

  • Key Participants: BNP Paribas, HSBC, Standard Chartered Bank, and MUFG served as Joint Global Coordinators and Joint Bookrunners, with J.P. Morgan as Joint Bookrunner.

  • Leadership Perspective: Rajiv Sabharwal, CEO of Tata Capital, highlighted strong investor confidence in the company’s credit profile and its strategic focus on diversification and strengthening liability management through this successful USD bond issuance.

takeaways_snippet: Capital's USD 400 million bond underscores global investor trust, credit strength, and effective funding diversification.

Vikram Solar Ventures into Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS).

  • U.S. Expansion Plans: The company plans a 3 GW solar cell and module factory in Colorado.

  • Feasibility Tied to U.S. Policy: The feasibility of the factory depends on U.S. policy, specifically President-elect Trump's stance on the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA).

title: RRP S4E Innovations Secures ₹9 Crore Defence Contract for Digital Spotter Scopes
content:
RRP S4E Innovations Secures ₹9 Crore Defence Contract for Advanced Digital Spotter Scopes.

Expert Analysis:

  • Contract Secured: RRP S4E Innovations has secured a ₹9 crore contract from the Ministry of Defence.

  • Scope Details: The contract is for the 101 Digital Spotter Scopes, branded as “Drishti SS-D60,” awarded through the Government e-Marketplace (GeM).

  • Enhanced Defence Capabilities: The Digital Spotter Scopes will boost the Indian Army’s surveillance, reconnaissance, border security, and tactical operations, reflecting RRP S4E Innovations' commitment to top-quality, stringent standards.

  • Advanced Features: Set for delivery between January and May, the AI-integrated Digital Spotter Scopes offer lightweight, durable design, enhancing portability, target acquisition, and situational awareness.

  • User-Friendly and High-Precision Design: The scopes feature intuitive software for easy operation, 1-micron mechanical tolerance, and a 3-nanometer optical finish for precision and reliability.

  • Self-Reliance Goal: RRP S4E Innovations aims for 95% local manufacturing by 2026, promoting self-reliance, reducing imports, and strengthening national security.

title: Tata Capital eyes first dollar bond amid India shadow bank rush
content:
Tata Capital Plans First Dollar Bond Amid Shadow Bank Offshore Rush.

Expert Analysis :

  • Bond Issuance Plan: Tata Capital is preparing its debut dollar bond sale, joining the surge of offshore debt issuance by Indian shadow lenders.

  • Benchmark-Sized Note Issuance: Tata Capital, a Tata Group unit, plans to issue a benchmark-sized note with a 3.5-year maturity to fund lending and other purposes.

  • Surge in Offshore Debt Sales: Tata Capital’s bond offering follows a surge in offshore debt sales due to RBI’s November 2023 rules restricting local bank funding for shadow lenders. Shriram Finance borrowed $1.28 billion in the largest offshore facility by an Indian financier.

  • Investor Demand: Investors are drawn to Tata Capital’s bond offering, attracted by high yields and the opportunity to diversify away from high-yielding Chinese debt.

  • Bond Sale Management and Rating: Tata Capital’s bond sale is managed by BNP Paribas, HSBC, Mitsubishi UFJ, Standard Chartered, and JPMorgan with the issue expected to receive a BBB- rating from S&P Global Ratings.

takeaways: Tata Capital’s dollar bond reflects a strategic funding shift, leveraging high-yield demand amidst RBI restrictions and investor diversification trends.
takeaways_snippet: Tata Capital’s dollar bond reflects a strategic funding shift, leveraging high-yield demand amidst RBI restrictions and investor diversification trends.

title: Nayara Energy to invest Rs 68,000 crore in foreign-led ethane cracker project
content:
Nayara Energy to Invest Rs 68,000 Crore in India's First Foreign-Owned Ethane Cracker at Vadinar Refinery.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investment Plan: Nayara Energy plans a Rs 68,000 crore investment in a 1.5 MTPA ethane cracker at its 20 MTPA refinery in Vadinar, Gujarat.

  • First Foreign Investment: This marks the first foreign investment in India’s petrochemical industry.

  • Strategic Location: The Vadinar refinery’s location offers integration benefits and convenient access to Gujarat’s port.

  • Industry Growth: India’s petrochemical capacity is expected to rise from 29.62 million tonnes to 46 million tonnes by 2030.

  • Competition in the Sector: Competitors like Gail India, Indian Oil Corporation, and Bharat Petroleum have collectively invested over Rs 1.5 lakh crore in petrochemical projects.

  • Ethane Cracker Function: Ethane crackers convert hydrocarbons like ethane into ethylene, used in synthetic rubber, adhesives, and plastics.

  • Competitor Investments: Bharat Petroleum is investing nearly USD six billion in a similar project at its Bina refinery, while Gail India has committed Rs 60,000 crore for a 1.5 MTPA cracker in Madhya Pradesh.
content_snippet: Nayara Energy to Invest Rs 68,000 Crore in India's First Foreign-Owned Ethane Cracker at Vadinar Refinery.Expert Analysis:Investment Plan: Nayara Energy plans a Rs 68,000 crore investment in a 1.5 MTP
takeaways: Nayara Energy’s Rs 68,000 crore investment strengthens its position in India’s expanding petrochemical market, joining key competitors.
takeaways_snippet: Nayara Energy’s Rs 68,000 crore investment strengthens its position in India’s expanding petrochemical market, joining key competitors.

title: Roadshows for HDB Financial’s IPO underway
content:
HDB Financial’s IPO Roadshows Begin, Targeting Rs 12,500 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Global Roadshows: HDB Financial Services has launched roadshows in the UK, US, Singapore, and Hong Kong for its IPO.

  • Roadshow and Investor Engagement: Roadshows began in late December, with HDB Financial engaging large foreign funds, including sovereign and pension funds, to gauge investment interest. Emails sent to HDFC Bank and HDB Financial remained unanswered.

  • Valuation Range: Valuation is pegged between $10-12 billion (Rs 85,000-1,00,000 crore).

  • Price Discovery and Market Cap: The final price discovery for HDB Financial’s IPO depends on investor response. Unlisted shares trade at Rs 1,225, valuing the company at Rs 97,154 crore.

  • IPO Timeline: Roadshows are ongoing, and HDFC Bank plans to launch the IPO within 2-3 weeks of receiving final comments from SEBI, aiming to complete the IPO by the end of the fiscal year. The draft red herring prospectus was filed on November 5, with plans to list by March 2025.

  • IPO Structure and Stake: The IPO includes a Rs 10,000 crore offer for sale and Rs 2,500 crore fresh issuance, representing just over 10% in the NBFC arm While HDFC Bank holds 94.36% in HDB Financial.

  • Impact on Valuations: Macquarie’s January 7 report highlighted that the RBI’s draft regulations on business overlap create an overhang, potentially affecting the valuations of HDB Financial and HDFC Bank.

title: HDB Financial overhyped on valuations, underperforms Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance on fundamentals
content:
HDB Financial overhyped on valuations, underperforming Bajaj Finance, and Shriram Finance on fundamentals.

Expert Analysis:

  • Fair Valuation Estimate: Given a 30% lower return on assets and higher performance variability, HDB Financial’s fair valuation is ₹800-₹900 per share, reflecting a 30% discount to Bajaj Finance’s multiples.

  • Diverse AUM: HDB Financial Services has an AUM of ₹1 lakh crore (H1FY25), with a portfolio of 47% vehicle finance, 21% loans against property, 15% business loans, 12% personal loans, and 29% unsecured loans.

  • Strong Growth and Narrowing NIMs: HDB achieved 29% YoY growth in FY24 and 27% in H1FY25, driven by consumer finance loans, while NIMs narrowed by 30-40 basis points from FY22-24 due to higher cost of funds.

  • Declining ROA and Rising Credit Costs: HDB Financial Services' ROA dropped to 2.6% in H1FY25 from 3% in FY24, while credit costs rose from 1.3% in FY24 to 1.9% in H1FY25, driven by higher stage-2 and stage-3 loans and stress in unsecured segments.

  • Valuation vs Peers: HDB Financial Services’ current unlisted market price of ₹1,240/share implies an FY26F P/B of 4.6x, higher than peers that deliver higher ROEs and growth but trade at lower multiples.

  • Valuation Comparison: Despite Bajaj Finance’s potential for 4% ROA and 34% growth in FY24E, it trades at 3.8x FY26E P/BV, while Shriram Finance, with 3% ROA potential, trades at a discount compared to HDB Financial Services.
content_snippet: HDB Financial overhyped on valuations, underperforming Bajaj Finance, and Shriram Finance on fundamentals.Expert Analysis:Fair Valuation Estimate: Given a 30% lower return on assets and higher perform
takeaways: HDB’s strong growth contrasts with weaker fundamentals and stretched valuations, demanding cautious optimism amid rising credit costs and narrowing NIMs.
takeaways_snippet: HDB’s strong growth contrasts with weaker fundamentals and stretched valuations, demanding cautious optimism amid rising credit costs and narrowing NIMs.

title: Polymatech Electronics Opens New Chhattisgarh Facility to Boost India’s 5G and 6G Tech Growth
content:
Polymatech Electronics Opens ₹1,143 Crore Facility in Chhattisgarh to Advance 5G, 6G Tech

Expert Analysis:

  • New Facility Location: Polymatech Electronics will establish its second Indian manufacturing facility in Nava-Raipur, Chhattisgarh following an invitation from the state government.

  • Announcement Details: The announcement was made on 23rd December 2024 by Hon'ble Chief Minister Shri Vishnu Deo Sai at the Chhattisgarh Investor Connect 2024 event in New Delhi.

  • Collaboration and Technology: Polymatech, in collaboration with Simply RF (Cardiff, UK), will produce advanced semiconductor chips for frequencies between 5.925 GHz and 13.75 GHz (Nodes N102-104).

  • Manufacturing and Packaging: Advanced chip packaging will be done at the new Raipur facility, with Chip fabrication outsourced until Polymatech’s foundry in Grenoble, France is operational.

  • Investment and Impact: The ₹1,143 crore investment will showcase India’s capabilities in the global semiconductor space.

  • Government Support: The Government of Chhattisgarh offers a 40% capital subsidy under its industrial policy, independent of the Government of India’s incentives, which Polymatech plans to avail.

  • Focus on 5G and 6G: The new facility will manufacture 5G and 6G base stations using Gallium Nitride (GaN) technology, with Doherty Architecture for superior performance and efficiency.

takeaways: Polymatech's ₹1,143 crore investment in Nava-Raipur, with a 40% capital subsidy, strengthens India's position in semiconductor and telecom tech.
takeaways_snippet: Polymatech's ₹1,143 crore investment in Nava-Raipur, with a 40% capital subsidy, strengthens India's position in semiconductor and telecom tech.

title: Goa Shipyard Ltd launches two indigenous fast patrol vessels for Indian Coast Guard
content:
Goa Shipyard Ltd Launches Two Indigenous Fast Patrol Vessels for Indian Coast Guard.

Expert Analysis:

  • Launch of FPVs: Goa Shipyard Limited (GSL) launched two indigenously designed Fast Patrol Vessels (FPVs) – Amulya and Akshay – for the Indian Coast Guard.

  • Commitment to Excellence: This launch marks GSL’s commitment to technological and operational excellence.

  • Revenue Growth: GSL has achieved a remarkable 100% increase in Gross Revenue, surpassing Rs 2,000 Crore.

  • Key Attendees: The launch was attended by Sanjeev Kumar, IAS, Secretary (Defence Production), IG HK Sharma, TM, DDG (M&M), and key stakeholders.

  • Fleet Expansion: GSL is constructing a fleet of eight FPVs for the Indian Coast Guard, with two more vessels launching in October 2024.

  • Indigenous Focus: The vessels are designed in-house by GSL, emphasizing the Atmanirbhar Bharat initiative and indigenous defense production.

  • Modernization Achievement: The simultaneous launch of two vessels using the Ship-Lift System highlights GSL's modernization and advanced capabilities.

  • Resilience and Collaboration: Secretary (DP) praised GSL’s resilience in overcoming challenges and its enduring collaboration with the Indian Coast Guard.

title: Sebi withdraws recognition to Indian Commodity Exchange, paves way for ICEX to exit as an exchange
content:
Sebi Revokes Recognition of ICEX, Facilitating Exit as a Stock Exchange.

Expert Analysis:

  • Sebi Withdraws Recognition: Sebi withdrew recognition granted to ICEX, allowing its exit as a stock exchange.

  • Revocation Due to Non-Compliance: In 2022, Sebi revoked ICEX's recognition for "non-compliance with the minimum net worth requirement."

  • Challenges in Fundraising: ICEX attempted to raise funds but faced restrictions due to shareholding norms (maximum 5% ownership), making it unattractive to investors.

  • Appeal to SAT: In 2022, ICEX appealed to the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT), which set aside Sebi's order, subject to ICEX raising requisite funds and meeting compliance conditions.

takeaways: ICEX exits as a stock exchange after failing to meet capital requirements, shareholding norms, and compliance conditions, despite appeal.
takeaways_snippet: ICEX exits as a stock exchange after failing to meet capital requirements, shareholding norms, and compliance conditions, despite appeal.

title: NSE's market cap increased by 21 pc to Rs 438 lakh crore in 2024
content:
NSE Achieved Historic ₹438.9 Lakh Crore Market Cap in 2024.

Expert Analysis :

  • Market Cap Growth: In 2024, the National Stock Exchange (NSE) achieved a 21% YoY market cap growth, reaching ₹438.9 lakh crore ($5.13 trillion) as of December 31, 2024, from ₹361.05 lakh crore ($4.34 trillion) on December 29, 2023.

  • Total Listings: A total of 301 companies were listed on the NSE, including 90 mainboard companies 178 SMEs, and 33 directly listed companies.

  • Mainboard IPO Performance: Mainboard companies raised ₹1.59 lakh crore, with an average IPO size of ₹1,772 crore.

  • SME IPO Performance: SME companies raised ₹7,348 crore, with an average IPO size of ₹41 crore.

  • NSE Benchmark Index Performance: On December 31, 2024, NSE's benchmark index closed at 23,645, up from 21,731 on December 29, 2023.

  • Index Performance: NSE indices performed well, with Nifty Next 50 delivering 27.4% returns, Nifty Smallcap 50 at 25.3%, and Nifty Midcap 50 at 21.5%.

  • Market Cap-to-GDP Ratio: NSE's market cap-to-GDP ratio increased by 29% from 117% in 2023 to 145% in 2024.

takeaways: NSE's 2024 highlights include 301 listings, ₹438.9 lakh crore market cap, ₹1.59 lakh crore raised via IPOs, and strong index returns.

  • Green Hydrogen Technology: Collaborating with international partners to acquire key technologies like electrolyzers and fuel cells for green hydrogen production.

  • FY25 Target & IPO Plans: Apollo Green aims for a Rs 1,100 crore topline in FY25 and plans to go public by Diwali 2025.

title: Tata Capital to raise up to $2 billion from overseas market
content:
Tata Capital Plans to Raise $2 Billion in Global Debt Market.

Expert Analysis:

  • Tata Capital's Global Debt Market Move: After merging with Tata Motors' NBFC subsidiary, Tata Capital has become the 12th largest non-bank player by AUM and is raising $2 billion through its medium-term notes (MTN) program.

  • Credit Rating and S&P Details: S&P has assigned a BBB- rating to the MTN issue, equalizing ratings on senior secured and unsecured notes with the issuer's long-term credit rating due to prudential regulation.

  • Senior Unsecured Notes: Senior unsecured notes will be direct, unconditional, and unsubordinated, ranking equally with other senior unsecured obligations.

  • Funding Mix (March 2024): Tata Capital had a wide funding base from multiple lenders, including NCDs (39%), term loans (42%), and ECBs (7%) from Tata Capital’s diversified funding base.

  • Financial Performance (FY24): The company reported ₹13,309 crore in revenue and ₹2,492 crore in net profit for FY24.

title: SBI Mutual Fund suspends fresh subscription in its international fund
content:
SBI Mutual Fund Suspends Fresh Subscriptions to International Fund.

Expert Analysis:

  • Suspension of Fresh Subscriptions: SBI Mutual Fund suspends fresh subscriptions to the SBI International Access - US Equity FoF.

  • Reason for Suspension: The suspension is due to the need to avoid breaching the RBI’s overseas investment limit.

  • Affected Subscriptions: Affected subscriptions include lump sum investments, additional purchases, switch-ins, and fresh SIP/STP registrations for IDCW transfers.

  • Effective Date: The suspension will be discontinued with effect from December 27, 2024, after the cut-off time on December 26, 2024.

  • Existing Systematic Registrations: Existing systematic registrations (SIPs, STPs, IDCW transfers) will continue until further notice.

  • Intra-Scheme Switches Unaffected: The suspension does not apply to intra-scheme switches between plans/options within the scheme and will remain in effect until further notice from the AMC.

  • Unchanged Terms and Conditions: All other terms and conditions of the scheme remain unchanged. This addendum forms an integral part of the SID and KIM, as amended from time to time.
content_snippet: SBI Mutual Fund Suspends Fresh Subscriptions to International Fund.Expert Analysis:Suspension of Fresh Subscriptions: SBI Mutual Fund suspends fresh subscriptions to the SBI International Access - US
takeaways: SBI Mutual Fund suspends fresh subscriptions to US Equity FoF from December 27 due to RBI's overseas investment limit.
takeaways_snippet: SBI Mutual Fund suspends fresh subscriptions to US Equity FoF from December 27 due to RBI's overseas investment limit.

title: PharmEasy investor cuts value of its stake drastically, implying new valuation of $456M
content:
PharmEasy Investor Slashes Stake Value, New Valuation Drops to $456M.

Expert Analysis:

  • New Valuation: PharmEasy is now valued at $456 million, based on Janus Henderson’s filing, which values its 12.9 million shares at $766,043.

  • Original Investment and Valuation Drop: Janus Henderson’s Global Research Fund originally spent $9.4 million to acquire the shares, with the current valuation down 92% from PharmEasy’s all-time high of $5.6 billion.

  • Fresh Capital and IPO Plans: Despite raising over $200 million in fresh capital earlier this year, the valuation remains low as PharmEasy prepares to file for an initial public offering next year.

  • Rights Issue and Capital Raised: In 2023, PharmEasy raised $417 million through a rights issue amidst a funding crunch, with $216 million secured by April 2024.

  • Backers and Market Position: PharmEasy, backed by Prosus, Temasek, TPG, and B Capital, operates one of India’s largest online pharmacies.

  • Thyrocare Acquisition: PharmEasy's current valuation is much lower than the $600 million it paid to acquire Thyrocare in 2021. 

  • Financial Challenges: After deferring an $843 million IPO in 2021, PharmEasy struggled with debt, including a $300 million loan from Goldman Sachs, and difficulties raising new equity in a tough market.

title: Metropolitan Stock Exchange raises Rs 238 crore; Groww promoters, Zerodha's Kamath brothers fund among investors
content:
MSEI approves ₹238 crore equity issuance via private placement.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investor Participation: Investors include Billionbrains Garage Ventures (Groww's promoter), Rainmatter Investments (Zerodha founders), Securicorp Securities, and Share India Securities.

  • Board Approval: The Board of Directors, in its adjourned meeting on December 24, 2024, approved the issuance of up to 119 crore equity shares.

  • Share Details: Each share will have a face value of ₹1 and a premium of ₹1, to be allocated to Billionbrains Garage Ventures, Rainmatter Investments, Securicorp Securities India, and Share India Securities, subject to consents and approval at the Extra Ordinary General Meeting.

  • Low Trading Activity: MSEI typically experiences limited trading activity across most of its segments, including equities, equity derivatives, currency derivatives, and interest rate derivatives.

  • Equity vs Derivatives: While the equity segment sees nil trading on most days, there is some traction in the derivatives segments, particularly in equity and currency instruments.

  • Investor Expectations: Market participants believe investments in MSEI are based on the expectation of rising trading activity and valuations, similar to BSE’s growth.

  • Business Overview & Financial Performance: Tata Capital serves 5.2 million customers, operates 900 branches, and posted ₹18,178 crore revenue (+34% YoY) and ₹3,315 crore net profit (+12%) in FY24.

  • Advisors for IPO: Tata Group has appointed Kotak Mahindra Capital as the investment bank and Cyril Amarchand Mangaldas as the legal advisor, with additional investment banks expected to join soon.

  • Market Anticipation and Impact: The announcement of Tata Capital’s IPO caused a 12% surge in Tata Investment Corporation shares, reflecting optimistic market sentiment.

title: HDFC Securities expects India GDP to moderate to 6.4% in 2024-25
content:
HDFC Securities Projects India's GDP Growth at 6.4% for 2024-25.

Expert Analysis:

  • GDP Growth Forecast: HDFC Securities expects India’s GDP to grow 6.4% in 2024-25, with downside risks from weak urban demand and slow private capex revival.

  • RBI’s Growth Revision: RBI revised its growth forecast to 6.6% from 7.2%, following India’s GDP growth of 5.4% in Q2 2024-25, lower than the 7% forecast.

  • Future Growth Projection: HDFC Securities projects a GDP growth of 6.7% in 2025-26.

  • Urban vs Rural Consumption: Urban consumption is slowing due to inflation, while rural demand is improving with better monsoons and crops. MNREGA demand is moderating, indicating a positive labour market.

  • Investment and Sectoral Growth: Investments will drive growth. Discretionary consumption is stronger than staples. HDFC Securities expects volume-led growth in FY 2026, driven by BFSI, industrials, cement, energy, and IT sectors.

  • Capital Expenditure and GDP Growth: Central government capex was muted in the first half of 2024-25 but is expected to pick up in the second half. India's GDP grew 8.2% in FY 2023-24, with the Economic Survey projecting 6.5-7% growth for 2024-25.
content_snippet: HDFC Securities Projects India's GDP Growth at 6.4% for 2024-25.Expert Analysis:GDP Growth Forecast: HDFC Securities expects India’s GDP to grow 6.4% in 2024-25, with downside risks from weak urban de
takeaways: India’s GDP growth for 2024-25 is expected at 6.4%, driven by rural demand and government spending.
takeaways_snippet: India’s GDP growth for 2024-25 is expected at 6.4%, driven by rural demand and government spending.

title: Sebi Extends Commodity Derivatives Suspension Until Jan 2025
content:
SEBI Extends Agricultural Derivatives Trading Suspension.

Expert Analysis:

  • Extension of Suspension: SEBI extended the suspension of derivatives trading in seven agricultural commodities until January 31, 2025 to rein in prices.

  • Commodities Affected: The suspension applies to wheat, moong, paddy (non-basmati), chana, crude palm oil, mustard seeds (and derivatives), and soybean (and derivatives).

  • Initial Directive: The suspension was first imposed on December 19, 2021, and initially set to last until December 20, 2022.

  • Previous Extensions: It was extended twice, first until December 20, 2023, and then until December 20, 2024.

  • Impact on Trading: Existing positions in these commodities can be squared, but no fresh futures trading is allowed.

  • The objective of the Move: This decision aims to curb excessive speculation, stabilize prices, and address food inflation concerns.
content_snippet: SEBI Extends Agricultural Derivatives Trading Suspension.Expert Analysis:Extension of Suspension: SEBI extended the suspension of derivatives trading in seven agricultural commodities until January 31
takeaways: SEBI extends agricultural derivatives suspension to January 2025, focusing on price stability and mitigating speculation-driven inflation.
takeaways_snippet: SEBI extends agricultural derivatives suspension to January 2025, focusing on price stability and mitigating speculation-driven inflation.

title: Sterlite Power unit raises ₹725 crore
content:
Sterlite Power's GPS Unit Secures ₹725 Crore Funding Post-Demerger.

Expert Analysis:

  • Funding Details: A unit of Sterlite Power Transmission Ltd (SPTL) raised ₹725 crore in a primary funding round led by GEF Capital Partners (₹450 crore) and Enam Holding  (₹275 crore).

  • Company Overview and Investors: Sterlite Power, Vedanta Group's privately held power transmission arm, provides solutions in India and Brazil. GEF Capital Partners is a private equity fund focused on climate growth investments, while Enam Holdings is a privately owned and managed firm.

  • Core Business of GPS: GPS specializes in manufacturing power conductors, optical ground wires (OPGW), and power cables for domestic and export markets.

  • Corporate Restructuring: Sterlite Power recently underwent a demerger, creating two entities: Sterlite Grid 5 Limited (Transmission Infrastructure) and SPTL (GPS and Specialized EPC services).

  • Strategic Focus: The restructuring aims to allow each business unit to pursue independent growth strategies and attract tailored capital investments.

title: Nuvama Wealth Acquires Rs 100 cr in OYO Shares
content:
Nuvama Wealth Acquires Rs 100 Crore Stake in OYO.

Expert Analysis:

  • Acquisition Details: Nuvama Wealth and Investment Limited acquired shares worth Rs 100 crore in OYO's parent firm Oravel Stays Limited at Rs 53 per share on behalf of its investors, a clutch of family offices, through a secondary market transaction.

  • Valuation: The transaction values OYO at USD 4.6 billion for the travel tech unicorn.

  • Early Investors’ Exit: Shares are being sold by OYO’s early investors, providing them a partial exit and potentially bringing in new strategic investors.

  • Discussions with Other Buyers: Discussions are underway with other buyers, including Incred, for shares priced between Rs 53-60, with a potential valuation of up to USD 5.2 billion.

  • Valuation Comparison: Despite the valuation increase, it is still significantly below OYO’s peak valuation of USD 10 billion.

  • Profit Report: OYO reported a net profit of Rs 132 crore in Q1 FY 2025, recovering from a loss of Rs 108 crore in the same quarter last year.

  • Leadership Vision: Mr. Prakash Lohia, Managing Director of Merino Industries, highlights the adoption of LNG-powered trucks as a pivotal step toward sustainable operations and responsible logistics in India.

title: SEBI okays Indian Commodity Exchange's exit, a year after exchange applied for a voluntary surrender
content:
ICEX Exits After SEBI Denial of Ownership Norm Relaxation.

Expert Analysis:

  • SEBI Grants Exit Approval: SEBI permitted the exit of ICEX over a year after its shareholders approved the voluntary surrender of recognition.

  • SEBI's Exit Order Requirements: SEBI's December 10 order mandates ICEX to change its name and avoid using 'exchange' or its variants if continuing as a corporate entity under the Companies Act 2013.

  • ICEX Recognition and Withdrawal: Granted recognition as a commodity derivatives exchange in 2009, ICEX had its recognition withdrawn in 2022 due to non-compliance with minimum net-worth requirements, SEBI's circulars, inspection observations, and infrastructural deficiencies.

  • Appeal to SAT: ICEX appealed SEBI's order at the Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT), which set aside the order in June 2022 with specific conditions.

  • Tribunal’s Condition for Suspension: The tribunal ruled that ICEX’s operations would remain suspended until it raises requisite funds and completes compliance within a year.

  • Future of ICEX Operations: If ICEX meets compliance, it can apply to SEBI to resume operations; failure to comply may result in recognition withdrawal after a hearing.

  • ICEX Requests Ownership Norm Relaxation: In February 2023, ICEX requested SEBI to relax ownership structure norms, citing that the SECC Regulations’ 5% shareholding limit made it unattractive for new investor

  • ICEX Requests Relaxed Investment Norms: ICEX requested SEBI to allow new investors to hold up to 51% stake in the exchange for five years.

  • Contingency on SEBI's Decision: ICEX stated that if SEBI denied the request, it could consider the letter as a voluntary surrender of recognition.

  • Voluntary Surrender of Recognition: SEBI accepted ICEX's letter as a voluntary surrender, with ICEX submitting a shareholder resolution on May 25, 2023, to approve the surrender.
content_snippet: ICEX Exits After SEBI Denial of Ownership Norm Relaxation.Expert Analysis:SEBI Grants Exit Approval: SEBI permitted the exit of ICEX over a year after its shareholders approved the voluntary surrender
takeaways: ICEX voluntarily surrendered its recognition after failing to meet SEBI’s requirements, with a directive to change its name.
takeaways_snippet: ICEX voluntarily surrendered its recognition after failing to meet SEBI’s requirements, with a directive to change its name.

title: NSE Likely To Set Up Data Centre For Algo Trading Validation
content:
NSE to Set Up a Data Centre for Trading Algorithm Validation.

Expert Analysis:

  • Data Collection, Establishment & Validation: NSE plans to set up a dedicated data center, managed by NSE-Administration and Supervision Ltd., which will collect and validate data from market institutions to support trading algorithm performance validation.

  • Project Preparations & Funding: Initial preparations are underway, with funding likely allocated for infrastructure development, and will serve as a central repository, processing data from market infrastructure institutions.

  • Oversight & Collaboration with CRAs: NSE will collaborate with qualified credit rating agencies (CRAs) to validate data from advisors and research analysts, ensuring compliance with SEBI regulations.

  • Advisor Data Submission: Advisors must submit data either at the time of advising clients or by the end of the trading day, transmitted to the data center via APIs or uploads.

  • Validation System Development: A robust validation system will be created, including a business requirement document and methodology.

  • Data Processing & Output Delivery: After processing, the data center will deliver validated outputs to the Performance Validation Agency (PVA) in a standardized format through an API, with necessary disclaimers and disclosures.

  • Regulatory Amendments & SEBI Circulars: Amendments to existing CRA regulations will be required, and SEBI is expected to issue circulars outlining data-sharing procedures and operational guidelines for the new system.
content_snippet: NSE to Set Up a Data Centre for Trading Algorithm Validation.Expert Analysis:Data Collection, Establishment & Validation: NSE plans to set up a dedicated data center, managed by NSE-Administration
takeaways: NSE’s data center will validate algorithms, involving CRAs and SEBI guidelines, ensuring compliance and performance validation.
takeaways_snippet: NSE’s data center will validate algorithms, involving CRAs and SEBI guidelines, ensuring compliance and performance validation.

title: NSE IPO: Public Forum Moves Delhi High Court Over Delay In SEBI Approval
content:
NSE IPO Approval Delayed as SEBI Withholds Decision, Legal Forum Seeks Action.

Expert Analysis:

  • Forum’s Legal Action: The People Activism Forum filed a rejoinder before the Delhi High Court highlighting the delay in regulatory approval for NSE’s IPO.

  • SEBI’s Inaction: Three months later, SEBI has not provided any valid explanation for withholding approval for NSE’s IPO.

  • NSE’s Reapplication Timeline: NSE reapplied for IPO approval in August 2024, following SEBI’s submissions on August 13, 2024.

  • Colocation Case Closure: SEBI closed the colocation case on September 13, 2024, citing insufficient evidence, but IPO approval remains withheld.

  • Forum’s Urgency for Action: The Forum urges SEBI to act promptly, emphasizing that NSE's listing would enhance market transparency and provide more opportunities for investors.

  • Court Hearings & Stakeholder Impact: The most recent hearing occurred on Dec. 3, 2024, with the next set for March 6, 2025. The Forum claims the delay prevents existing shareholders from unlocking share value and restricts public participation in NSE’s profits.

takeaways: SEBI’s delay in IPO approval affects shareholder value and public participation, raising questions about regulatory transparency and investor interests.
takeaways_snippet: SEBI’s delay in IPO approval affects shareholder value and public participation, raising questions about regulatory transparency and investor interests.

title: OYO Bucks Industry Trend in the US with 16% Jump in Online Summer Booking Revenue
content:
OYO Achieves 16% Online Summer Booking Revenue Growth in the US.

Expert Analysis:

  • Revenue Growth: OYO achieved a 16% increase in summer booking revenue (June-August) in the US compared to last year, outperforming the 2% decline in the economy hotel segment.

  • Booking Surge: Total bookings grew by 15%, with exceptional performance in the Midwest (+46%) and Northeast (+44%), exceeding market averages of 22% and 24%.

  • State-Wise Contributions: Texas emerged as the top-performing state led with 22% of revenue, followed by Oregon (18%), Florida (7%), and Louisiana (6%).

  • City-Level Highlights: Cities like Newport (Oregon) and Houston (Texas) each contributed 5% of the total revenue, while Seaside accounted for 4%,  with Houston, Port Allen, Rockford, and Portland showing exceptional growth beyond market averages.

  • Technology-Driven Growth: OYO leveraged its tech platform for seamless bookings, AI-powered support, flexible cancellations, and an integrated loyalty program for competitive rates.

title: MobiKwik Reduces IPO Size for the third time to around Rs 572 Crore.
content:
• IPO Size Reduction and History: MobiKwik reduced its IPO size to Rs 572 crore from Rs 700 crore (January 2024), after postponing a Rs 1,900 crore IPO in July 2021 due to market volatility and weak peer performance.

  • Price Band and Subscription Dates: The IPO price band is Rs 265-279 per share, with subscriptions opening on December 11, 2024.

  • Anchor Bidding and Allotment Timeline: Anchor bidding will begin on December 10, and the issue will close on December 13. The allotment is set for December 16, with refunds and equity credit on December 17, total mcap at Rs 2,165 crore at the upper price band.

  • Use of Proceeds: The firm aims to use proceeds for expanding its financial services business (Rs 150 crore), payment services (Rs 135 crore), AI and technology R&D (Rs 107 crore), and capital expenditure for payment devices (Rs 70.2 crore).

  • User and Merchant Base: MobiKwik, founded by Bipin Preet Singh and Upasana Taku, connects 161.03 million users and 4.26 million merchants (as of June 30, 2024).

  • Market Position and Services Expansion: As of May 2024, MobiKwik holds a 23.11% market share in PPI wallet GTV and ranks sixth in 80 BBPS units, in August 2024 for offering diverse financial services including MobiKwik ZIP, UPI, Zaakpay, and peer-to-peer lending.

  • FY24 Financial Performance: MobiKwik reported FY24 revenue of Rs 875 crore (up from Rs 539.46 crore), with a net profit of Rs 14.08 crore (recovered from a loss of Rs 83.81 crore); Q1 FY24 revenue was Rs 342.27 crore with a net loss of Rs 6.62 crore.

title: Tata Capital's valuations soar in unlisted market
content:
Tata Capital Valuation Triples to ₹3.5 Lakh Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Valuation Surge: Tata Capital’s valuation soared from ₹300 to ₹900 per share within a year, tripling its market value to ₹3.5 lakh crore.

  • Strong Financial Performance: Tata Capital provides mortgage-based loans, business loans, and investment services, with an FY24 top line of ₹11,500 crore, net profit of ₹664 crore, EPS of ₹6.78, a loan book of ₹1.4 lakh crore, and a customer base of 4 million.

  • Net Worth and Book Value: With a net worth of ₹20,800 crore, the book value per share is ₹57, highlighting an aggressive valuation.

  • High Valuation Metrics: The price-to-book value is 15x, and the price-to-earnings ratio exceeds 120x, signaling caution for new investors.

title: Moody’s Upgrades OYO to B2 Amid Profitability Gains.
content:
• Credit Rating Upgrade: Moody’s upgraded Oravel Stays’ credit rating from B3 to B2 with a stable outlook, reflecting improved profitability and higher creditworthiness.

  • Record Profitability: OYO posted its first-ever profit-after-tax of ₹229 crore in FY24, driven by eight consecutive quarters of positive-adjusted EBITDA.

  • Strong Liquidity Position: Liquidity is strong, with $203 million as of September 2024 in cash, $299 million in operational cash flow, and $825 million in committed credit facilities that will cover its $525M Motel 6 acquisition and debts service over the next 24 months.

  • Financial Restructuring and Market Performance: OYO will use $174M in equity capital raised between June and August 2024 and refinancing to repay its term loan B maturing in June 2026, despite a share price drop from ₹82 to ₹55.

  • Projected Financial Growth: OYO’s EBITDA is projected to reach $200 million in FY26, reducing its debt/EBITDA leverage from 4.8X in FY24 to 4.2X in FY26.
content_snippet:
Credit Rating Upgrade: Moody’s upgraded Oravel Stays’ credit rating from B3 to B2 with a stable outlook, reflecting improved profitability and higher creditworthiness.



Record Profitability: OYO posted its first-ever profit-after-tax of ₹229 crore in FY24, driven by eight consecutive quarters of positive-adjusted EBITDA.



Strong Liquidity Position: Liquidity is strong, with $203 million as of September 2024 in cash, $299 million in operational cash flow, and $825 million in committed credit facilities that will cover its $525M Motel 6 acquisition and debts service over the next 24 months.



Financial Restructuring and Market Performance: OYO will use $174M in equity capital raised between June and August 2024 and refinancing to repay its term loan B maturing in June 2026, despite a share price drop from ₹82 to ₹55.



Projected Financial Growth: OYO’s EBITDA is projected to reach $200 million in FY26, reducing its debt/EBITDA leverage from 4.8X in FY24 to 4.2X in FY26.
takeaways: • Despite share price challenges, Moody’s B2 rating underscores OYO’s financial growth, liquidity strength, and expansion plans.

title: Greenzo to partner France's EODev to launch H2-powered gensets in India
content:
Greenzo Partners with France's EODev to Launch Hydrogen-Powered Gensets in India.

Expert Analysis

  • Partnership Details: Greenzo Energy India (GEIL) partnered with France's EODev for a 20-year collaboration to introduce hydrogen fuel cell generators in South Asia.

  • Investment Plans: Greenzo plans an initial investment of ₹200 crore, setting up to ₹1,000 crore within two years.

  • Role of Greenzo: GEIL will distribute EODev’s zero-emission generators, provide maintenance services, and assemble products locally.

  • Toyota Technology Introduction: The partnership brings Toyota's hydrogen fuel cell technology to the region for the first time.

  • Hydrogen Generator Market: India’s hydrogen generator market is valued at $20 million going forward with facilities like telecom towers, data centres, and microgrids running diesel generators, with 80 lakh diesel generators in telecom alone.
