  • Location and Size: The hotel spans 44,000 square feet, located in Bur Dubai next to Burjuman Mall and Metro Station on the western side of Dubai Creek.

  • Expansion Plan: The brand plans to expand in major Dubai areas including Deira, Sheikh Zayed Road, Downtown, Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Marina, Jumeirah Beach Residences, Business Bay, and Al Barsha.

  • Brand Focus: SUNDAY aims to provide modern luxury and personalized guest experiences, catering to both leisure and business travelers.

  • Current and Future Hotel Count: Today, India has three SUNDAY hotels, with plans to increase this number to 25 by the end of March 2025.

title: NSE sets November 2 as record date for 4:1 bonus share issue
content:
NSE Sets November 2nd as  Record Date for Bonus Shares.

Expert Analysis:

  • Record Date Finalized: The NSE has set November 2, 2024, as the record date for its upcoming bonus share issuance, finalized during the board meeting 

  • Bonus Share Ratio: Shareholders on record as of November 2, 2024, will receive bonus shares at a ratio of four shares for every one held.

  • Book Closure Period: The register of members and share transfer books will be closed from October 4 to November 2, 2024.

  • Transfer Application Processing: No new share transfer applications will be processed during the closure period; only those with complete documents submitted before the closure will be handled.
content_snippet:
NSE Sets November 2nd as  Record Date for Bonus Shares.


Expert Analysis:

Record Date Finalized: The NSE has set November 2, 2024, as the record date for its upcoming bonus share issuance, finalized during the board meeting 

Bonus Share Ratio: Shareholders on record as of November 2, 2024, will receive bonus shares at a ratio of four shares for every one held.

Book Closure Period: The register of members and share transfer books will be closed from October 4 to November 2, 2024.

Transfer Application Processing: No new share transfer applications will be processed during the closure period; only those with complete documents submitted before the closure will be handled.
takeaways: NSE has set November 2, 2024, as the record date for its 4:1 bonus share issue. The book will be closed from October 4 to November 2, 2024, during which no new transfer applications will be accepted.

title: This new transmission line promises to end Mumbai's power problems
content:
Sterlite Power Boosts Mumbai’s Energy Supply 2,000 MW Transmission Line.

Expert Analysis:

  • Project Overview: The Panvel-Padgha transmission line is a ₹900 crore project spanning 90 kilometers.

  • Power Supply Impact: It brings 2,000 megawatts of power to the Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR). 

  • Demand Stabilization: Mumbai requires 3,500 megawatts annually, spiking to 4,500 megawatts in summer and October; the new infrastructure aims to handle these fluctuations and prevent prolonged outages.

  • COVID-19 Delays: Originally delayed by 9 months due to COVID-19, advanced technology saved 6-8 months of construction time.

Sterlite Power Boosts Mumbai’s Energy Supply 2,000 MW Transmission Line.

Project Overview: The Panvel-Padgha transmission line is a ₹900 crore project spanning 90 kilometers.

Demand Stabilization: Mumbai requires 3,500 megawatts annually, spiking to 4,500 megawatts in summer and October; the new infrastructure aims to handle these fluctuations and prevent prolonged outages.

COVID-19 Delays: Originally delayed by 9 months due to COVID-19, advanced technology saved 6-8 months of construction time.
takeaways: Sterlite Power’s ₹900 crore project adds 2,000 MW to MMR, stabilizing energy and lowering costs.

title: Nayara Energy Boosts Domestic Fuel Sales Amid Declining Exports
content:
Nayara Energy Hikes Domestic Fuel Sales by 14.3% as Exports Decline.

Expert Analysis:

  • Fuel Sales Growth: Fuel sales grew by 14.3% in Q2 2024, driven by rising domestic demand.

  • Local Market Focus: In Q2 2024, Nayara Energy sold 75% of diesel and 60% of petrol locally, using its extensive retail network to boost its presence in underserved markets and support India's economic growth.

  • Diesel and Petrol Sales Increase: Retail diesel sales rose by 14% to 2.08 million tonnes, while petrol sales increased by 14.7% to 0.916 million tonnes compared to the previous year.

  • Expansive and Automated Network: Nayara Energy operates over 6,500 highly automated petrol pumps across India, enhancing mobility and connectivity.

title: SEBI closes proceedings against NSE, others in co-location case; decision clears path for IPO
content:
SEBI Closes NSE Co-Location Case, Clearing Path for Delayed IPO.

Expert Analysis

  • Case Disposal Date: On September 13, 2024, SEBI disposed of the proceedings against NSE in the co-location case.

  • Insufficient Evidence: SEBI found gaps in NSE's due diligence but concluded there was insufficient evidence for the conclusion and closed the case without any direction 

  • Impact on IPO: The closure of the case may facilitate NSE’s delayed IPO, which had been stalled since 2016 due to the controversy.

  • IPO Reapplication: Last month, NSE resumed its long-pending public offering process and reapplied for a ‘no-objection’ from the market regulator.
content_snippet:
SEBI Closes NSE Co-Location Case, Clearing Path for Delayed IPO.

Expert Analysis

Case Disposal Date: On September 13, 2024, SEBI disposed of the proceedings against NSE in the co-location case.

Insufficient Evidence: SEBI found gaps in NSE's due diligence but concluded there was insufficient evidence for the conclusion and closed the case without any direction 

Impact on IPO: The closure of the case may facilitate NSE’s delayed IPO, which had been stalled since 2016 due to the controversy.

IPO Reapplication: Last month, NSE resumed its long-pending public offering process and reapplied for a ‘no-objection’ from the market regulator.
takeaways: SEBI closed the co-location case due to insufficient evidence, which may enable NSE’s long-delayed IPO.
takeaways_snippet: SEBI closed the co-location case due to insufficient evidence, which may enable NSE’s long-delayed IPO.

title: Tata Motors Finance-Tata Capital merger approved by CCI
content:
CCI Approves Tata Motors Finance-Tata Capital Merger.

Expert Analysis:

  • CCI Approval: The Competition Commission of India approved the merger of Tata Motors Finance Ltd (TMFL) into Tata Capital Ltd (TCL) which is a subsidiary of Tata Sons and operates as a (NBFC-ICC).

  • Business Synergy: TMFL provides loans for Tata Motors vehicles and refinances existing vehicle loans, as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Tata Motors Ltd..

  • Equity Share Issuance: TCL will issue equity shares, allowing Tata Motors to acquire a 4.7% stake in the merged entity.

  • Strategic Alignment: The merger aligns with Tata Motors' goal to exit non-core businesses and focus on emerging technologies and products.
content_snippet:
CCI Approves Tata Motors Finance-Tata Capital Merger.


Expert Analysis:

CCI Approval: The Competition Commission of India approved the merger of Tata Motors Finance Ltd (TMFL) into Tata Capital Ltd (TCL) which is a subsidiary of Tata Sons and operates as a (NBFC-ICC).

Business Synergy: TMFL provides loans for Tata Motors vehicles and refinances existing vehicle loans, as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Tata Motors Ltd..

Equity Share Issuance: TCL will issue equity shares, allowing Tata Motors to acquire a 4.7% stake in the merged entity.

Strategic Alignment: The merger aligns with Tata Motors' goal to exit non-core businesses and focus on emerging technologies and products.
takeaways: Tata Motors will hold a 4.7% stake in Tata Capital following the CCI-approved merger of TCL & TMF.
takeaways_snippet: Tata Motors will hold a 4.7% stake in Tata Capital following the CCI-approved merger of TCL & TMF.

title: Gensol and Matrix to Establish India's First Green Hydrogen Valley in Pune
content:
Gensol and Matrix to Launch India’s First Green Hydrogen Valley in Pune.

Expert Analysis:

  • Project Awarded: Matrix Gas & Renewables Ltd., in collaboration with Gensol Engineering Ltd., has secured the project to set up India’s first Green Hydrogen Valley in Pune.

  • Development Basis: The alliance will develop a green hydrogen production plant on a Build Own and Operate (BOO) basis.

  • Offtake Agreement: A 20-year firm offtake agreement is in place to supply green hydrogen to the specialty chemical sector.

  • Government Support: The project is supported by the Department of Science & Technology (DST) and facilitated by National Chemicals Laboratories (NCL), Pune.

Gensol and Matrix to Launch India’s First Green Hydrogen Valley in Pune.

Development Basis: The alliance will develop a green hydrogen production plant on a Build Own and Operate (BOO) basis.

Offtake Agreement: A 20-year firm offtake agreement is in place to supply green hydrogen to the specialty chemical sector.

Government Support: The project is supported by the Department of Science & Technology (DST) and facilitated by National Chemicals Laboratories (NCL), Pune.

title: Swiggy increases IPO size: Company to now raise Rs 5,000 crore in fresh issue up from Rs 3,750 crore
content:
Swiggy Increases Fresh Issue Size to Rs 5,000 Crore from Rs 3,750 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Increased IPO Size: Swiggy is increasing its IPO size from Rs 3,750 crore ($450 million) to Rs 5,000 crore ($600 million) via a fresh issue.

  • Initial IPO Expectations: Swiggy was initially expected to raise $1.25 billion through its IPO, including Rs 3,750 crore ($450 million) in fresh issue and up to Rs 6,664 crore ($800 million) through the offer-for-sale.

  • Total IPO Size Increase: The total IPO size will rise from $1.25 billion to $1.4 billion, pending approval at the EGM on October 3.

  • Revenue Growth and Loss Reduction: Swiggy’s revenue grew 36%, from Rs 8,265 crore in FY23 to Rs 11,247 crore in FY24, while losses decreased 44%, from Rs 4,179 crore to Rs 2,350 crore, driven by stronger expense control ahead of its planned IPO later this year.
content_snippet:
Swiggy Increases Fresh Issue Size to Rs 5,000 Crore from Rs 3,750 Crore.


Expert Analysis:

Increased IPO Size: Swiggy is increasing its IPO size from Rs 3,750 crore ($450 million) to Rs 5,000 crore ($600 million) via a fresh issue.

Initial IPO Expectations: Swiggy was initially expected to raise $1.25 billion through its IPO, including Rs 3,750 crore ($450 million) in fresh issue and up to Rs 6,664 crore ($800 million) through the offer-for-sale.

Total IPO Size Increase: The total IPO size will rise from $1.25 billion to $1.4 billion, pending approval at the EGM on October 3.

Revenue Growth and Loss Reduction: Swiggy’s revenue grew 36%, from Rs 8,265 crore in FY23 to Rs 11,247 crore in FY24, while losses decreased 44%, from Rs 4,179 crore to Rs 2,350 crore, driven by stronger expense control ahead of its planned IPO later this year.
takeaways: Swiggy increased its IPO size to $1.4 billion, showcasing financial recovery, though it still trailed Zomato in profitability and revenue.

content:

  • Net Profit After Tax: Net profit after tax reached ₹412.58 crore, up 54.4% from ₹267.17 crore in the previous year.

title: Chennai Super Kings FY24 Results: Net Profit Jumps 340% to Rs 229 Crore, Revenue Grows 131%
content:
Chennai Super Kings FY24: Net Profit Soars 340% to ₹229 Crore, Revenue Up 131%.

Expert Analysis:

  • Net Profit Surge: Chennai Super Kings reported a 340% rise in net profit to ₹229.20 crore in FY24, compared to Rs 52 crore in the previous financial year.

  • Revenue Growth: Total revenue grew by 131%, reaching ₹676.40 crore, driven by BCCI central rights and ticketing income.

  • AGM and Leadership: CSKCL’s 10th AGM will be held on September 27, 2024, with KS Viswanathan’s reappointment as Managing Director approved by the board.

  • IPL Performance: CSK finished 5th in IPL 2024, narrowly missing the Knockouts, maintaining its legacy with 5 IPL titles, 10 finals, and 12 playoff appearances.

  • SVPL Financials: SVPL reported a turnover of ₹5.47 crore, more than double the ₹2.56 crore from the previous year, with operating loss narrowing to ₹1.61 crore from ₹6.34 crore in FY23.

  • Stock Performance: CSK's unlisted stock price surged 22% in the last year and 95% over three years.

Revenue Growth: Total revenue grew by 131%, reaching ₹676.40 crore, driven by BCCI central rights and ticketing income.

AGM and Leadership: CSKCL’s 10th AGM will be held on September 27, 2024, with KS Viswanathan’s reappointment as Managing Director approved by the board.

IPL Performance: CSK finished 5th in IPL 2024, narrowly missing the Knockouts, maintaining its legacy with 5 IPL titles, 10 finals, and 12 playoff appearances.

title: HDB Financial Services in talks with top global banks to shortlist advisers for IPO
content:
HDB Financial Services Mulls IPO, Engages Top Global Banks for Advisers.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Preparation: HDB Financial Services, the non-banking finance arm of HDFC Bank, is preparing for an IPO and is talking to several global investment banks to pick advisers.

  • Adviser Selection: The company is discussing with Bank of America Securities, Morgan Stanley, and Nomura to shortlist advisers, with the possibility of additional banks joining in the coming weeks.

  • Fundraising Target: The IPO is projected to raise approximately Rs 10,000 crore ($1.2 billion), with HDFC Bank diluting its stake by nearly 10 percent.

  • Board Approval: HDFC Bank's board approved the listing process for HDB on July 20.

  • Regulatory Deadline: HDB must complete its IPO by September 2025 under RBI regulations.

  • Valuation Reduction: API Holdings Pvt. (PharmEasy) valuation was cut by 92% to $460 million by Janus Henderson.

  • Valuation Drop: At the end of 2021, Janus Henderson valued PharmEasy at approximately $5.6 billion In the latest SEC filing, the value of these shares has dropped by 92%, reducing PharmEasy's worth to around $460 million.

  • Earlier Valuation Slash: The struggling e-pharmacy experienced a 90% reduction from its peak valuation in April this year when it raised Rs 1,800 crore, led by Ranjan Pai's Manipal Education and Medical Group and existing investors.

  • Revenue Growth: FY23 revenue increased 16% to Rs 6,644 crore, up from Rs 5,728.8 crore in FY22.

  • Losses Widened: The Company Losses widened to Rs 5,211.7 crore from Rs 3,992.4 crore in FY22. It faces increased competition from rivals like Tata 1mg, Medlife, Flipkart Health+, and Reliance-backed Netmeds.
content_snippet:
PharmEasy Valuation Plummets 92% in Latest Janus Henderson Filing.


Expert Analysis:

Valuation Reduction: API Holdings Pvt. (PharmEasy) valuation was cut by 92% to $460 million by Janus Henderson.

Valuation Drop: At the end of 2021, Janus Henderson valued PharmEasy at approximately $5.6 billion In the latest SEC filing, the value of these shares has dropped by 92%, reducing PharmEasy's worth to around $460 million.

Earlier Valuation Slash: The struggling e-pharmacy experienced a 90% reduction from its peak valuation in April this year when it raised Rs 1,800 crore, led by Ranjan Pai's Manipal Education and Medical Group and existing investors.

Revenue Growth: FY23 revenue increased 16% to Rs 6,644 crore, up from Rs 5,728.8 crore in FY22.

Losses Widened: The Company Losses widened to Rs 5,211.7 crore from Rs 3,992.4 crore in FY22. It faces increased competition from rivals like Tata 1mg, Medlife, Flipkart Health+, and Reliance-backed Netmeds.
takeaways: PharmEasy's valuation slashed 92% to $460 million due to widening losses, modest revenue growth, and rising competition.

title: CIAL net profit soars 54% to Rs412.58cr for FY24
content:
CIAL Net Profit Surges 54% to Rs412.58 Crore in FY24.

Expert Analysis:

  • Net Profit Growth: CIAL's net profit increased by 54.43%, from Rs267.17 crore to Rs.412.58 crore in FY24.

  • Dividend Recommendation: The board recommended a Rs 4.5 per share dividend based on approval. 

  • Financial Performance Overview: Revenue grew by 25.25% from Rs759.37 crore to Rs951.10 crore.. Total expenses increased from Rs413.60 crore to Rs461.84 crore, at a slower rate than revenue growth.

  • Aero Revenue & UDF Growth: Aero revenue rose from Rs478.85 crore to Rs620.43 crore, with the User Development Fee (UDF) increasing by two-thirds, from Rs114.60 crore to Rs189.78 crore, accounting for 31% of the aero revenue.

  • Infrastructure Projects: CIAL completed the Import Cargo Complex and initiated seven mega projects.
content_snippet:
CIAL Net Profit Surges 54% to Rs412.58 Crore in FY24.


Expert Analysis:

Net Profit Growth: CIAL's net profit increased by 54.43%, from Rs267.17 crore to Rs.412.58 crore in FY24.

Dividend Recommendation: The board recommended a Rs 4.5 per share dividend based on approval. 

Financial Performance Overview: Revenue grew by 25.25% from Rs759.37 crore to Rs951.10 crore.. Total expenses increased from Rs413.60 crore to Rs461.84 crore, at a slower rate than revenue growth.

Aero Revenue & UDF Growth: Aero revenue rose from Rs478.85 crore to Rs620.43 crore, with the User Development Fee (UDF) increasing by two-thirds, from Rs114.60 crore to Rs189.78 crore, accounting for 31% of the aero revenue.

Infrastructure Projects: CIAL completed the Import Cargo Complex and initiated seven mega projects.
takeaways: CIAL’s strong financial growth contrasts with its low book value, despite high grey market share prices.
takeaways_snippet: CIAL’s strong financial growth contrasts with its low book value, despite high grey market share prices.

title: CIAL to give Rs 50 Cr for Akapparambu ROB
content:
Cochin Airport to Invest Rs 50 Crore in New Railway Overbridge for Direct Thrissur Access.

Expert Analysis:

  • Investment Details: Cochin International Airport Ltd (CIAL) is investing Rs 50 crore for a new railway overbridge (ROB) at Akapparambu near Nedumbassery.

  • Route Improvement: The ROB will provide a direct route from Thrissur to the airport, bypassing the Kariyad-Mattoor ROB.

  • Travel Savings: Passengers will save 5km of travel Residents of Kalady, Mattoor, Nayathode and Avanamkode would benefit by directly accessing the NH via Kariyad-Mattoor Road without detouring through airport road.

Travel Savings: Passengers will save 5km of travel Residents of Kalady, Mattoor, Nayathode and Avanamkode would benefit by directly accessing the NH via Kariyad-Mattoor Road without detouring through airport road.

title: Sterlite Power wins its second green energy evacuation scheme in Gujarat
content:
Sterlite Power Wins Second Green Energy Project in Gujarat.

Expert Analysis:

  • Project Acquisition: Sterlite Power Transmission Secures Khavda Phase-IV (7GW): Part C project for developing a transmission system.

  • Renewable Energy Park: The project aims to evacuate power from Gujarat’s 30 GW hybrid (solar and wind) renewable energy park at Khavda, inaugurated by the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India in December 2020.

  • Company Milestones: This is Sterlite Power’s second green energy transmission project in Gujarat and its fifth overall.

  • Alignment with National Goals: The project aligns with India's goal of installing 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

title: Cial sets sights on hub status with major upgrades & new services
content:
Cochin International Airport Expands with New Facilities and Ambitious Growth Plans.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Positioning: Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL) aims to become a major hub for airline services in South India, with several small airline companies interested in starting operations once Union government approvals are granted.

  • Passenger Volume: CIAL handles over 10  million passengers annually, with 670 international and 795 domestic flights operating each week from Kochi.

  • Development Projects: Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL) is planning several development projects to enhance the passenger experience, including a Rs 550-crore international terminal expansion to be completed in three years, and a Rs 160-crore commercial zone development.

  • Aero Lounge and Major Projects: Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL) is enhancing its facilities with a new 50,000 sq. ft. aero lounge, completed in 10 months, and the initiation of seven major projects, including an international terminal expansion, a 15 lakh sq. ft. apron, and eight new aerobridges.

title: Oravel Stays And M3M India Set New Luxury Benchmarks With SUNDAY Hotel In Gurugram
content:
Oravel Stays Partners with M3M India to Launch India’s Largest 220-Room SUNDAY Hotel in Gurugram.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Partnership: Oravel Stays, in partnership with M3M India, is launching India’s largest SUNDAY Hotel at M3M SkyLoft on New Golf Course Road Extension, Gurugram.

  • Hotel Features: The hotel will feature 220 rooms and span approximately 1.67 lakh square feet within a larger 15 lakh square feet development.

  • Prime Location: Strategically located near the upcoming Cyber City 2 on Southern Peripheral Road (SPR) in Gurugram, it targets business travelers and conference attendees as well as attendees of meetings and conferences.

  • Broader Initiative: This project is part of a broader initiative, creating luxurious hospitality spaces totaling 15 lakh square feet with 1000 keys across multiple locations in Gurugram and NOIDA.

  • Investor and Tourism Appeal: The project is expected to attract investors, business leaders, and tourists by offering premium experiences in a prime location, catering to the growing demand for high-end accommodation.

content:

Expert Analysis:

  • Current Transaction Volume: Pocket UPI transactions from a PPI perspective, handling 2,00,000 transactions daily.

  • PPI Wallet Growth: MobiKwik's market share in PPI wallet transactions increased from 11% in March to 23% after RBI’s crackdown on Paytm.

  • Long-Term User Target: MobiKwik aims to serve 500 million people in India's middle and lower middle-income categories over the next 10 years, leveraging smartphone reach in Tier 2, 3 cities, and villages.

  • Strategic Focus Areas: MobiKwik is focusing on general trade merchants, expanding QR code, sound box, and electronic data capture machine reach, increasing investments in field force and merchant networks, and converting its AI chatbot into a financial advisor.

title: NSE seeks NoC from Sebi to launch IPO
content:
NSE Seeks SEBI NoC for IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • Reapplication for IPO: NSE has reapplied for a 'no-objection' certificate with SEBI, advancing towards its long-pending IPO.

  • Regulatory and Legal Issues: NSE faced a Rs 1,100 crore fine in April 2019 for non-compliance.

  • SEBI Approval and Legal Status: NSE has received SEBI approval to increase share capital and issue bonus shares in a 4:1 ratio; the co-location case is pending in the Supreme Court, with the next hearing on September 3.

  • IPO Size: The IPO could raise Rs 28,000-30,000 crore, making it the largest since LIC's Rs 21,000 crore and Paytm's (Rs 18,300 crore) offering.
content_snippet:
NSE Seeks SEBI NoC for IPO.


Expert Analysis:

Reapplication for IPO: NSE has reapplied for a 'no-objection' certificate with SEBI, advancing towards its long-pending IPO.

Regulatory and Legal Issues: NSE faced a Rs 1,100 crore fine in April 2019 for non-compliance.

SEBI Approval and Legal Status: NSE has received SEBI approval to increase share capital and issue bonus shares in a 4:1 ratio; the co-location case is pending in the Supreme Court, with the next hearing on September 3.

IPO Size: The IPO could raise Rs 28,000-30,000 crore, making it the largest since LIC's Rs 21,000 crore and Paytm's (Rs 18,300 crore) offering.
takeaways: NSE's IPO progress is promising but depends on resolving ongoing legal and regulatory challenges.
takeaways_snippet: NSE's IPO progress is promising but depends on resolving ongoing legal and regulatory challenges.

title: Swiggy plans to welcome ex Flipkart executive Amitesh Jha as Instamart head
content:
Swiggy Plans to Appoint Amitesh Jha as Instamart Head Amid Executive Reshuffle.

Expert Analysis:

  • Leadership Transition: Swiggy is in advanced discussions with Amitesh Jha, former Senior Vice President at Flipkart, to lead Instamart.

  • Jha’s Background: Jha, with over 14 years at Flipkart, will replace Phani Kishan Addepalli, who will move to a growth role within Swiggy.

  • Addepalli’s New Role: Addepalli has been with Swiggy for almost 10 years and was recently promoted to co-founder of the company in 2021, and previously served as CEO of Insanely Good, which merged with Instamart earlier this year.

  • Recent Executive Changes: The transition follows Karthik Gurumurthy’s resignation and the hiring of Sairam Krishnamurthy as Senior Vice President and COO.

  • Instamart Resignations: Instamart also saw the resignations of Karan Arora, Vice President and Head of Supply Chain Management, and Sidharth Satpathy, former Vice President.
content_snippet:

Addepalli’s New Role: Addepalli has been with Swiggy for almost 10 years and was recently promoted to co-founder of the company in 2021, and previously served as CEO of Insanely Good, which merged with Instamart earlier this year.

title: India's biggest solar panel maker Waaree Energies’ IPO delayed over deemed public offer non-compliance
content:
Waaree Energies' IPO Faces Delay Due to Regulatory Non-Compliance.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Delay: Waaree Energies, has faced a delay in IPO approval due to non-compliance with the Companies Act, which restricts unlisted companies from selling shares to more than 200 people without a public offer.

  • Fundraising: The company raised Rs 1,000 crore from investment firm ValueQuest in August 2023 and Rs 1,040 crore from a clutch of investors, including family offices and HNI’s in an earlier round.

  • Regulatory Resolution: Due to non-compliance with the Companies Act, Waaree Energies must resolve the issue with the Registrar of Companies and MCA, potentially facing penalties and a lengthy delay in its IPO process.
content_snippet:
Waaree Energies' IPO Faces Delay Due to Regulatory Non-Compliance.


Expert Analysis:

IPO Delay: Waaree Energies, has faced a delay in IPO approval due to non-compliance with the Companies Act, which restricts unlisted companies from selling shares to more than 200 people without a public offer.

Fundraising: The company raised Rs 1,000 crore from investment firm ValueQuest in August 2023 and Rs 1,040 crore from a clutch of investors, including family offices and HNI’s in an earlier round.

Regulatory Resolution: Due to non-compliance with the Companies Act, Waaree Energies must resolve the issue with the Registrar of Companies and MCA, potentially facing penalties and a lengthy delay in its IPO process.
takeaways: Waaree Energies' IPO is delayed due to regulatory non-compliance, requiring a resolution with authorities.
takeaways_snippet: Waaree Energies' IPO is delayed due to regulatory non-compliance, requiring a resolution with authorities.

title: FPIs sell NSE shares on IPO uncertainty, derivatives volume concerns
content:
FPIs Sell NSE Shares Amid IPO Delays.

Expert Analysis:

  • FPI Selling Volume: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) have sold NSE shares worth Rs 3,500 crore over June and July. FPIs have been net sellers of NSE shares for six consecutive months, with the last instance of net buying in January.

  • Reasons for Selloff: Increased FPI selling is attributed to uncertainties around NSE's long-delayed IPO, heightened competition from BSE, and regulatory impacts on NSE’s derivatives segment.

  • NSE Earnings: Q1FY25 net profit for NSE was Rs 2,567 crore (up 39%) and revenue was Rs 4,510 crore (up 51%). Rs 587 crore was added to the Core Settlement Guarantee Fund, reaching Rs 10,500 crore.

  • Investor Growth: The number of  investor in NSE increased from 650 in FY21 to over 4,300, including notable figures like DMart founder Radhakishan Damani.
content_snippet:
FPIs Sell NSE Shares Amid IPO Delays.

Expert Analysis:


FPI Selling Volume: Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) have sold NSE shares worth Rs 3,500 crore over June and July. FPIs have been net sellers of NSE shares for six consecutive months, with the last instance of net buying in January.

Reasons for Selloff: Increased FPI selling is attributed to uncertainties around NSE's long-delayed IPO, heightened competition from BSE, and regulatory impacts on NSE’s derivatives segment.

NSE Earnings: Q1FY25 net profit for NSE was Rs 2,567 crore (up 39%) and revenue was Rs 4,510 crore (up 51%). Rs 587 crore was added to the Core Settlement Guarantee Fund, reaching Rs 10,500 crore.

Investor Growth: The number of  investor in NSE increased from 650 in FY21 to over 4,300, including notable figures like DMart founder Radhakishan Damani.

title: RBI Approves SBI Mutual Fund to Acquire Up to 9.99% Stake in Karur Vysya Bank
content:
RBI Approves SBI MF to Acquire 9.99% Stake in Karur Vysya Bank.

Expert Analysis:

  • RBI Approval: RBI approved SBI Mutual Fund to acquire up to 9.99% of Karur Vysya Bank's paid-up share capital or voting rights.

  • Ownership Structure: SBI holds a 63% majority stake in SBI Funds Management Pvt Ltd (SBIFMPL), while AMUNDI Asset Management owns the remaining 37%.

  • Approval Conditions: SBI Mutual Fund must secure the majority shareholding within one year, keep ownership below 9.99%, and seek new RBI approval if it drops below 5%. Failure to comply may lead to revocation.
content_snippet:
RBI Approves SBI MF to Acquire 9.99% Stake in Karur Vysya Bank.


Expert Analysis:

RBI Approval: RBI approved SBI Mutual Fund to acquire up to 9.99% of Karur Vysya Bank's paid-up share capital or voting rights.

Ownership Structure: SBI holds a 63% majority stake in SBI Funds Management Pvt Ltd (SBIFMPL), while AMUNDI Asset Management owns the remaining 37%.

Approval Conditions: SBI Mutual Fund must secure the majority shareholding within one year, keep ownership below 9.99%, and seek new RBI approval if it drops below 5%. Failure to comply may lead to revocation.
takeaways: The RBI has approved SBI Mutual Fund's acquisition of up to 9.99% of Karur Vysya Bank, with strict regulatory conditions and timelines.
takeaways_snippet: The RBI has approved SBI Mutual Fund's acquisition of up to 9.99% of Karur Vysya Bank, with strict regulatory conditions and timelines.

title: MobiKwik posts Rs 875 Cr revenue and Rs 14 Cr profit in FY24
content:
MobiKwik Reports Rs 875 Cr Revenue and Rs 14 Cr Profit for FY24.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strong FY24 Recovery: IPO-bound MobiKwik made a strong comeback in FY24, with revenue surging over 62% and achieving profitability, recovering from an Rs 84 crore loss in FY23.

  • Revenue Growth: MobiKwik’s revenue from operations increased to Rs 875 crore in FY24 from Rs 539 crore in FY23, driven by commissions on transactions, loan servicing interest, and payment gateways.

  • Payment Gateway Growth: In FY24, MobiKwik expanded its loan offerings, leading to a 3X increase in lending operational costs to Rs 270 crore from Rs 69 crore in FY23, while its payment gateway revenue grew 18.4% to Rs 201 crore.

  • IPO Preparation: MobiKwik filed its DRHP with SEBI to raise Rs 700 crore and raised Rs 50 crore ($6 million) in debt from BlackSoil Capital.

MobiKwik Reports Rs 875 Cr Revenue and Rs 14 Cr Profit for FY24.


Expert Analysis:

Strong FY24 Recovery: IPO-bound MobiKwik made a strong comeback in FY24, with revenue surging over 62% and achieving profitability, recovering from an Rs 84 crore loss in FY23.

Revenue Growth: MobiKwik’s revenue from operations increased to Rs 875 crore in FY24 from Rs 539 crore in FY23, driven by commissions on transactions, loan servicing interest, and payment gateways.

Payment Gateway Growth: In FY24, MobiKwik expanded its loan offerings, leading to a 3X increase in lending operational costs to Rs 270 crore from Rs 69 crore in FY23, while its payment gateway revenue grew 18.4% to Rs 201 crore.

IPO Preparation: MobiKwik filed its DRHP with SEBI to raise Rs 700 crore and raised Rs 50 crore ($6 million) in debt from BlackSoil Capital.
takeaways: MobiKwik's strong financial recovery and profitability in FY24 significantly enhanced its IPO prospects.

title: Swiggy Gears Up to File its Draft Red Herring Prospectus by September
content:
Swiggy Gears Up to File its DRHP for a Rs 10,400 Crore IPO By September.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Filing Plans: Swiggy plans to file its DRHP by early September, aiming to raise Rs 10,400 crore through its IPO, potentially making it the second venture capital-backed startup after Paytm to exceed Rs 10,000 crore in a public listing.

  • Confidential SEBI Filing: Swiggy filed documents confidentially with SEBI for the Rs 10,400 crore IPO, using SEBI’s confidentiality route, allowing private preliminary filings and flexibility in adjusting fresh shares by up to 50% until an updated DRHP is submitted.

  • Shareholder Approval: In April, Swiggy secured shareholder approval for an IPO, including a fresh issue of Rs 3,750 crore, an offer for sale worth Rs 6,664 crore, and a pre-IPO order of around Rs 750 crore.
content_snippet:

Confidential SEBI Filing: Swiggy filed documents confidentially with SEBI for the Rs 10,400 crore IPO, using SEBI’s confidentiality route, allowing private preliminary filings and flexibility in adjusting fresh shares by up to 50% until an updated DRHP is submitted.

Shareholder Approval: In April, Swiggy secured shareholder approval for an IPO, including a fresh issue of Rs 3,750 crore, an offer for sale worth Rs 6,664 crore, and a pre-IPO order of around Rs 750 crore.

title: Oyo founder Ritesh Agarwal invests Rs 830 crore in latest funding round
content:
OYO Raises Rs 1,457 Crore in Funding Round, Founder Ritesh Agarwal Invests Rs 830 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • Founder Investment: Ritesh Agarwal invested Rs 830 crore in the company’s latest funding round through the Singapore-based entity Patient Capital.

  • Funding Breakdown: Includes Rs 416.85 crore from Incred Wealth in July 2024 and Rs 1,047 crore approved by shareholders on August 8 in an extraordinary general meeting (EGM).

  • Leading Investors: InCred Wealth invested Rs 76 crore in addition to Rs 416.85 crore previously, while J&A Partners, the Mankind Pharma family office, invested Rs 120 crore, and ASK contributed Rs 14 crore.

  • IPO Plans: The Sources confirmed that it will proceed with the IPO only after achieving several profitable quarters, which it anticipates will occur.

  • Founder’s Ownership: Ritesh Agarwal's stake will increase from 29.97% to 32.57% following the Series G funding round dilution.
content_snippet:
OYO Raises Rs 1,457 Crore in Funding Round, Founder Ritesh Agarwal Invests Rs 830 Crore.


Expert Analysis:

Founder Investment: Ritesh Agarwal invested Rs 830 crore in the company’s latest funding round through the Singapore-based entity Patient Capital.

Funding Breakdown: Includes Rs 416.85 crore from Incred Wealth in July 2024 and Rs 1,047 crore approved by shareholders on August 8 in an extraordinary general meeting (EGM).

Leading Investors: InCred Wealth invested Rs 76 crore in addition to Rs 416.85 crore previously, while J&A Partners, the Mankind Pharma family office, invested Rs 120 crore, and ASK contributed Rs 14 crore.

IPO Plans: The Sources confirmed that it will proceed with the IPO only after achieving several profitable quarters, which it anticipates will occur.

Founder’s Ownership: Ritesh Agarwal's stake will increase from 29.97% to 32.57% following the Series G funding round dilution.
takeaways: OYO's valuation Stands at $2.4 billion, with Ritesh Agarwal increasing it’s stake.
takeaways_snippet: OYO's valuation Stands at $2.4 billion, with Ritesh Agarwal increasing it’s stake.

title: Nayara Energy secures patent for revolutionary process enhancing petroleum distillate quality
content:
Nayara Energy secures patent for revolutionary process enhancing petroleum distillate quality.

Expert Analysis:

  • Patent Achievement: Nayara Energy Limited patents an innovative adsorbent technology, significantly enhancing the quality and stability of petroleum distillates, including kerosene, aviation turbine fuel, and mineral turpentine oil.

  • Patent Details: Indian Patent No. 545797 outlines a method for enhancing the purification of kerosene-type petroleum distillates, addressing previous stability and color issues.

  • Environmental Impact: The adsorbent is treated with water to recover hydrocarbon content and regenerate via thermal oxidation, minimizing environmental influence.

  • Refinery Benefits: The process increases refinery productivity and flexibility by adjusting operations for various processing scenarios. Amar Kumar, Head of Refinery at Nayara Energy, highlights the patent’s role in showcasing innovation and improving product quality.
content_snippet:
Nayara Energy secures patent for revolutionary process enhancing petroleum distillate quality.


Expert Analysis:

Patent Achievement: Nayara Energy Limited patents an innovative adsorbent technology, significantly enhancing the quality and stability of petroleum distillates, including kerosene, aviation turbine fuel, and mineral turpentine oil.

Patent Details: Indian Patent No. 545797 outlines a method for enhancing the purification of kerosene-type petroleum distillates, addressing previous stability and color issues.

Environmental Impact: The adsorbent is treated with water to recover hydrocarbon content and regenerate via thermal oxidation, minimizing environmental influence.

Refinery Benefits: The process increases refinery productivity and flexibility by adjusting operations for various processing scenarios. Amar Kumar, Head of Refinery at Nayara Energy, highlights the patent’s role in showcasing innovation and improving product quality.
takeaways: Nayara Energy’s patent boosts petroleum distillate quality and refinery efficiency, highlighting their industry innovation.
takeaways_snippet: Nayara Energy’s patent boosts petroleum distillate quality and refinery efficiency, highlighting their industry innovation.

title: OYO Secures ₹1,457 Crore Funding for Global Expansion
content:
OYO Raises ₹1,457 Crore for Global Growth.

Expert Analysis:

  • Funding Amount & Utilization: Oravel Stays Ltd (OYO) has successfully raised ₹1,457 crore from a group of investors in its latest Series G funding round. The funding will be used for global expansion and growth.

  • Investors: Key investors include InCred Wealth, J&A Partners, ASK Financial Holdings, and Patient Capital Investments Pte Ltd.

  • Valuation: The company’s valuation remains at $2.4 billion, as observed in the first tranche of Series G issued to InCred in July, with investment made through Compulsory Convertible Cumulative Preference Shares each priced at ₹29 per share.

  • Shareholder Approvals and Key Appointments: Shareholders approved increasing authorized share capital from ₹1,341 crore to ₹1,631 crore. Sumer Juneja Managing Partner and Head of EMEA & India at SoftBank Investment Advisors was appointed as a Non-Executive Director with 99.99% approval and will represent SoftBank Vision Fund.
content_snippet:
OYO Raises ₹1,457 Crore for Global Growth.


Expert Analysis:

Funding Amount & Utilization: Oravel Stays Ltd (OYO) has successfully raised ₹1,457 crore from a group of investors in its latest Series G funding round. The funding will be used for global expansion and growth.

Investors: Key investors include InCred Wealth, J&A Partners, ASK Financial Holdings, and Patient Capital Investments Pte Ltd.

Valuation: The company’s valuation remains at $2.4 billion, as observed in the first tranche of Series G issued to InCred in July, with investment made through Compulsory Convertible Cumulative Preference Shares each priced at ₹29 per share.

Shareholder Approvals and Key Appointments: Shareholders approved increasing authorized share capital from ₹1,341 crore to ₹1,631 crore. Sumer Juneja Managing Partner and Head of EMEA & India at SoftBank Investment Advisors was appointed as a Non-Executive Director with 99.99% approval and will represent SoftBank Vision Fund.
takeaways: OYO secured ₹1,457 crore in Series G funding, maintaining a $2.4 billion valuation, with strong investor and shareholder backing.

title: Arohan Financial Services Reports Record Profit Surge in Q1 2024-25
content:
Arohan Financial Services Reports Fourfold Profit Increase in Q1 FY 2024-25.

Expert Analysis:

  • Net Profit Surge: Arohan Financial Services achieved its net profit, reaching Rs 104.08 crore for Q1 FY 2024-25.

  • Drivers of Profit Growth: The increase from Rs 25.40 crore in the same quarter of the PY is attributed to steady growth in business operations and earnings.

  • Income and Asset Growth: Total income surged 36% to Rs 469 crore, up from Rs 345 crore last year, while assets under management grew to Rs 7,062 crore by the end of June, compared to Rs 5,564 crore a year earlier.

  • Operational Efficiency: 

    • MD Manoj Nambiar attributed the profit increase to a quality-first approach and zero net NPAs. 

    • The company's strategic expansion into South India and its network of 975 branches serving 2.4 million customers across the country.
content_snippet:

Income and Asset Growth: Total income surged 36% to Rs 469 crore, up from Rs 345 crore last year, while assets under management grew to Rs 7,062 crore by the end of June, compared to Rs 5,564 crore a year earlier.

The company's strategic expansion into South India and its network of 975 branches serving 2.4 million customers across the country.

title: Polymatech Acquires Nisene Technology to Revolutionize Semiconductors Industry
content:
Polymatech Acquires Nisene, Invests $500M to Lead in Advanced Semiconductors.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Acquisition: Polymatech Electronics strengthens its position in advanced semiconductor chip fabrication by acquiring Nisene Technology Group Inc., a California-based firm with a 50-year legacy.

  • Industry Leader: Nisene Technology Group, established in the 1970s, is renowned for its groundbreaking advancements in Silicon and Silicon Carbide ICs, with 50+ IC designs and numerous patents, solidifying its role as a semiconductor industry cornerstone.

  • Major Investment and Production Plans: Polymatech, plans to invest up to $500 million to produce Silicon Carbide and Sapphire wafers, high-performance CPUs, GPUs, and advanced networking chips, with  6G standards targeting $5 billion in revenue by 2030.

  • Management View :

    • CEO Eswara Rao Nandam highlights that Nisene’s 50 years of expertise, now part of Polymatech, will drive next-generation semiconductor development.

    • Polymatech plans to double chip power and halve costs every 18 months, fostering innovation in autonomous driving and machine learning.

CEO Eswara Rao Nandam highlights that Nisene’s 50 years of expertise, now part of Polymatech, will drive next-generation semiconductor development.

Polymatech plans to double chip power and halve costs every 18 months, fostering innovation in autonomous driving and machine learning.
takeaways: Polymatech’s acquisition of Nisene boost its semiconductor capabilities, aiming for $5 billion in revenue by 2030.

title: Studds Accessories re-ignites plans for IPO
content:
Studds Accessories Revives IPO Plans Amid Strong Market Demand.

Expert Analysis:

  • Market Position: Studds Accessories Ltd holds a 25% market share in two-wheeler helmets and is revisiting its IPO plans. The 2018 IPO aimed to raise ₹98 crore through a fresh issue and offer for sale of 3.9 million shares; the renewed IPO might feature a larger offer for sale due to strong cash generation.

  • Share Price Trends: Shares are trading around ₹950, up from ₹825 in May but down from ₹2000 in 2021.

  • Financial Performance and Sales Health: 

    • Revenue grew over three times to ₹506.5 crore by FY23 Despite consistent profitability over the past decade, net profit have been Halved from its Peak. 

    • The company sold over 6.4 million helmets and luggage boxes and while it exports to over 65 countries in FY23, with 87% of sales in India.

Share Price Trends: Shares are trading around ₹950, up from ₹825 in May but down from ₹2000 in 2021.

title: Tata Capital’s unlisted shares surge 46% over last 6 months
content:

title: Hero FinCorp Files Draft Papers To Raise Rs 3,668 Crore Via IPO
content:
Hero Fincorp Files DRHP with SEBI for IPO to Raise Rs 3,668 Crore.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Details: 

    • Hero Fincorp Ltd. an associate company of Hero MotoCorp., has filed its preliminary papers with SEBI to raise Rs 3,668 crore Via IPO.

    • The IPO includes a fresh issue of Rs 2,100 crore and an offer-for-sale of Rs 1,568 crore, according to the DRHP.

  • Selling Shareholders in OFS:

    • AHVF II Holdings Singapore II Pte., Apis Growth II (Hibiscus) Pte., Link Investment Trust, and Otter Ltd. will be the investors selling shareholders under the OFS.

    • AHVF II Holdings holds about 10.34% stake, while Apis Growth holds 1.24% stake in the non-banking financial lender.

  • IPO Use: Proceeds from the fresh issue will be used to increase the company's capital to meet future funding, with equity shares of the company to be listed on BSE and NSE.

  • Financial Product Offerings: The non-banking financial company provides a range of financial products primarily to retail and micro, small, and medium enterprises (MSME) segments, including vehicle, personal, and mortgage loans.

  • Assets Under Management (AUM): 

    • As of FY24, Assets Under Management (AUM) stand at Rs 51,820.8 crore.

    • which retail and MSME loan verticals contributed 65.08% and 20.80%, respectively. 

  • Brand and Corporate Heritage: The NBFC operates under the 'Hero' brand, benefiting from the long-standing corporate heritage, strong track record, and reputation of its parent company, Hero MotoCorp, as noted in the DRHP.

title: NSE inks pact with Defence Ministry to boost capital market access for MSMEs
content:
NSE Signed MoU With Defence Ministry to Boost Capital Market Access for MSME Defence Sector.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Alignment: NSE has signed a five-year MoU with the Ministry of Defence to boost capital market access for MSMEs in the defence sector.

  • Capital Raising Platform: The MoU leverages the NSE Emerge platform for efficient and transparent capital raising.

  • Awareness Campaigns: A collaborative effort between the Department of Defence Production (DDP) and NSE to conduct Extensive awareness campaigns, including seminars, MSME camps, knowledge sessions, road shows, and workshops, to guide defence sector corporates in fundraising on the NSE.

  • Intermediary Connections: NSE will connect MSMEs with intermediaries such as merchant bankers, registrars, transfer agents, and depositories.

  • Guidance on Compliance: The exchange will guide capital markets, capital raising mechanisms, and regulatory compliance requirements.

  • Growth and Innovation: The partnership aims to help MSMEs scale up their operations, explore new markets, and fund their R&D activities.

title: Nayara Energy opts for a digital-first approach in all customer-facing schemes
content:
Nayara Energy Leads with Digital Innovation and Customer-Centric Amenities.

Expert Analysis:

  • Digital-First Approach: Nayara Energy, India's youngest petroleum brand, differentiates itself in the traditionally conservative marketing sector by adopting a digital-first approach, utilizing chatbots and AI to enhance loyalty programs and customer service interactions.

  • Retail Presence: Operates 6,500 retail outlets in the country, contributing more than 4% to India's retail fuel sales.

  • Market Leadership: 

    • It competes with public sector companies Indian Oil Corporation Limited, Hindustan Petroleum, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited, Jio-BP, and Shell.

    •  In the private sector, its rivals are Reliance-backed Jio-BP and Shell, which has just about 1% market share.

  • Market Share and Growth Claims: Private sector companies hold 8.7% of the market, with Nayara as the leading player, followed by Jio-BP; Nayara claims 20% of the market share of annual incremental sales of the sector.

  • Post-Rebrand Loyalty and Value Additions: Nayara focused on offering unique value additions like clean washrooms and certified drinking water to build brand loyalty after rebranding.

  • Rural Expansion: The company emphasizes rural expansion with 50% of new outlets in rural or highway areas or major district roads, It is adding 500 outlets focusing on both urban and rural areas, especially in Western and Southern India.

  • Digital Consumer Schemes: Transitioned consumer schemes online via WhatsApp and QR codes, improving trust and engagement, Taneja reports these schemes have been successful.

  • High Engagement: Over 3 million customers participated in recent promotions, demonstrating the success of digital initiatives.

  • Future AI Integration: Plans to integrate AI into marketing and point-of-sale experiences to further enhance customer interactions.

High Engagement: Over 3 million customers participated in recent promotions, demonstrating the success of digital initiatives.

Future AI Integration: Plans to integrate AI into marketing and point-of-sale experiences to further enhance customer interactions.

    • Prioritizes rural expansion and clean facilities as core offerings.

    • Online consumer schemes via WhatsApp and QR codes boosted trust and engagement, attracting over 3 million participants.

    • Upcoming AI integration in marketing and sales highlights Nayara's commitment to innovation and competitive advantage.

title: Cochin Airport to Implement MHA Trusted Traveller Programme by August 2024
content:
Cochin International Airport to Implement MHA Trusted Traveller Programme in August 2024.

Expert Analysis:

  • Implementation Timeline: 

    • Cochin International Airport Limited (CIAL) is to implement the Ministry of Home Affairs Trusted Traveller Programme expected launch by August 2024.

    • CIAL will be the second airport in India to do so, using biometric e-gates to significantly reduce immigration verification time.

  • Technological Features:

    • Eight state-of-the-art biometric e-gates at Cochin International Airport will enable international travellers to complete the immigration process in just 20 seconds.

    • This innovation follows a similar implementation at Delhi International Airport in June.

  • MHA's Fast Track Immigration: The MHA's Fast Track Immigration-Trusted Travellers' Programme (FTI-TTP) expedites clearance for eligible Indian and OCI cardholders through smart gates, allowing self-scanning of passports and face recognition, eliminating long queues and human interaction.

  • Passenger Experience and Safety: Managing Director S Suhas IAS stated that Enrollment facilities and help desks are set up at CIAL, with the biometric system reducing immigration verification time from four minutes to 20 seconds, enhancing travel experience and safety.

  • Operational Capacity: 

    • Handling 10 million passengers and 70,200 air traffic movements annually, CIAL is the fourth busiest airport for international traffic in India.

    • it also features the Digiyatra facility and has several modernization projects, including a Perimeter Intrusion Detection System.

title: IRDAI fines Care Health Insurance Rs 1 cr, asks to cancel ESOPs issued to Rashmi Saluja
content:
IRDAI Orders CHIL to Cancel ESOPs for Rashmi Saluja and Pay Rs 1 Crore Fine.

Expert Analysis:

  • IRDAI Order: IRDAI directs Care Health Insurance to Cancel ESOPs  75,69,685 shares from Rashmi Saluja at Rs 45.32 per share within 30 days, and cancel unexercised stock options within 15 days, and pay a Rs 1 crore fine.

  • IRDAI Act: IRDAI's July 23 order cites CHIL's stock options issuance to Saluja as a violation of the Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority Act, 1999, and related guidelines.

  • Prior Approval: IRDAI mandates that CHIL must seek prior approval for board decisions on remuneration, payments, and benefits until further notice.

  • IRDAI Stresses Compliance: IRDAI clarified that Saluja's role as executive chairperson of REL does not exempt CHIL from adhering to IRDAI regulations and guidelines.

IRDAI Stresses Compliance: IRDAI clarified that Saluja's role as executive chairperson of REL does not exempt CHIL from adhering to IRDAI regulations and guidelines.

title: HDFC Bank Prepares for HDB Financial Services IPO to Meet RBI Listing Mandate
content:
HDFC Bank Gears Up for HDB Financial Services IPO to Comply with RBI Mandate.

Expert Analysis:

  • HDFC Bank Approves IPO for HDBFS : HDFC Bank Ltd, India's largest private bank, announced on July 20, 2024, that its Board of Directors approved the process to publicly list its subsidiary, HDB Financial Services (HDBFS), through an initial public offering (IPO), coinciding with the release of its April-June quarterly results, as reported by CNBC-TV18.

  • IPO Approval: HDFC Bank plans to take its subsidiary, HDB Financial Services, public to comply with the Reserve Bank of India’s October 2022 directive requiring “upper layer” Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) to be listed on stock exchanges.

  • Expected Valuation: Expected IPO valuation for HDBFS is between $9 billion and $12 billion. confirmed by an unidentified investment banker, which is dependent on the state of the market.

  • Committee Authorization: A committee of directors is authorized to monitor the listing process and obtain the required approvals from regulatory bodies.

  • New Appointment: Ajay Agarwal is appointed as company secretary and compliance officer, effective July 21, 2024, replacing Santosh Haldankar.
content_snippet:

IPO Approval: HDFC Bank plans to take its subsidiary, HDB Financial Services, public to comply with the Reserve Bank of India’s October 2022 directive requiring “upper layer” Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) to be listed on stock exchanges.

Expected Valuation: Expected IPO valuation for HDBFS is between $9 billion and $12 billion. confirmed by an unidentified investment banker, which is dependent on the state of the market.

Committee Authorization: A committee of directors is authorized to monitor the listing process and obtain the required approvals from regulatory bodies.

New Appointment: Ajay Agarwal is appointed as company secretary and compliance officer, effective July 21, 2024, replacing Santosh Haldankar.

    • HDFC Bank's decision to list HDB Financial Services via an IPO aligns with RBI's mandate.

  • Anticipated Valuation:

    • Anticipated valuation for HDBFS is between $9 billion and $12 billion.

  • Committee Tasking:

    • A committee is tasked with managing the IPO process and securing regulatory approvals.

  • Strategic Move:

    • This strategic move is expected to maximize shareholder value and strengthen HDBFS’s market position.

title: KLM Axiva Finvest To Raise Up To ₹15,000 Lakhs Via NCDs
content:
KLM Axiva Finvest To Raise Up To ₹15,000 Lakhs Via NCDs.

Expert Analysis:

  • Strategic Capital Bolstering: KLM Axiva Finvest Ltd's tenth public issue of secured redeemable non-convertible debentures (NCDs)  with a face value of ₹1000.

  • Flexible Issue Size: It will raise aggregating up to ₹7,500 lakhs (“base issue”) with an option to retain over-subscription up to ₹7,500 lakhs, aggregating up to ₹15,000 lakhs.

  • Issue Dates: The NCD issue, which opened on 22nd July 2024, will be closed on 2nd August 2024.

title: boAt and Zepto Collaborate to Bring Music and Fun in 10 Minutes
content:
boAt Partners with Zepto for 10-Minute Audio Product Delivery.

Expert Analysis:

  • Partnership Announcement: boAt, India’s leading audio and wearable brand, has announced a new partnership with Zepto, the fastest-growing consumer internet company, to enhance consumer access to high-quality audio and entertainment.

  • Delivery Promise: The collaboration aims to deliver top-notch audio experiences directly to consumers’ doorsteps in just 10 minutes.

  • Enhanced Experience: By combining boAt’s expertise in audio products with Zepto’s rapid delivery, the partnership will transform how people enjoy music, movies, podcasts, and more.

  • Promotional Film: The alliance is celebrated with a whimsical film featuring iconic pop culture doppelgängers, humorously depicted being towed around by a Zepto delivery partner.

  • Campaign Highlights: The film concludes with the message: "Delivering the Best of Music and Entertainment in Just 10 Minutes, Powered by boAt and Zepto."

  • Viral Trend: The campaign includes a viral trend on X, "BoatOnRoads," which quickly rose to No. 1.

  • Billboard Campaign: Outdoor billboards feature humorous takes on everyday scenarios, highlighting boAt’s convenient solutions.

  • Statements: boAt spokesperson and Zepto’s Chief Brand Officer emphasize the shared goal of delighting customers with innovation and convenience, promising a seamless and delightful experience.

title: SBI can restrict offerings to only SBI MF schemes: Sebi chairperson Madhabi Puri Buch
content:
SEBI Chairperson: SBI can restrict offerings to only SBI MF schemes.

Expert Analysis:

  • Madhabi Puri Buch, SEBI chairperson, clarified:

    • SEBI Chairperson Madhabi Puri Buch clarified at an SBI Mutual Fund event that Banks only selling mutual fund products offered by their sister concern is acceptable unless they’re mis-selling.

    • According to FY22 data from the Association of Mutual Funds of India, about 97% of State Bank of India's mutual fund commissions came from SBI Mutual Fund.

    • In the same period, 52% of SBI AMC’s commission payout (₹711 crore) went to its parent bank, as per the AMC’s annual disclosure data.  

  • Buch emphasized:

    • Buch told Mint, “If an entity decides to sell its own group’s products, I see no issue with it. If SBI has the might of its distribution, why should they not use it.”

    • Buch added that if a fund is underperforming and its sale amounts to mis-selling, safeguards are necessary, but she is confident that the mutual fund ecosystem will address this issue.

    • During the event, Buch recounted how, while leading ICICI Caps (later ICICI Securities), she was questioned about not promoting ICICI Mutual Fund schemes.

  • SBI Open Architecture: Dinesh Khara, SBI chairperson, states that SBI's open architecture allows the bank to sell mutual fund schemes from any company.

  • SBI Commission Data: Despite this, data shows that an overwhelming majority of SBI’s commissions come from SBI Mutual Fund.
content_snippet:
SEBI Chairperson: SBI can restrict offerings to only SBI MF schemes.


Expert Analysis:

Madhabi Puri Buch, SEBI chairperson, clarified:

SEBI Chairperson Madhabi Puri Buch clarified at an SBI Mutual Fund event that Banks only selling mutual fund products offered by their sister concern is acceptable unless they’re mis-selling.

According to FY22 data from the Association of Mutual Funds of India, about 97% of State Bank of India's mutual fund commissions came from SBI Mutual Fund.

In the same period, 52% of SBI AMC’s commission payout (₹711 crore) went to its parent bank, as per the AMC’s annual disclosure data.  

Buch emphasized:

Buch told Mint, “If an entity decides to sell its own group’s products, I see no issue with it. If SBI has the might of its distribution, why should they not use it.”

Buch added that if a fund is underperforming and its sale amounts to mis-selling, safeguards are necessary, but she is confident that the mutual fund ecosystem will address this issue.

During the event, Buch recounted how, while leading ICICI Caps (later ICICI Securities), she was questioned about not promoting ICICI Mutual Fund schemes.

SBI Open Architecture: Dinesh Khara, SBI chairperson, states that SBI's open architecture allows the bank to sell mutual fund schemes from any company.

SBI Commission Data: Despite this, data shows that an overwhelming majority of SBI’s commissions come from SBI Mutual Fund.
takeaways:
• SEBI’s Madhabi Puri Buch stressed:

    • Selling only sister-concern mutual funds is fine unless mis-selling occurs.

  • FY22 data highlights:

    • 97% of SBI's mutual fund commissions came from SBI Mutual Fund.

    • ₹711 crore (52%) of SBI AMC’s commission payout went to SBI.

  • SBI chairperson Dinesh Khara noted:

    • SBI’s open architecture for selling various mutual fund schemes.
takeaways_snippet:
SEBI’s Madhabi Puri Buch stressed:

Selling only sister-concern mutual funds is fine unless mis-selling occurs.

FY22 data highlights:

97% of SBI's mutual fund commissions came from SBI Mutual Fund.

₹711 crore (52%) of SBI AMC’s commission payout went to SBI.

SBI chairperson Dinesh Khara noted:

SBI’s open architecture for selling various mutual fund schemes.

title: Bira 91 Maker Plans To Go Public In 2026
content:
B9 Beverages (Bira 91) Plans 2026 IPO.

Expert Analysis:

  • IPO Plans: B9 Beverages Ltd, owner of Bira 91, plans an IPO in 2026 and Morgan Stanley has been tapped for the pre-IPO process.

  • Recent Funding: The company raised $25 Mn (about INR 208 Cr) last month via External Commercial Borrowing (ECB) from its existing backer Kirin Holdings.

  • Public Entity Status: Founded in 2015, Bira 91 is now a public entity renamed B9 Beverages Ltd in December 2022.

  • Market Position: Bira 91 claims to be the fourth-largest beer company in India with a 5% market share across key regions.

  • Company Background and Investor Profile: Founded in 2015 by Jain, B9 Beverages is known for its craft beer, ciders, and seltzers, with investors including Peak XV Partners, Sofina, and DS Group, having raised $449 Mn in funding so far.

  • Operational Reach: Bira 91 claims to sell its beer in 550 towns across 18 countries.

  • Financial Performance: The company's net loss rose 12% to INR 445.4 Cr in FY23 from INR 396 Cr in FY22, burdened by higher expenses and excise duty.

title: Share price below Rs 5 and DIVIDEND Rs 20 - July 22 is record date
content:
Taparia Tools set to pay 200% Dividend, Shares Surge 103% in Last Year.

Expert Analysis:

  • Dividend Announcement: Earlier in May, Taparia Tools, a penny stock below Rs 5, announced a 200% dividend on each stock of Rs 10 face value, resulting in a Rs 20 per share payout.

  • Dividend Dates Set: Taparia Tools has set July 22 as the ex-date for the Rs 20 dividend and scheduled book closure from July 23 to 29, as per the BSE website.

  • Board Recommendation: The Board of Directors of the Company has inter-alia considered and recommended this dividend from the Distributable Profit for FY 2023-24, as per the company’s regulatory filing.

  • Share Performance: Taparia Tools, specializing in manufacturing and trading hand tools, saw its shares close at a 5% upper circuit of Rs 4.27 on Monday.

  • Dividend Explanation: Dividends, typically paid in cash, represent a percentage of a company's profits recommended by the board and approved by its members, distributed to shareholders.

  • Dividend History and Bonus Issue: Taparia Tools, listed on the BSE, has a dividend history that includes a Rs 20 dividend per share in February, with additional dividends of Rs 77.50 each in March and June 2023, and a 4:1 bonus issue in July. In 2022 and 2021, the company rewarded investors with dividends of Rs 102.50 and Rs 70 per share were distributed, respectively.

  • Share Performance and Market Cap: Taparia Tools shares achieved a 103% return over the past year, as reported by BSE analytics on July 17, with a market cap of Rs 6.48 crore.

title: IRDAI puts Care Health Insurance on notice over ESOPs to Saluja
content:
IRDAI Issues Notice to Care Health Insurance Over Unauthorized ESOPs to Chairperson Saluja.

Expert Analysis: 

  • Regulatory Action: India's insurance regulator IRDAI issued a show cause notice to Care Health Insurance, a Religare Enterprises subsidiary, over stock options granted to Religare chairperson Rashmi Saluja.

  • Board Conflict: The Religare board and Dabur's Burman family are in a protracted control battle, with the Burmans accusing the board of unlawfully granting stock options to Rashmi Saluja and alleging non-compliance with IRDAI regulations.

  • IRDAI Directive: Stock options were issued to Rashmi Saluja despite IRDAI's May 2022 directive not approving Care from granting ESOPs to her as a non-executive director.

  • ESOP Value: Despite IRDAI guidelines capping non-executive director payments at ₹10 lakh per year, Care issued ESOPs to Rashmi Saluja as a Religare Enterprises employee, which IRDAI views as defiance of its directives.

  • Regulatory Consequences: The company faces a show cause notice demanding justification for avoiding penalties and proceedings under the Insurance Act and IRDAI Act 1999.

  • Investigation into Stock Options: ET reported that IRDAI is investigating the issuance of stock options worth ₹250 crore to Rashmi Saluja by Care Health, representing approximately 2.5% of the insurer's unlisted shareholding.

  • Discrepancy in Share Pricing: Proxy advisory firm InGovern reported that Care Health Insurance issued stock options at an exercise price of ₹45.32 per share in January 2022, while the rights issue that year was priced at ₹110 per share.

  • Burman Family Allegations: The Burman family, the largest shareholder of Religare Enterprises claims 22.7 million options were issued illegally and sought to cancel these stock options.

  • SAT Compliance Directive: The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) directed Religare Enterprises to apply for an open offer and change in control by July 22, following orders from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) issued on June 19, 2024.
content_snippet:
IRDAI Issues Notice to Care Health Insurance Over Unauthorized ESOPs to Chairperson Saluja.


Expert Analysis: 

Regulatory Action: India's insurance regulator IRDAI issued a show cause notice to Care Health Insurance, a Religare Enterprises subsidiary, over stock options granted to Religare chairperson Rashmi Saluja.

Board Conflict: The Religare board and Dabur's Burman family are in a protracted control battle, with the Burmans accusing the board of unlawfully granting stock options to Rashmi Saluja and alleging non-compliance with IRDAI regulations.

IRDAI Directive: Stock options were issued to Rashmi Saluja despite IRDAI's May 2022 directive not approving Care from granting ESOPs to her as a non-executive director.

ESOP Value: Despite IRDAI guidelines capping non-executive director payments at ₹10 lakh per year, Care issued ESOPs to Rashmi Saluja as a Religare Enterprises employee, which IRDAI views as defiance of its directives.

Regulatory Consequences: The company faces a show cause notice demanding justification for avoiding penalties and proceedings under the Insurance Act and IRDAI Act 1999.

Investigation into Stock Options: ET reported that IRDAI is investigating the issuance of stock options worth ₹250 crore to Rashmi Saluja by Care Health, representing approximately 2.5% of the insurer's unlisted shareholding.

Discrepancy in Share Pricing: Proxy advisory firm InGovern reported that Care Health Insurance issued stock options at an exercise price of ₹45.32 per share in January 2022, while the rights issue that year was priced at ₹110 per share.

Burman Family Allegations: The Burman family, the largest shareholder of Religare Enterprises claims 22.7 million options were issued illegally and sought to cancel these stock options.

SAT Compliance Directive: The Securities Appellate Tribunal (SAT) directed Religare Enterprises to apply for an open offer and change in control by July 22, following orders from the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) issued on June 19, 2024.
takeaways:
• Regulatory Challenge:

    • Care Health Insurance is scrutinized for issuing ₹250 crore in stock options to Rashmi Saluja against IRDAI's directive.

  • Governance Conflict:

    • The Burman family, Religare's major shareholders, alleges that 22.7 million options were illicitly issued and demands their cancellation.

  • Compliance Mandate:

    • The Securities Appellate Tribunal has ordered Religare Enterprises to follow SEBI's directions on open offer and control change by July 22.

  • Governance and Compliance Risks:

    • This situation highlights significant governance and regulatory adherence issues within Care Health Insurance.

title: HDFC Bank in talks with credit funds to sell 20% of HDB Financial
content:
HDFC Bank in Early Talks with Credit Funds to Sell 20% Stake in HDB Financial.

Expert Analysis:

  • HDFC Bank's Stake Sale Discussions: 

    • HDFC Bank is in initial discussions with five domestic credit funds to sell a 4-7% stake each in its IPO-bound lending arm, HDB Financial Services. 

    • As part of an ongoing value discovery process, valuations will be a critical factor for the transaction to go through

  • Company and Bank Comment: 

    • HDB Financial’s spokesperson stated that the company does not comment on market speculations, A spokesperson for parent HDFC Bank declined to comment. 

    • The current talks with private credit funds follow a rough patch in earlier discussions with large investors due to valuation disagreements.

  • Investor Demands and Previous Interest: 

    • Some investors have requested board seats in exchange for acquiring a significant stake, such as 20%, in HDB Financial, though the specifics of these requests remain unclear.

    • Additionally, Japan’s Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group had shown interest in acquiring a significant stake in HDB Financial, as reported by the Economic Times in April.

  • HDB Financial's Valuation and Financials:

    • HDFC Bank owns a 94.64% stake in HDB Financial Services, which had a loan book of over Rs 90,000 crore as of March 31. 

    • HDB Financial is seeking a valuation of 4-5 times its book value. As of FY23, the book value per share was Rs 144.52, and shareholders' funds amounted to Rs 11,437 crore. 

    • However, sector-wide valuation declines, including those affecting Bajaj Finance, suggest that HDB’s high valuation request might be challenging.

  • Secondary Share Sale and IPO Requirement: A secondary share sale by HDFC Bank is considered a crucial step for price discovery ahead of HDB Financial’s IPO, which is required by RBI regulations governing NBFCs, to be listed by October 2025.

  • Historical Listing Aspirations and Regulatory Deadline: 

    • HDB Financial Services, a project favored by former HDFC Bank CEO Aditya Puri, has been aiming for a listing for many years. 

    • Puri had sought to list the company before his term ended in October 2020. With NBFC regulations implemented in October 2022, unlisted systemically important NBFCs like HDB Financial, Tata Capital, and Bajaj Housing Finance must be listed within three years.

  • Current IPO Progress: While Bajaj Housing Finance has initiated its IPO process, progress for other companies, including HDB Financial, is slow. Investment bankers report that discussions about HDB Financial’s IPO are minimal.
content_snippet:
HDFC Bank in Early Talks with Credit Funds to Sell 20% Stake in HDB Financial.

Expert Analysis:

HDFC Bank's Stake Sale Discussions: 

HDFC Bank is in initial discussions with five domestic credit funds to sell a 4-7% stake each in its IPO-bound lending arm, HDB Financial Services. 

As part of an ongoing value discovery process, valuations will be a critical factor for the transaction to go through

Company and Bank Comment: 

HDB Financial’s spokesperson stated that the company does not comment on market speculations, A spokesperson for parent HDFC Bank declined to comment. 

The current talks with private credit funds follow a rough patch in earlier discussions with large investors due to valuation disagreements.

Investor Demands and Previous Interest: 

Some investors have requested board seats in exchange for acquiring a significant stake, such as 20%, in HDB Financial, though the specifics of these requests remain unclear.

Additionally, Japan’s Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group had shown interest in acquiring a significant stake in HDB Financial, as reported by the Economic Times in April.

HDB Financial's Valuation and Financials:

HDFC Bank owns a 94.64% stake in HDB Financial Services, which had a loan book of over Rs 90,000 crore as of March 31. 

HDB Financial is seeking a valuation of 4-5 times its book value. As of FY23, the book value per share was Rs 144.52, and shareholders' funds amounted to Rs 11,437 crore. 

However, sector-wide valuation declines, including those affecting Bajaj Finance, suggest that HDB’s high valuation request might be challenging.

Secondary Share Sale and IPO Requirement: A secondary share sale by HDFC Bank is considered a crucial step for price discovery ahead of HDB Financial’s IPO, which is required by RBI regulations governing NBFCs, to be listed by October 2025.

Historical Listing Aspirations and Regulatory Deadline: 

HDB Financial Services, a project favored by former HDFC Bank CEO Aditya Puri, has been aiming for a listing for many years. 

Puri had sought to list the company before his term ended in October 2020. With NBFC regulations implemented in October 2022, unlisted systemically important NBFCs like HDB Financial, Tata Capital, and Bajaj Housing Finance must be listed within three years.

Current IPO Progress: While Bajaj Housing Finance has initiated its IPO process, progress for other companies, including HDB Financial, is slow. Investment bankers report that discussions about HDB Financial’s IPO are minimal.
takeaways:
• Stake Sale Negotiations:

    • HDFC Bank is negotiating with five domestic credit funds to sell a 4-7% stake each in HDB Financial Services.

  • Valuation Discovery:

    • These discussions aim to determine HDB Financial's valuation ahead of its IPO, mandated by October 2025.

  • Financial Overview:

    • HDB Financial's loan book is over Rs 90,000 crore, with shareholders' funds at Rs 11,437 crore.

  • Valuation Challenges:

    • The company seeks a high valuation of 4-5 times book value, despite sector-wide valuation challenges.

Valuation Discovery:

These discussions aim to determine HDB Financial's valuation ahead of its IPO, mandated by October 2025.

Financial Overview:

Valuation Challenges:

title: India's Nayara Energy has a rare cargo of vacuum gasoil for Sept
content:
India's Nayara Energy Offers Rare Cargo of Vacuum Gasoil for September Loading.

Expert Analysis:

  • Rare Tender Issuance: As per the sources, Nayara Energy issued a rare tender for 70,000 metric tonnes of vacuum gasoil loading(VGO) with a maximum of 3% sulphur.

  • Loading Schedule: Scheduled for loading from Vadinar Port in Gujarat state on the west coast of India between September 1 and 10.

  • Historical Context: Based on data from Kpler & LSEG ship tracking, Last VGO cargo was exported during Vadinar maintenance in December 2022.

  • Pricing and Tender Details: Sources indicated that the cargo is typically priced on a Dubai Crude basis. The latest tender will close on Thursday, with same-day validity.

title: Fintech Startup MobiKwik's Wallet soars & market shares expand to 12.11%
content:
Fintech Startup MobiKwik's Wallet Soars, Market Share Expands to 12.11%.

Expert Analysis:

  • Market Share Growth: MobiKwik’s PPI (prepaid payment instrument wallet market share increased to 12.11% in May, up from 9.3% in January.

  • Expansion Strategy: The Gurugram-based organization saw this surge and extended its presence into Tier II and - III urban communities as other key part in the financial space went through a slump. 

  • Pocket UPI Drive MobiKwik's Growth: MobiKwik attributes its growth to expansion efforts and the success of its Pocket UPI service introduced in February, which nearly doubled its Gross Transaction Value (GTV) share from 11% in March to 23% in May.

  • Wallet Growth and Ranking: MobiKwik's wallet base increased by 2% from 134.1 million in January to 136.8 million in May, placing it second among non-bank players, just behind PhonePe's 199.3 million wallets as of May.

  • Impact on Competitors: Paytm Payments Bank experienced a significant 39% decline in wallet numbers, dropping from 630.7 million in January to 380.5 million in May, due to stringent RBI restrictions halting its operations.

  • Innovation in Wallet Interoperability: Upasana Taku, Co-Founder, and CFO, highlights MobiKwik's strategy of enhancing wallet interoperability through the Pocket UPI feature, allowing users to utilize their wallet within the UPI ecosystem. This innovation addresses user needs amid regulatory challenges faced by competitors.

  • Expansion in User Base: Taku notes a significant influx of new users into the digital ecosystem, particularly from tier-II and III cities and towns in India, comprising approximately 70-75% of MobiKwik's new user acquisitions.

title: INKEL net profit increases by 114% to ₹30.74 crore
content:
INKEL Reports 114% Surge in Net Profit to ₹30.74 Crore for FY 2023-24.

Expert Analysis:

  • Turnover Growth: INKEL Limited, a state government public-private partnership, has reported a turnover of ₹115.10 crore for FY 2023-24, up 15% from the previous financial year, with net profit increased by 114% to ₹ 30.74 crore.

  • Corporate Performance and Strategic Initiatives: Based on improved performance through resource optimization and project diversification, INKEL's board recommends dividends, pending approval at the annual general meeting. The company is pivotal in local renewable energy initiatives and plans expansion into projects outside Kerala.

  • Renewable Energy Expansion: The company's strategic focus on renewable energy includes plans to establish 200 MW plants in Maharashtra, Andhra Pradesh, and Rajasthan within two years.

  • Hybrid Renewable Project: The subsidiary INKEL-Re has been set up to implement 32 MW project, including 14 MW from wind and 18 MW from solar in Palakkad for a hybrid renewable energy project.

  • Consultancy Expertise: INKEL is currently overseeing the construction of 17 government hospital buildings, part of a ₹2,279 crore project funded by the Kerala Infrastructure Investment Fund Board (KIIFB).
