title: Tata Technologies settles shares issuance case with Sebi
content:
Tata Technologies settled with SEBI for an alleged violation of disclosure and investor protection guidelines by paying a settlement amount of Rs 25 lakh.

Justification

  • Tata Technologies proposed the settlement through a suo moto application without admitting or denying any conclusions.

  • SEBI settled the proceedings related to the alleged violations in its order dated September 27, 2023.

  • Tata Technologies had made preferential allotments multiple times between December 27, 2000, and March 13, 2008, issuing shares to over 49 shareholders in a single financial year, violating SEBI's Disclosure and Investor Protection guidelines.

  • Tata Motors, the promoter of Tata Technologies, was required to make an offer to eligible equity shareholders of Tata Technologies on November 11, 2022, for them to tender their shares and obtain a refund. However, no eligible shareholders tendered their shares to the promoter.

  • The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) had previously approved compounding, subject to Tata Technologies paying compounding fees of Rs 3.42 crore.
content_snippet:
Tata Technologies settled with SEBI for an alleged violation of disclosure and investor protection guidelines by paying a settlement amount of Rs 25 lakh.

Justification

Tata Technologies proposed the settlement through a suo moto application without admitting or denying any conclusions.

SEBI settled the proceedings related to the alleged violations in its order dated September 27, 2023.

Tata Technologies had made preferential allotments multiple times between December 27, 2000, and March 13, 2008, issuing shares to over 49 shareholders in a single financial year, violating SEBI's Disclosure and Investor Protection guidelines.

Tata Motors, the promoter of Tata Technologies, was required to make an offer to eligible equity shareholders of Tata Technologies on November 11, 2022, for them to tender their shares and obtain a refund. However, no eligible shareholders tendered their shares to the promoter.

The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) had previously approved compounding, subject to Tata Technologies paying compounding fees of Rs 3.42 crore.
takeaways:
• Tata Technologies can redirect its focus to the core operations of the business by resolving the issue. 

  • Settlements also avoid lengthy legal proceedings & save time & effort for the companies.
takeaways_snippet:
Tata Technologies can redirect its focus to the core operations of the business by resolving the issue. 

Settlements also avoid lengthy legal proceedings & save time & effort for the companies.

title: Exclusive: Waaree Group aims to raise Rs 2,000 crore through IPO: CMD Hitesh Doshi
content:
Waaree Energies Limited’s Chairman & MD Hitesh Chimanlal Doshi’s interview Highlights 

Justification

  • It plans to raise Rs 2000 Cr through IPO within this FY, & is working on its DRHP.

  • It plans for solar expansion in two phases-

    • Setting up 5.4 GW solar cell manufacturing capacity in an existing plant in Chikhli, Gujarat, costing Rs 2,800 crore by March 2024 

    • planning to set up a 6 GW ingot-to-wafer-to-module manufacturing unit, costing approx Rs 8,000 crore, expected to be ready in March 2025. Waaree is discussing plant locations with the governments of Gujarat, Odisha & Rajasthan.

  • Waaree’s subsidiary, Waaree Renewable Technologies Ltd, plans to set up a 2 GW solar module manufacturing factory in Texas, US.

  • Waaree Group also runs India’s largest NABL-accredited solar module testing laboratory.

  • The imports of solar modules by India are on the rise due to low solar prices by China, from 26% to 14%. 

  • It plans on diversifying into the electrolyser manufacturing business in India starting next year.

  • Grid connectivity and finite land are two of the biggest challenges a/c to the company.

title: Philips OneBlade partners with College Rivals to drive campus presence
content:
Philips OneBlade partners with College Rivals to drive campus presence

Deepali Agarwal, business head of personal health, Philips Indian Subcontinent’s words

“Philips One Blade is our proposition for teenage boys. We want to empower them to make bold and unconventional choices”. 

Justification 

  • With Philip’s India's third strategic partnership in a year, the company aims to focus on Gen Z.

  • The collaboration is done with College Rivals, a collegiate esports platform, to increase the visibility of Philips male grooming products.

  • College Rivals will represent Philips OneBlade as it takes its gaming trucks to twenty college campuses in Hyderabad, Mumbai, Pune, and Bangalore. This activation will continue until February 2024.

  • Philips will also be playing its brand film, ‘Move Fearlessly’ on the truck & applying various marketing assets such as L-Brands and other inventory to deliver its key message. 

  • The collaboration aims to redefine the intersection of gaming, grooming, and innovation, offering the brand a chance to connect with a vibrant audience.

title: Waaree plans $350 million IPO fundraise, targets $3 billion valuation
content:
Waaree plans a $350 million IPO fundraising, targeting a $3 billion valuation.

Justification

  • The biggest Solar panel manufacturer with an installed capacity of 12 GW, Waaree Energies, is working with investment banks  Axis Capital, Jefferies, and Nomura on the proposed IPO to raise $300-350 mn at a valuation of $3 billion.

  • The IPO will serve as a means to finance its capex initiatives and offer an exit strategy for recent private investors. In August 2022, it secured Rs 1000 Cr in funding, led by ValueQuest.

  • Waaree, boasting a $5 billion order book and a strong presence in exports, aims to backward integrate into solar cell production with a 5.4 GW capacity. Over the next two years, plans to reach 20 GW in module capacity, 11.4 GW in cell manufacturing, and 6 GW in wafer manufacturing capacity.

  • The Indian government has allocated a manufacturing capacity of 48,337 MW under the PLI scheme to reduce imports from China in the growing renewables sector.
content_snippet:
Waaree plans a $350 million IPO fundraising, targeting a $3 billion valuation.

Justification


The biggest Solar panel manufacturer with an installed capacity of 12 GW, Waaree Energies, is working with investment banks  Axis Capital, Jefferies, and Nomura on the proposed IPO to raise $300-350 mn at a valuation of $3 billion.

The IPO will serve as a means to finance its capex initiatives and offer an exit strategy for recent private investors. In August 2022, it secured Rs 1000 Cr in funding, led by ValueQuest.

Waaree, boasting a $5 billion order book and a strong presence in exports, aims to backward integrate into solar cell production with a 5.4 GW capacity. Over the next two years, plans to reach 20 GW in module capacity, 11.4 GW in cell manufacturing, and 6 GW in wafer manufacturing capacity.

The Indian government has allocated a manufacturing capacity of 48,337 MW under the PLI scheme to reduce imports from China in the growing renewables sector.
takeaways:
• With an EBITDA of over 850 Cr in FY23, expected to double in FY24, the company expects huge growth potential.

  • The proceeds will deleverage the company, providing capital for growth opportunities.
takeaways_snippet:
With an EBITDA of over 850 Cr in FY23, expected to double in FY24, the company expects huge growth potential.

The proceeds will deleverage the company, providing capital for growth opportunities.

title: Lava Eyes 10% Smartphone Market Share In 2 Years With Investment Of Rs 600 Cr In R&D, Marketing
content:
Lava Eyes 10% Smartphone Market Share In 2 Years With Investment Of Rs 600 Cr 

Lava International President Sunil Raina’s words 

‘With the new strategy, Lava's smartphone business grew over 200%  last year, and this year it is growing at 300%’.

Justification 

  • The home-grown mobile device manufacturer currently holds a 2% market share, eyes 10% in the under Rs 30k smartphone segment, followed by Rs. 600 Cr investment in R&D & Marketing.

  • Lava relaunched its smartphone business with a slight pause with a focus on customer experience around devices and providing repair service at home & realized the value of competitive tech for growth & visibility.

  • It has opted not to include bloatware in its smartphones to enhance speed, improve user experience, and address security concerns. With 800 service centers, Lava offers convenient repair-at-home services and hardware replacement for defects.

  • It currently sells products through 1.2 lakh retail stores, which primarily include feature phones only, & claims to have a 25% market share.

title: BOARDWALKTECH AND HEXAWARE ANNOUNCE PARTNERSHIP TO DRIVE BANKING and FINANCIAL SERVICES COMPLIANCE AND PROCESS IMPROVEMENT
content:
Boardwalktech And Hexaware Announce Partnership 

Justification

  • Boardwalktech Software Corp and Hexaware Technologies Limited have signed a teaming agreement to enhance value in the financial services sector.

  • Hexaware, with its offices in 19 countries & over 27k employees, headquartered in Mumbai, is a global tech & business process service company.

  • Hexaware's mission is to accelerate digital transformation for enterprises by enabling them to build, transform, run, and optimize their technology and business processes at scale and speed.

  • Boardwalktech's Velocity platform is tailored for banks and insurance companies, focusing on improving business processes, collaboration, reporting, and compliance for the numerous End User Computing applications in these sectors. It's designed to be delivered through IT services and consulting partners.

  • Fortune 500 companies currently use this platform to manage vast amounts of structured & unstructured data & enable collaboration b/w companies.

  • Boardwalktech is headquartered in Cupertino, California, with offices in India and operations in North America.
content_snippet:
Boardwalktech And Hexaware Announce Partnership 

Justification


Boardwalktech Software Corp and Hexaware Technologies Limited have signed a teaming agreement to enhance value in the financial services sector.

Hexaware, with its offices in 19 countries & over 27k employees, headquartered in Mumbai, is a global tech & business process service company.

Hexaware's mission is to accelerate digital transformation for enterprises by enabling them to build, transform, run, and optimize their technology and business processes at scale and speed.

Boardwalktech's Velocity platform is tailored for banks and insurance companies, focusing on improving business processes, collaboration, reporting, and compliance for the numerous End User Computing applications in these sectors. It's designed to be delivered through IT services and consulting partners.

Fortune 500 companies currently use this platform to manage vast amounts of structured & unstructured data & enable collaboration b/w companies.

Boardwalktech is headquartered in Cupertino, California, with offices in India and operations in North America.
takeaways:
• The combined partnership of the two tech giants will increase operational efficiency for both.

  • This partnership may further drive both companies towards profitability.

This partnership may further drive both companies towards profitability.

title: Tata Technologies’ use of AI/ML reduced power consumption by 18% on shopfloor
content:
Tata Technologies’ use of AI/ML reduced power consumption by 18% on shopfloor

Yogesh Deo, Senior VP and Delivery Head, ER&D (Engineering Research and Development), Tata Technologies’s words

“The company is putting a lot of effort and investment into building a Centre of Excellence for AI technology.”

Justification

  • The company is using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI & ML)  to make its manufacturing processes more robust for its clients, along with reducing curtail time & pollution.

  • A/c to Deo, with the use of AI/ML, the power consumption was reduced by  18%, followed by a 10% decline in fuel consumption & 8% decrease in chemical consumption.

  • The model effectively detects anomalies (diversion from standard behavior) & lets the company take corrective actions before the actual failure.

  • To predict tooling failure in the case of dye design, it co-relates the historical  & present operational data to predict failures.
content_snippet:
Tata Technologies’ use of AI/ML reduced power consumption by 18% on shopfloor


Yogesh Deo, Senior VP and Delivery Head, ER&D (Engineering Research and Development), Tata Technologies’s words

“The company is putting a lot of effort and investment into building a Centre of Excellence for AI technology.”


Justification

The company is using Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning (AI & ML)  to make its manufacturing processes more robust for its clients, along with reducing curtail time & pollution.

A/c to Deo, with the use of AI/ML, the power consumption was reduced by  18%, followed by a 10% decline in fuel consumption & 8% decrease in chemical consumption.

The model effectively detects anomalies (diversion from standard behavior) & lets the company take corrective actions before the actual failure.

To predict tooling failure in the case of dye design, it co-relates the historical  & present operational data to predict failures.
takeaways:
• With the use of AI & ML, the company is obtaining operational efficiency & leading to faster operations.

  • Such tools save time & effort for the company, stressing over increased process timings  & improving overall performance.

title: Hero FinCorp's personal loan game-changer: Upto Rs. 5 Lakhs for low-income earners
content:
Hero FinCorp introduces the Jumbo Personal Loan, targeting individuals with incomes starting from Rs. 15,000.

Justification 

  • The loan offers up to Rs 5 lakhs with no end-use restrictions, allowing borrowers to pursue various aspirations.

  • Eligibility criteria include a minimum monthly income of Rs. 15,000, an age range of 21-58 years, a credit score 600+, and valid KYC documents.

  • It empowers borrowers to pursue higher education, skill enhancement, home improvements, or vacations.

  • Self-employed individuals can also avail themselves of this incredible opportunity by fulfilling the minimum criteria.

  • To apply for an online personal loan, visit the Hero FinCorp website, complete the form, and upload the required documents.

  • It offers instant personal loans up to 3 Lakh through its user-friendly app.

  • It is a renowned name in the financial sector, dedicated to providing innovative solutions for individuals and businesses with a nationwide presence, offering competitive interest rates, and having easy-to-meet personal loan eligibility criteria.

title: Capital Small Finance Bank to hit IPO street, files draft papers with Sebi
content:
Capital Small Finance Bank Ltd, backed by private equity and venture capital, files draft papers for an IPO with SEBI.

Justification

  • The IPO includes a fresh issue of Rs 450 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of up to 24.12 lakh shares by existing shareholders and promoters.

  • The OFS comprises shares from & up to: 

    • Oman India Joint Investment Fund II -  8.37 lakh shares 

    • PI Ventures LLP - 3.37 lakh shares

    • Amicus Capital Private Equity I LLP - 6.05 lakh shares 

    • Amicus Capital Partners India Fund-I - 70,178 shares 

    • Other shareholders- 5.64 lakh shares 

  • Lead managers for the issue are Nuvama Wealth Management, DAM Capital Advisors, and Equirus Capital.

  • Funds raised will boost Tier-I capital to support future asset growth, particularly loans and investments, and ensure compliance with RBI capital adequacy regulations.

  • The bank is backed by private investors like Amicus Capital, Oman India Joint Investment Fund (OIJIF), and Pi Ventures. It recently secured an equity investment from Max Life Insurance Company Ltd, valuing it over Rs 1,600 crore for a minority equity stake.

  • The bank, with its 172 branches, recorded business growth of 15.6 % to Rs 12,583.68 crore & registered PAT Rs 30.10 crore as compared to Rs 18.28 crore for June 2023 quarter.

Justification


The IPO includes a fresh issue of Rs 450 crore and an offer-for-sale (OFS) of up to 24.12 lakh shares by existing shareholders and promoters.

The OFS comprises shares from & up to: 

Oman India Joint Investment Fund II -  8.37 lakh shares 

PI Ventures LLP - 3.37 lakh shares

Amicus Capital Private Equity I LLP - 6.05 lakh shares 

Amicus Capital Partners India Fund-I - 70,178 shares 

Other shareholders- 5.64 lakh shares 

Lead managers for the issue are Nuvama Wealth Management, DAM Capital Advisors, and Equirus Capital.

Funds raised will boost Tier-I capital to support future asset growth, particularly loans and investments, and ensure compliance with RBI capital adequacy regulations.

The bank is backed by private investors like Amicus Capital, Oman India Joint Investment Fund (OIJIF), and Pi Ventures. It recently secured an equity investment from Max Life Insurance Company Ltd, valuing it over Rs 1,600 crore for a minority equity stake.

The bank, with its 172 branches, recorded business growth of 15.6 % to Rs 12,583.68 crore & registered PAT Rs 30.10 crore as compared to Rs 18.28 crore for June 2023 quarter.

title: Sebi renews licences of NSE Clearing, Indian Clearing Corp for three years
content:
SEBI renews licenses for NSE Clearing Ltd and Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd for three years.

Justification

  • The renewed licenses are valid until October 2, 2026.

  • NSE Clearing Ltd is a subsidiary of the National Stock Exchange  (NSE), while Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd ( ICCL)  is an arm of BSE.

  • SEBI considers the renewals in the interest of the trade, securities market, and the public.

  • Both entities handle clearing, settlement, deposit, collateral management, and risk management for NSE and BSE trades.
content_snippet:
SEBI renews licenses for NSE Clearing Ltd and Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd for three years.


Justification

The renewed licenses are valid until October 2, 2026.

NSE Clearing Ltd is a subsidiary of the National Stock Exchange  (NSE), while Indian Clearing Corporation Ltd ( ICCL)  is an arm of BSE.

SEBI considers the renewals in the interest of the trade, securities market, and the public.

Both entities handle clearing, settlement, deposit, collateral management, and risk management for NSE and BSE trades.
takeaways:
• The renewed licenses will help smoother trade operations for NSE Clearing Ltd and ICCL.

  • NSE recently proposed to extend the trading hours to give people the chance to react quickly to global events & compete with other key market players
takeaways_snippet:
The renewed licenses will help smoother trade operations for NSE Clearing Ltd and ICCL.

NSE recently proposed to extend the trading hours to give people the chance to react quickly to global events & compete with other key market players

content:

  • Adjusted gross profit margin surged by 43%, with Adjusted Gross Profit at Rs 2,347 crore,23% growth.

title: ADIA may infuse $600 million in Reliance Retail: Report
content:
ADIA plans to infuse $600 Million into Reliance Retail for an additional stake.

Justification

  • The Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) is contemplating infusing $600 Million in  Reliance Retail, over & above its 1.2% stake.

  • Earlier this week, RIL's retail division received ₹2,069.50 crore from global investment firm KKR. In return, RIL allocated 1.71 crore equity shares, raising KKR's stake in the company from 1.42% to 1.17%.

  • Earlier this month, RIL received ₹8,278 crore from Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) in exchange for an approximately 1% stake in Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRVL), valued at ₹8.278 lakh crore (USD 100 billion).

  • RRVL had raised funds from Silver Lake, KKR, Mubadala, Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, GIC, TPG, General Atlantic, and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund at a valuation of around $57 billion in 2020.
content_snippet:
ADIA plans to infuse $600 Million into Reliance Retail for an additional stake.

Justification


The Abu Dhabi Investment Authority (ADIA) is contemplating infusing $600 Million in  Reliance Retail, over & above its 1.2% stake.

Earlier this week, RIL's retail division received ₹2,069.50 crore from global investment firm KKR. In return, RIL allocated 1.71 crore equity shares, raising KKR's stake in the company from 1.42% to 1.17%.

Earlier this month, RIL received ₹8,278 crore from Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) in exchange for an approximately 1% stake in Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRVL), valued at ₹8.278 lakh crore (USD 100 billion).

RRVL had raised funds from Silver Lake, KKR, Mubadala, Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, GIC, TPG, General Atlantic, and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund at a valuation of around $57 billion in 2020.
takeaways: • The company is poised for substantial profitability with ample funding and expansion plans. Alongside its numerous subsidiaries operating 18,000+ stores in India, it aims to diversify into fashion, retail, and grocery sectors through enhanced partnerships and small business acquisitions.

Alongside its numerous subsidiaries operating 18,000+ stores in India, it aims to diversify into fashion, retail, and grocery sectors through enhanced partnerships and small business acquisitions.

title: ICL Fincorp expands in Mumbai with five new branches and a regional office: Initiating the first step of larger expansion
content:
ICL FinCorp, a leading gold-oriented NBFC, initiates the first step of larger expansion.

Justification

  • With 32 years of serving as a preferred financial partner, the company is expanding in Mumbai. They have opened 5 new branches and a regional office, with plans to establish 25 more branches in Mumbai during the financial year.

  • Adding to the existing 350 branches, the new branches will be inaugurated by Chairman & MD K. G. Anilkumar. In contrast, Whole-time Director Uma Anilkumar will inaugurate the new business launch on 22nd September.

  • The new branches and a regional office will be located in Matunga, Mulund, Vasai, Borivali, and Ulhasnagar.

  • The company acquired Salem Erode Investments, a BSE-listed NBFC with over 92 years of service in Tamil Nadu, & launched 'ICL Support' for underprivileged upliftment.

  •  Additionally, they've expanded to the Middle East with ICL Investments LLC and Gold Trading. & have also diversified into travel & tourism, fashion, health diagnostics, and charitable trusts.
content_snippet:
ICL FinCorp, a leading gold-oriented NBFC, initiates the first step of larger expansion.

Justification

With 32 years of serving as a preferred financial partner, the company is expanding in Mumbai. They have opened 5 new branches and a regional office, with plans to establish 25 more branches in Mumbai during the financial year.

Adding to the existing 350 branches, the new branches will be inaugurated by Chairman & MD K. G. Anilkumar. In contrast, Whole-time Director Uma Anilkumar will inaugurate the new business launch on 22nd September.

The new branches and a regional office will be located in Matunga, Mulund, Vasai, Borivali, and Ulhasnagar.

The company acquired Salem Erode Investments, a BSE-listed NBFC with over 92 years of service in Tamil Nadu, & launched 'ICL Support' for underprivileged upliftment.

 Additionally, they've expanded to the Middle East with ICL Investments LLC and Gold Trading. & have also diversified into travel & tourism, fashion, health diagnostics, and charitable trusts.
takeaways: • The opening of the new branches will lead to increased operations of the company & might the company towards profitability in future.
takeaways_snippet: The opening of the new branches will lead to increased operations of the company & might the company towards profitability in future.

title: Fincare Small Finance Bank IPO gets SEBI approval
content:
Being a digital-first Small Finance Bank, the bank has received SEBI approval for an Initial Public Offering (IPO).

Justification

  • The IPO, filed in May, involves issuing equity shares with a face value of Rs 10 each. It includes a fresh issuance of up to Rs 625 crore and an offer for sale of up to 17,000,000 equity shares by promoters and investors selling shares.

  • The aim is to create a Tier-1 capital base for the bank's future needs with the net proceeds.

  • The book-running lead managers for the IPO issue are ICICI Securities Limited, Axis Capital Limited, IIFL Securities Limited, SBI Capital Markets Limited, and Ambit Private Limited.

  • In the equity share offering, Fincare Business Services Limited may offer up to 1.49 crore equity shares, Wagner up to 4.7 lakh equity shares, True North Fund V LLP up to 4.4 lakh equity shares, Indium IV (Mauritius) Holdings Limited up to 4.3 lakh equity shares, and Omega TC Holdings PTE up to 2.3 lakh equity shares.
content_snippet:
Being a digital-first Small Finance Bank, the bank has received SEBI approval for an Initial Public Offering (IPO).

Justification

The IPO, filed in May, involves issuing equity shares with a face value of Rs 10 each. It includes a fresh issuance of up to Rs 625 crore and an offer for sale of up to 17,000,000 equity shares by promoters and investors selling shares.

The aim is to create a Tier-1 capital base for the bank's future needs with the net proceeds.

The book-running lead managers for the IPO issue are ICICI Securities Limited, Axis Capital Limited, IIFL Securities Limited, SBI Capital Markets Limited, and Ambit Private Limited.

In the equity share offering, Fincare Business Services Limited may offer up to 1.49 crore equity shares, Wagner up to 4.7 lakh equity shares, True North Fund V LLP up to 4.4 lakh equity shares, Indium IV (Mauritius) Holdings Limited up to 4.3 lakh equity shares, and Omega TC Holdings PTE up to 2.3 lakh equity shares.
takeaways: The IPO serves as an opportunity for the people to take a part in the company’s profitability, a company that practices Financial Inclusion.

title: CIAL to unveil seven  mega projects for infra development
content:
Cochin International Airport Ltd (CIAL) is gearing up to unveil seven major projects worth 1,000 crore aimed at infrastructure enhancement, security enhancement, and improving passenger mobility on October 2.

Justification

  • The fourth-busiest Airport in the country in terms of International Traffic, CIAL is focused on the launCIAL to unveil seven  mega projects for infra developmentch of the following projects-

    • A new import cargo terminal, implementation of the DigiYatra software developed by CIAL, modernization of airport emergency service, phase-1 expansion of international terminal (T-3), the golf tourism project, Aero Lounges & the Perimeter Intrusion Detection System (PIDS).

  • Pinarayi Vijayan, CM of Kerala, will inaugurate the projects at a function on the cargo terminal premises.

  • The launch envisions endorsing CIAL’s new vision of infrastructural development, ‘Flying into Tomorrow.’

  • In FY23, it achieved its highest turnover at Rs 770.91 Cr and a profit of Rs 265.08 Cr. Through a Rights Issue, it raised Rs 478.22 Cr, with the Kerala Government holding 33.38%. A 35% dividend for 2022–23, totaling Rs 167.38 Cr, was also approved for shareholders.

title: HDFC Sec takes on Zerodha, Groww with new platform 'Sky', bets big on margin trading facility
content:
HDFC Sec takes on Zerodha, Groww with new platform 'Sky', bets big on margin trading facility.

Justification

  • HDFC Securities, launching  HDFC SKY, a  discount broking all-in-one app for investing and trading at the one-price slab of Rs 20 on 25th Sep 2023, targets new age investors, Gen-Z, and millennials.

  • The app will access Indian stocks, ETFs, Mutual Funds, F&O, and IPOs.

  • It is working on getting Government of India bonds debt securities on the platform shortly.

  • App’s Unique selling proposition includes its in-house research team, providing stock recommendations followed by a margin trading facility (MTF) with an interest of 12% against a market rate of 18-21%.

  • The investors can monitor their MTF holdings as a separate ledger.

  • The company holds a 3% market share in active NSE Clients against 19.4% and 19% by Zerodha & Groww, respectively.
content_snippet:
HDFC Sec takes on Zerodha, Groww with new platform 'Sky', bets big on margin trading facility.


Justification


HDFC Securities, launching  HDFC SKY, a  discount broking all-in-one app for investing and trading at the one-price slab of Rs 20 on 25th Sep 2023, targets new age investors, Gen-Z, and millennials.

The app will access Indian stocks, ETFs, Mutual Funds, F&O, and IPOs.

It is working on getting Government of India bonds debt securities on the platform shortly.

App’s Unique selling proposition includes its in-house research team, providing stock recommendations followed by a margin trading facility (MTF) with an interest of 12% against a market rate of 18-21%.

The investors can monitor their MTF holdings as a separate ledger.

The company holds a 3% market share in active NSE Clients against 19.4% and 19% by Zerodha & Groww, respectively.
takeaways:
• HDFC Securities, with this launch, might practice cost-effective trading,& aiming for diverse investment opportunities.

  • This move might attract investment into the company securities due to convenience & user-friendliness.

title: Tata Capital launches instant digital sanctions for commercial vehicle loans
content:
Tata Capital launches instant digital sanctions for commercial vehicle loans.

Abonty Banerjee, COO of Digital & Marketing at Tata Capital’s words

 “This is in line with our strategy of constantly providing innovative digital products and making the customer journey frictionless and simple.”

Justification

  • Tata Capital Ltd (TCL), the financial branch of Tata Group, introduced instant digital onboarding for commercial vehicle loans, streamlining the loan approval process.

  • The company has joined forces with major Original Equipment Manufacturers OEMs to offer customers a broad selection of new commercial vehicles.

  • The relationship managers of TCL will assist customers. 

  • It will use Vahan APIs for direct customer data retrieval. & will streamline the underwriting process with a scorecard.

  • Minimal documentation is required  & customers will receive loan sanction letters within minutes.

  • The new digital sanction is an industry-first initiative, adding to the existing bouquet of offerings specially tailored for SME customers.

title: NSE on its way to extend trading hours for F&O, awaiting Sebi nod
content:
NSE to introduce extended trading hours for equity derivatives such as futures and options (F&O)

  • The global giant National Stock Exchange (NSE) is mulling over extending evening trading hours early this year from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM, with the possibility of further extension until 11:30 PM for futures and options trading.

  • The proposal has already been submitted to SEBI & SEBI  has already laid out a set of rules that will facilitate the bourses' decision to keep futures and options (F&O) trading open until 11:55 PM and shares until 5:00 PM.

  • The move is being made, giving Indian traders to react quickly to global events & growing concerns over big traders shifting to 24*7 trading in GIFT City.

  • The extended hours are likely to increase the trading volume of NSE, with NSE making the move through F&O, including  Nifty and Bank Nifty & later with stock derivatives.
content_snippet:
NSE to introduce extended trading hours for equity derivatives such as futures and options (F&O)

The global giant National Stock Exchange (NSE) is mulling over extending evening trading hours early this year from 6:00 PM to 9:00 PM, with the possibility of further extension until 11:30 PM for futures and options trading.

The proposal has already been submitted to SEBI & SEBI  has already laid out a set of rules that will facilitate the bourses' decision to keep futures and options (F&O) trading open until 11:55 PM and shares until 5:00 PM.

The move is being made, giving Indian traders to react quickly to global events & growing concerns over big traders shifting to 24*7 trading in GIFT City.

The extended hours are likely to increase the trading volume of NSE, with NSE making the move through F&O, including  Nifty and Bank Nifty & later with stock derivatives.
takeaways:
• Increased trading volume will lead to increased revenue for NSE, paving its way towards higher profitability.

  • This move will also aid the company to sustain its competitive position.
takeaways_snippet:
Increased trading volume will lead to increased revenue for NSE, paving its way towards higher profitability.

This move will also aid the company to sustain its competitive position.

title: Reliance Retail receives full subscription amount of Rs 2,069 cr from KKR
content:
Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRVL) receives a full subscription amount of Rs 2,069.50 Cr from the global investment firm KKR.


  • On September 24, 2023, RRVL allotted 17,158,752 equity shares to Alyssum Asia Holdings II Pte. Ltd. (KKR) following full payment. This investment from KKR,  previously announced by Reliance Industries for its subsidiary earlier this month, valued RRVL at ₹8.36 trillion.

  • This allotment boosted KKR's stake in RRVL from 1.17% to 1.42%. KKR has assets under management worth $519 billion as of June 30, 2023.

  • In 2020, RRVL secured ₹47,265 crores from global private equity funds for a 10.09% stake, valuing RRVL at ₹4.2 trillion with Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) investing ₹8,278 crore earlier this month for about 1% stake in RRVL, valuing RRVL  at ₹8.278 trillion.
content_snippet:
Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRVL) receives a full subscription amount of Rs 2,069.50 Cr from the global investment firm KKR.


On September 24, 2023, RRVL allotted 17,158,752 equity shares to Alyssum Asia Holdings II Pte. Ltd. (KKR) following full payment. This investment from KKR,  previously announced by Reliance Industries for its subsidiary earlier this month, valued RRVL at ₹8.36 trillion.

This allotment boosted KKR's stake in RRVL from 1.17% to 1.42%. KKR has assets under management worth $519 billion as of June 30, 2023.

In 2020, RRVL secured ₹47,265 crores from global private equity funds for a 10.09% stake, valuing RRVL at ₹4.2 trillion with Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) investing ₹8,278 crore earlier this month for about 1% stake in RRVL, valuing RRVL  at ₹8.278 trillion.
takeaways:
• RRVL, already expanding its reach, will prosper more after receiving capital assistance from a global giant like KKR.

  • The capital might be used to expand RRVL's existing operations, which already with its parent Reliance Industries, has  18k stores nationwide.
takeaways_snippet:
RRVL, already expanding its reach, will prosper more after receiving capital assistance from a global giant like KKR.

The capital might be used to expand RRVL's existing operations, which already with its parent Reliance Industries, has  18k stores nationwide.

title: Philips India FY23 Revenue Jumps Nearly 5% To Rs 5,734 Crore
content:
Philips India FY23 Revenue Jumps Nearly 5% To Rs 5,734 Crore.

  • Philips India, a Dutch MNC  Koninklijke Philips NV subsidiary, reported a 4.6% increase in revenue from operations at Rs 5,734 crore, according to financial data accessed by the business intelligence platform Tofler.

  • The company operates in 3 segments: Personal Health, Health Systems, and Innovation Services. The company’s financial highlights for FY 2022-23-

    • Other Income increased by 10.95% to Rs 71.9 crore.

    • Total income up by 4.68% to Rs 5,805.9 crore.

    •  PAT declined by 2.2% to Rs 260 crore.

    • Revenue from Innovation Services surged by 27.35%.

    • The Health Systems segment’s revenue declined by 4.42%, and

    • The personal health revenue declined by 13.78%. 

  • The company has a diversified product portfolio.

  • The company has manufacturing facilities in Pune and Maharashtra and maintains a Software Development Centre in Bengaluru.
content_snippet:
Philips India FY23 Revenue Jumps Nearly 5% To Rs 5,734 Crore.


Philips India, a Dutch MNC  Koninklijke Philips NV subsidiary, reported a 4.6% increase in revenue from operations at Rs 5,734 crore, according to financial data accessed by the business intelligence platform Tofler.

The company operates in 3 segments: Personal Health, Health Systems, and Innovation Services. The company’s financial highlights for FY 2022-23-

Other Income increased by 10.95% to Rs 71.9 crore.

Total income up by 4.68% to Rs 5,805.9 crore.

 PAT declined by 2.2% to Rs 260 crore.

Revenue from Innovation Services surged by 27.35%.

The Health Systems segment’s revenue declined by 4.42%, and

The personal health revenue declined by 13.78%. 

The company has a diversified product portfolio.

The company has manufacturing facilities in Pune and Maharashtra and maintains a Software Development Centre in Bengaluru.
takeaways:

  • It is an opportunity for people to take part in the company’s profit by investing in it.

title: Sterlite Power Expands Portfolio with Acquisition of Beawar Transmission Limited Project from REC
content:
Sterlite Power expands its Green Energy corridor portfolio by acquiring Beawar Transmission Ltd from REC.

Regarding the development, Pratik Agarwal, MD, Sterlite Power, said, “Sterlite Power is proud to be building the green energy corridor that will enable the flow of 20 GW of renewable energy”.

Justification

  • The country's first private transmission infrastructure developer to implement a STATCOM Sterlite power has announced the acquisition of  Beawar Transmission Limited, a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) from REC Power Development and Consultancy Limited –a wholly owned subsidiary of Rural Electricity Corporation. 

  • It secured the project through the Tariff Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) process in August 2023 &  will execute it via the acquired SPV on a 35-year BOOT (build, own, operate, transfer) basis, marking Sterlite's nineteenth power transmission project in India through the TBCB process.

  • The company now manages approximately INR 12,000 crore worth of projects.

  • The acquired SPV will entail the construction of -

    • A 350 km 765kV transmission corridor linking Fatehgarh III's renewable energy zone to the Beawar substation.

    • Building a 3000 MVA 765/400kV Substation at Beawar.

    • Constructing two LILO lines spanning around 120 km.
content_snippet:

Justification

It secured the project through the Tariff Based Competitive Bidding (TBCB) process in August 2023 &  will execute it via the acquired SPV on a 35-year BOOT (build, own, operate, transfer) basis, marking Sterlite's nineteenth power transmission project in India through the TBCB process.

The company now manages approximately INR 12,000 crore worth of projects.

title: Indian Potash hopes to extend Canadian deal despite Nijjar spat
content:
Indian Potash optimistic over potash supplies as a result of signed MoU of Canadian deal despite Nijjar spat 

Justification

  • Indian Potash’s MD P.S. Gahlaut said he hopes to extend a contract with Canadian supplier Canpotex beyond the end of September & Canadians have already signalled their willingness to extend the contract regardless of political upheavals,

  • Indian firms, leading Canadian fertiliser importers, signed a 3-year MoU with Canpotex starting in 2023 to buy 1.5 million metric tons of potash, aiming to boost agriculture.

  • India and Canada's diplomatic ties soured significantly due to the expulsion of each other's diplomats in connection with the murder of a Sikh separatist leader in British Columbia, Canada, in June, which has sparked concerns about the stability of Canadian potash supplies.

  • Indian companies depend on imports for their 4 million metric tons of muriate of potash (MOP) and brought in around 550,000 tonnes between April and September 2023.

Indian companies depend on imports for their 4 million metric tons of muriate of potash (MOP) and brought in around 550,000 tonnes between April and September 2023.
takeaways:
• The uninterrupted India-Canada potash deal ensures companies like Indian Potash receive essential supplies, maintaining operational efficiency.

  • Operational efficiency might lead to increased revenue & profitability due to a lack of supply chain disruptions.
takeaways_snippet:
The uninterrupted India-Canada potash deal ensures companies like Indian Potash receive essential supplies, maintaining operational efficiency.

Operational efficiency might lead to increased revenue & profitability due to a lack of supply chain disruptions.

title: Reliance Retail opens 2 new Yousta stores
content:
Reliance Retail opens up two new Yousta Stores this month, after opening in August in Hyderabad.

Justification

  • Reliance Retail Ltd, a Reliance group company, has recently opened two new stores, one in Bilaspur on 16 September and another in Eddapal, Kerala, on 19 September this year.

  • According to an IndiaRetailing report, Reliance plans to roll out 200-250 Yousta stores to compete with Tata Group’s Zudio chain.

  • The Yousta known for its ‘starring section' has a price range of Rs 499 to Rs 999; its products are available through Ajio & JioMart online.

  • YOUSTA was tagged as ‘Your absolute star Store’ by its company’s officials on a LinkedIn post while sharing a video of the store in Bilaspur & Eddapal.

  • Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd, a holding company of Reliance’s retail companies, has over 18.5k stores & has partnered with over 3 million merchants.
content_snippet:
Reliance Retail opens up two new Yousta Stores this month, after opening in August in Hyderabad.

Justification

Reliance Retail Ltd, a Reliance group company, has recently opened two new stores, one in Bilaspur on 16 September and another in Eddapal, Kerala, on 19 September this year.

According to an IndiaRetailing report, Reliance plans to roll out 200-250 Yousta stores to compete with Tata Group’s Zudio chain.

The Yousta known for its ‘starring section' has a price range of Rs 499 to Rs 999; its products are available through Ajio & JioMart online.

YOUSTA was tagged as ‘Your absolute star Store’ by its company’s officials on a LinkedIn post while sharing a video of the store in Bilaspur & Eddapal.

Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd, a holding company of Reliance’s retail companies, has over 18.5k stores & has partnered with over 3 million merchants.
takeaways:
• The already profitable chain with consolidated turnover of $31.7 billion in 2023, by adding more chain of stores, shows strong growth prospects.

  • The opening of new stores will lead to increased revenue, profitability & operations for Reliance Retail & its subsidiaries.
takeaways_snippet:
The already profitable chain with consolidated turnover of $31.7 billion in 2023, by adding more chain of stores, shows strong growth prospects.

The opening of new stores will lead to increased revenue, profitability & operations for Reliance Retail & its subsidiaries.

title: Tata Capital plans IPO, expanding board and merging subsidiaries to go public
content:
Tata Capital plans for an IPO in 2025; the IPO process is to kickstart in March 2024, with the appointment of investment bankers, targeting adding members to its board.

Justification

  • Tata Capital, upon being classified as an ‘upper layer’ Non-banking Financial Company (NBFC), according to which the unlisted companies must complete their listing within three years of receiving this notice.

  • RBI tagged the company as upper layer NBFC in September 2022, the implication of which company needs to be listed by September 2025. (3 year duration)

  • The company is in the final stages of merging some crucial subsidiaries, awaiting RBI approval by 2023, like Tata Capital Financial Services Ltd, Tata Capital Housing Finance Ltd, Tata Cleantech Capital Ltd, Tata Securities Ltd, and Tata Capital Pte. Ltd, among others.

  • This IPO might reduce promoter ownership by 10-20%, making it one of the biggest IPOs ever.

  • Tata Capital Financial Services, another Tata company identified with the same tag by RBI, is merging with Tata Sons.

  • Tata Sons is the Holding company for the entire Tata Group, with Tata Trusts holding a 66% stake and Shapoorji Pallonji Group holding an 18.4% stake in Tata Sons.

  • Tata Capital is valued at Rs 1.5 Trillion, with its shares trading at Rs 425 in the grey market.
content_snippet:
Tata Capital plans for an IPO in 2025; the IPO process is to kickstart in March 2024, with the appointment of investment bankers, targeting adding members to its board.

Justification

Tata Capital, upon being classified as an ‘upper layer’ Non-banking Financial Company (NBFC), according to which the unlisted companies must complete their listing within three years of receiving this notice.

RBI tagged the company as upper layer NBFC in September 2022, the implication of which company needs to be listed by September 2025. (3 year duration)

The company is in the final stages of merging some crucial subsidiaries, awaiting RBI approval by 2023, like Tata Capital Financial Services Ltd, Tata Capital Housing Finance Ltd, Tata Cleantech Capital Ltd, Tata Securities Ltd, and Tata Capital Pte. Ltd, among others.

This IPO might reduce promoter ownership by 10-20%, making it one of the biggest IPOs ever.

Tata Capital Financial Services, another Tata company identified with the same tag by RBI, is merging with Tata Sons.

Tata Sons is the Holding company for the entire Tata Group, with Tata Trusts holding a 66% stake and Shapoorji Pallonji Group holding an 18.4% stake in Tata Sons.

Tata Capital is valued at Rs 1.5 Trillion, with its shares trading at Rs 425 in the grey market.
takeaways:
• The IPO by Tata Group company serves as a gateway to have a part in the company’s earnings by being its shareholder.

  • The merging of various companies under Tata Capital will lead to a combined increased pool of resources & capital for future operations.
takeaways_snippet:
The IPO by Tata Group company serves as a gateway to have a part in the company’s earnings by being its shareholder.

The merging of various companies under Tata Capital will lead to a combined increased pool of resources & capital for future operations.

title: Nifty, NSE scale new heights
content:
NSE outperformed 98% of the 2,137 companies listed on the exchange as of March 31, 2023, with only 37 companies reporting higher profits.

Justification

  • In Q1 FY24, NSE recorded a total revenue of Rs 3,357 crore, a 22% YoY increase, and a PAT of Rs 1,844 crores. For FY 2022-23, it achieved profits of Rs 6,400 crores and EPS of Rs 146.12, with its net worth growing to Rs16,676 crores in FY23.

  • Bharti Airtel and Maruti Suzuki India were the only companies to surpass NSE in profitability during FY 22-23.

  • Despite being heavyweight stocks on the exchange's indices, companies like Bajaj Auto, Adani Ports, and Asian Paints earned lower profits than NSE.

  • NSE holds a dominant market share of 98% in derivatives trading; additionally, the Nifty index recently reached record levels, closing at 20,192.35 points.
content_snippet:
NSE outperformed 98% of the 2,137 companies listed on the exchange as of March 31, 2023, with only 37 companies reporting higher profits.

Justification


In Q1 FY24, NSE recorded a total revenue of Rs 3,357 crore, a 22% YoY increase, and a PAT of Rs 1,844 crores. For FY 2022-23, it achieved profits of Rs 6,400 crores and EPS of Rs 146.12, with its net worth growing to Rs16,676 crores in FY23.

Bharti Airtel and Maruti Suzuki India were the only companies to surpass NSE in profitability during FY 22-23.

Despite being heavyweight stocks on the exchange's indices, companies like Bajaj Auto, Adani Ports, and Asian Paints earned lower profits than NSE.

NSE holds a dominant market share of 98% in derivatives trading; additionally, the Nifty index recently reached record levels, closing at 20,192.35 points.
takeaways:
• With the company's robust growth & strong financials, it poses an opportunity for people to take a part of NSE’s profit by becoming a shareholder.

  • NSE recently proposed to SEBI to increase trading hours, which might increase trading volume for NSE.
takeaways_snippet:
With the company's robust growth & strong financials, it poses an opportunity for people to take a part of NSE’s profit by becoming a shareholder.

NSE recently proposed to SEBI to increase trading hours, which might increase trading volume for NSE.

title: Tata Capital Unveils Next-Gen API Financial Solutions
content:
Tata Capital Ltd launched a user-friendly API for its corporate & retail clients for customized solutions.

Abonty Banerjee, COO, Digital and Marketing at Tata Capital’s words

We already work closely with the fintech ecosystem across the lending value chain. The launch of our API solutions is one more step towards making such partnerships more collaborative and seamless. We believe it will help in bringing to life many more new ideas along with fintech partners and aid in the continuous innovation process.


Justification

  • In the era of digitalization, Tata Capital Ltd has launched next-gen user-friendly API (Application Programming Interface) ecosystem to cater to dynamic & tailor made needs of corporate & retail clients for increased efficiency & productivity also aiding in digital collaborations.

  • Its API solutions simplify digital collaborations, enhancing the digital experience for retail and corporate customers in various loan categories, including payments and collections.

title: Philips India launches Move Fearlessly campaign for OneBlade
content:
Philips India launches ‘Move Fearlessly’ campaign for One Blade.

Deepali Agarwal, Business Head of personal health at Philips India Subcontinent’s words

"With the New Philips One Blade, we aspire to be their companion, enabling them to face each day unafraid. Our 'No Nick. No Cuts' promise epitomizes our commitment to well-being, ensuring that young individuals always present their best selves to the world.”

Justification

  • Philips India to resonate with India’s Gen Z launched a campaign named ‘Move Fearlessly’  for One Blade.

  • The campaign, driven by the motive of empowering young males & instill confidence, features a young man's journey on a moving train, as he conquers uncertainty and gains remarkable confidence."

  • The film encapsulates the transformative power of encouraging individuals to face challenges with confidence.

  • One blade incorporates protective plate, glide coating, and rounded tips, with a promise of ‘No Nick. No Cuts.’

  • The brand strives to inspire and empower one to tackle life's challenges, both in grooming and beyond.

title: Potential merger between AU Small Finance and Fincare SFB: What’s at stake for investors?
content:
AU Small Finance bank is in talks to acquire Fincare Small Finance Bank, making it the first deal in the small finance bank space, symbolizing consolidation in the industry. 


  • AU Small Finance bank with a loan book value of ₹58,421 crore (as of March 31, 2023) is planning to acquire Fincare Small Finance Bank attributed to higher profitability & yields resulting due to unsecured loans & flexible pricing.

  • Another valid reason to acquire is the target’s strong presence in southern markets, which will aid the acquirer to diversify its geographical risk & tap into better asset quality.

  • The consolidation might lead to stock dilution for AU’s shareholders, but the long term positive effects will outweigh short-term investor concerns.
content_snippet:
AU Small Finance bank is in talks to acquire Fincare Small Finance Bank, making it the first deal in the small finance bank space, symbolizing consolidation in the industry. 


AU Small Finance bank with a loan book value of ₹58,421 crore (as of March 31, 2023) is planning to acquire Fincare Small Finance Bank attributed to higher profitability & yields resulting due to unsecured loans & flexible pricing.

Another valid reason to acquire is the target’s strong presence in southern markets, which will aid the acquirer to diversify its geographical risk & tap into better asset quality.

The consolidation might lead to stock dilution for AU’s shareholders, but the long term positive effects will outweigh short-term investor concerns.
takeaways:
• The acquisition will lead to increased operations due to increased resources, customer base, geographical presence for AU Small Finance Bank.

  • The rise in operations will lead to increase in revenue & profitability, with the anticipation of higher returns to shareholders.
takeaways_snippet:
The acquisition will lead to increased operations due to increased resources, customer base, geographical presence for AU Small Finance Bank.

The rise in operations will lead to increase in revenue & profitability, with the anticipation of higher returns to shareholders.

title: Google, Capgemini looking for 50,000 sq ft office space each in GIFT City
content:
Technology giants Google & Capgemini in process of establishing domestic office space in Gujarat International Finance Tec-City (GIFT City), negotiations are in line, soon an announcement is expected.

Justification

  • Capgemini, a French MNC, recently disclosed its intention to establish a new office space with 500 seats capacity in the GIFT City.

  • The company, with an impressive workforce count of 180k employees across 13 cities, plans to create this office space for software product development by occupying approx 50,000 sq ft of space.

  • On the other hand, Google CEO Sundar Pichai announced a new global fintech center in GIFT City, where specialized teams will support GPay and other products.

  • Both these moves will be of significant benefit to the overall GIFT City.

title: Sterlite Power net profit rises 30 pc to Rs 322 cr in 2022-23
content:
Sterlite Power’s standalone net profit up by 30% for FY 2022-23, backed by higher revenues.


Pratik Agarwal, MD of Sterlite words

"The results demonstrate our continuous focus on garnering new opportunities and operational efficiencies and underscore our commitment to be a key player in building the nation's energy landscape".

Justification

  • Sterlite Power, a leading power player’s standalone net profit stands at Rs 322.01 crore in FY 2022-23, an increase of 30% from FY 2021-22’s standalone net profit of Rs 246.57 Cr.

  • The Company’s revenue from operation also rose by 3% from Rs 3,797.38 in FY 2021-22 to Rs. 3,923.51 Cr in FY 2022-23.

  • Recently Rs 75,408 Cr worth of power transmission projects have been approved by the 14th National Committee on Transmission (NCT) and are likely to be awarded this year through the bidding route, the company plans to win it.
content_snippet:

Pratik Agarwal, MD of Sterlite words

"The results demonstrate our continuous focus on garnering new opportunities and operational efficiencies and underscore our commitment to be a key player in building the nation's energy landscape".

Justification

Sterlite Power, a leading power player’s standalone net profit stands at Rs 322.01 crore in FY 2022-23, an increase of 30% from FY 2021-22’s standalone net profit of Rs 246.57 Cr.

The Company’s revenue from operation also rose by 3% from Rs 3,797.38 in FY 2021-22 to Rs. 3,923.51 Cr in FY 2022-23.

Recently Rs 75,408 Cr worth of power transmission projects have been approved by the 14th National Committee on Transmission (NCT) and are likely to be awarded this year through the bidding route, the company plans to win it.

  • The company, with a strong focus on making ample use of new opportunities, will pave its way for profitability in the future.

title: KKR buys additional stake in Reliance Retail Ventures for Rs 2,069.50 crore
content:
Private Equity giant KKR buys an additional stake in Reliance Retail Ventures worth Rs 2,069.50 crore. 


Joe Bae, co-CEO of KKR words

“We are pleased to extend our relationship with Reliance Retail. Throughout our investment period, we have been impressed by the company’s vision and extensive work to empower retailers through digitalization, as well as by its resilience and performance in spite of the pandemic” 

Justification


  • KKR global investment firm invested an additional Rs 2,069.50 crore in Reliance Industries Ltd’s (RIL) retail arm, Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRLV), acquiring 0.25% stake in RRVL.

  • In 2020, KKR had invested Rs 5,550 crore in RRVL at a valuation of Rs 47,265 Cr, the additional investment will make KKR’s total holdings in RRVL up to 1.42% as per RIL.

  • Earlier, Qatar Investment Authority invested Rs 8,278 crore in RRVL for 1% stake, at RRVL’s valuation of 8.28 lakh Cr.

  • On 11/03/2023 RIL with a 1.04% up gain over its previous close, closed at Rs 2,474.40. 

  • The group & its subsidiaries & associates operates with 18k+ outlets, with over 27 Cr loyalty customers & has partnership with brands like Jimmy Choo, Pret A Manger etc.
content_snippet:
Private Equity giant KKR buys an additional stake in Reliance Retail Ventures worth Rs 2,069.50 crore. 


Joe Bae, co-CEO of KKR words

“We are pleased to extend our relationship with Reliance Retail. Throughout our investment period, we have been impressed by the company’s vision and extensive work to empower retailers through digitalization, as well as by its resilience and performance in spite of the pandemic” 

Justification


KKR global investment firm invested an additional Rs 2,069.50 crore in Reliance Industries Ltd’s (RIL) retail arm, Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd (RRLV), acquiring 0.25% stake in RRVL.

In 2020, KKR had invested Rs 5,550 crore in RRVL at a valuation of Rs 47,265 Cr, the additional investment will make KKR’s total holdings in RRVL up to 1.42% as per RIL.

Earlier, Qatar Investment Authority invested Rs 8,278 crore in RRVL for 1% stake, at RRVL’s valuation of 8.28 lakh Cr.

On 11/03/2023 RIL with a 1.04% up gain over its previous close, closed at Rs 2,474.40. 

The group & its subsidiaries & associates operates with 18k+ outlets, with over 27 Cr loyalty customers & has partnership with brands like Jimmy Choo, Pret A Manger etc.
takeaways:
• With the increasing valuation of Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd, the private equity investment in the company is increasing, making it a hub of private equity investments.

  • The investments might be used to expand the network of Retail business with increased focus over operational efficiency & increasing overall revenue.

  • The company, by getting huge amounts of capital, is paving the way for a surge in profitability, making the efficient use of the capital infusion by the players.

With the increasing valuation of Reliance Retail Ventures Ltd, the private equity investment in the company is increasing, making it a hub of private equity investments.

The company, by getting huge amounts of capital, is paving the way for a surge in profitability, making the efficient use of the capital infusion by the players.

title: Hero FinCorp Instant Personal Loan App: Get up to ₹ 3 Lakhs With Minimum KYC
content:
Hero FinCorp, an NBFC, launches an instant personal loan app up to 3 Lakhs with minimum KYC.

Justification

  • Hero FinCorp, one of the India’s most trusted brands launches a hassle-free instant personal loan app, allowing users to get loan up to Rs. 3 Lakhs with minimum KYC

  • The entire loan process is 100% paperless, quick & collateral free & is different for salaried & non-salaried employees.

  • The basic documentation like PAN, Aadhaar, identity proof etc. are required, which are verified online & ensures confidentiality of information & time saving.

  • Hero will check the credit ratings of the applicant, but loan would still be granted subject to T/C if the credit rating is not ideal.

  • Hero FinCorp is the fastest instant loan app that lets you repay the loan in EMIs with a tenure of up to 60 months.
content_snippet:
Hero FinCorp, an NBFC, launches an instant personal loan app up to 3 Lakhs with minimum KYC.

Justification

Hero FinCorp, one of the India’s most trusted brands launches a hassle-free instant personal loan app, allowing users to get loan up to Rs. 3 Lakhs with minimum KYC

The entire loan process is 100% paperless, quick & collateral free & is different for salaried & non-salaried employees.

The basic documentation like PAN, Aadhaar, identity proof etc. are required, which are verified online & ensures confidentiality of information & time saving.

Hero will check the credit ratings of the applicant, but loan would still be granted subject to T/C if the credit rating is not ideal.

Hero FinCorp is the fastest instant loan app that lets you repay the loan in EMIs with a tenure of up to 60 months.
takeaways:
• Launching a hassle-free & collateral less instant personal loan app indicates company’s commitment towards financial inclusion & bringing about a positive change in the society.

  • In the era of digitalization & paperless processes, this app would bring profitability to Hero FinCorp, leading to long term growth of the company.
takeaways_snippet:

In the era of digitalization & paperless processes, this app would bring profitability to Hero FinCorp, leading to long term growth of the company.

title: NTPC, Nayara Energy sign MoU on green hydrogen production
content:
NTPC Green Energy Ltd (NGEL) & Nayara Energy enter into a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to explore opportunities in green space.

Mohit Bhargava, CEO of NGEL’s words

  “Green hydrogen would be a crucial element of India’s clean energy future, and with this partnership, we will explore and implement cutting-edge technologies to produce green hydrogen, contributing to a cleaner and more resilient energy landscape.”  

Justification

  • National Thermal Power Corporation (NTPC)’s renewable energy arm, NTPC Green Energy Ltd. & Nayara Energy, an oil refining & marketing company, have entered into an MoU for exploring opportunities in the green hydrogen & energy space.

  • The MoU aims to create green hydrogen for Nayara Energy's use, expedite decarbonization, and reduce the carbon footprint.

  • Nayara Energy, the owner of India’s second-largest single-site refinery at Vadinar, Gujarat with a capacity of 20 million metric tons p.a (mmtpa) has 6k+ outlets & its service offering ranges from refining to retail.

  • Leading power producer NTPC aims for 60 GW of RE capacity by the year 2032 and is currently working on a pipeline of 20+ GW, out of which over 3 GW is operational capacity.

title: CIAL generates 312 million units of solar power, cuts two lakh tonnes of emissions
content:
Cochin International Airport Ltd generates 312 million units of solar power, cuts two lakh tonnes of emissions. 


  • Cochin International Airport Ltd (CIAL) also plans to achieve total green status in airport operations by setting up effluent treatment plant facilities.

  • The airport authority plans on setting up a pilot-size green hydrogen plant in collaboration with Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) on CIAL’s land & use solar power.

  • Mr. Pai, deputy general manager of the company, recalled that in 2012, power tariff for CIAL went up from ₹4 a unit to ₹7.

  • The company made its first entry into solar power in 2013 with a 100 KWp solar PV plant on top of the international arrival block along with several other plants, totaling to ₹62 crore investment in the venture in 2015.

  • Later, the company also introduced agri photovoltaics to optimize land use with farming. This system cools solar panels, prevents weed growth, recycles panel cleaning water for irrigation, yields safe-to-eat vegetables, and generates revenue.
content_snippet:
Cochin International Airport Ltd generates 312 million units of solar power, cuts two lakh tonnes of emissions. 


Cochin International Airport Ltd (CIAL) also plans to achieve total green status in airport operations by setting up effluent treatment plant facilities.

The airport authority plans on setting up a pilot-size green hydrogen plant in collaboration with Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited (BPCL) on CIAL’s land & use solar power.

Mr. Pai, deputy general manager of the company, recalled that in 2012, power tariff for CIAL went up from ₹4 a unit to ₹7.

The company made its first entry into solar power in 2013 with a 100 KWp solar PV plant on top of the international arrival block along with several other plants, totaling to ₹62 crore investment in the venture in 2015.

Later, the company also introduced agri photovoltaics to optimize land use with farming. This system cools solar panels, prevents weed growth, recycles panel cleaning water for irrigation, yields safe-to-eat vegetables, and generates revenue.
takeaways:
• The increasing solar generation capacity of the company along with focusing on green energy concept highlights strong operational efforts driving the company towards profitability.

  • The setting up of the new plant, in collaboration with BPCL, will boost the airport company’s revenue & will lead to increased goodwill.
takeaways_snippet:
The increasing solar generation capacity of the company along with focusing on green energy concept highlights strong operational efforts driving the company towards profitability.

The setting up of the new plant, in collaboration with BPCL, will boost the airport company’s revenue & will lead to increased goodwill.

title: Reliance Retail total debt up 73%; RRVL expected to continue pumping money
content:
Reliance Retail Ltd (RRL) borrowed a Non-current long term loan worth Rs 32,303 crore from banks in the FY 2022-23, with debt-to-equity up from 1.35 in FY 22 to 1.91 in FY 23.

Justification

  • According to the experts, the expansion of the company is primarily funded through debt.

  • RRL also raised Rs 13,304 Cr in long-term debt from the holding company Reliance Retail Ventures Limited (RRVL) increasing its total debt up to 70,943 Cr as per the Annual report 2023.

  • The debt has been raised as Rs 11,459 Cr as short term and Rs 1,599 Cr as working capital.

  • The forecast is that the funding from the holding company(RRVL) to the subsidiary (RRL)  is likely to increase in the future

  • Reliance Industries raised Rs 8,278 crore by diluting a 0.99% stake in RRVL and is likely to dilute another 8-10%  over the next few months ahead of the proposed IPO of RRVL.
content_snippet:
Reliance Retail Ltd (RRL) borrowed a Non-current long term loan worth Rs 32,303 crore from banks in the FY 2022-23, with debt-to-equity up from 1.35 in FY 22 to 1.91 in FY 23.

Justification

According to the experts, the expansion of the company is primarily funded through debt.

RRL also raised Rs 13,304 Cr in long-term debt from the holding company Reliance Retail Ventures Limited (RRVL) increasing its total debt up to 70,943 Cr as per the Annual report 2023.

The debt has been raised as Rs 11,459 Cr as short term and Rs 1,599 Cr as working capital.

The forecast is that the funding from the holding company(RRVL) to the subsidiary (RRL)  is likely to increase in the future

Reliance Industries raised Rs 8,278 crore by diluting a 0.99% stake in RRVL and is likely to dilute another 8-10%  over the next few months ahead of the proposed IPO of RRVL.
takeaways:
• The infusion of the capital in the form of debt will be leveraging the company as well as taking benefits of the tax implications over borrowed fund to increase profitability.

  • Reliance Retail Ltd’s expansion capital will be fulfilled, leading to efficient growth operations & increased profitability.
takeaways_snippet:
The infusion of the capital in the form of debt will be leveraging the company as well as taking benefits of the tax implications over borrowed fund to increase profitability.

Reliance Retail Ltd’s expansion capital will be fulfilled, leading to efficient growth operations & increased profitability.

title: LIC Housing Finance, HDB cut fundraise amount due to volatile market
content:
Private sector fundraisers such as LIC Housing Finance & HDB cut the fundraising amount due to the volatile market.

Senior executive at a bond placement firm’s words

The issuers are looking for stability. However, the rising crude oil prices and ups and downs in global markets have made the outlook uncertain

Justification

  • HBD Financial services, earlier with the intent of raising up to Rs 1800 Cr through 23-months Non-Convertible debentures, now raised only Rs 720 Cr.

  • The company’s had a base issue size of Rs. 600 Cr plus a green shoe option worth Rs 1200 Cr, coupon fixed at 7.7% with ‘AAA’ ratings from CRISIL for Rs, 20000 Cr worth NCDs, assigned on 1/09/2023.

  • LIC Housing Finance, another giant mortgage lender raised Rs 941 crore against an indicative amount of Rs 1,500 crore through 59 month maturity, privately placed bond with fixed coupon of 7.7%.

  • LIC had a base issue size of Rs 500 crore, plus a green shoe option of Rs 1,000 crore.

  • According to market dealers, smaller bond issuances with lower fundraising amounts are trending against big-ticket fundraise.

  • Varroc Engineering raised Rs 250 Cr through a five-year bond with a coupon of 9.2%. Its NCDs carry an “A+” rating from India Ratings.

  • Spandana Sphoorty, a MicroFinance company, issued Rs 100 crore worth of 18-month debentures at a 10.43 % coupon rate having an 'A' rating from India Ratings, according to bond dealers.

  • The bond dealers are sticking with pre-determined coupon rates, due to high volatility of yield, leading the yield to move up & down by 25-50 basis points in a week.

  • The issuers will only take up higher amounts only if the yield (coupon rates) are near the pre-determined level.
content_snippet:
Private sector fundraisers such as LIC Housing Finance & HDB cut the fundraising amount due to the volatile market.

Senior executive at a bond placement firm’s words

The issuers are looking for stability. However, the rising crude oil prices and ups and downs in global markets have made the outlook uncertain


Justification

HBD Financial services, earlier with the intent of raising up to Rs 1800 Cr through 23-months Non-Convertible debentures, now raised only Rs 720 Cr.

The company’s had a base issue size of Rs. 600 Cr plus a green shoe option worth Rs 1200 Cr, coupon fixed at 7.7% with ‘AAA’ ratings from CRISIL for Rs, 20000 Cr worth NCDs, assigned on 1/09/2023.

LIC Housing Finance, another giant mortgage lender raised Rs 941 crore against an indicative amount of Rs 1,500 crore through 59 month maturity, privately placed bond with fixed coupon of 7.7%.

LIC had a base issue size of Rs 500 crore, plus a green shoe option of Rs 1,000 crore.

According to market dealers, smaller bond issuances with lower fundraising amounts are trending against big-ticket fundraise.

Varroc Engineering raised Rs 250 Cr through a five-year bond with a coupon of 9.2%. Its NCDs carry an “A+” rating from India Ratings.

Spandana Sphoorty, a MicroFinance company, issued Rs 100 crore worth of 18-month debentures at a 10.43 % coupon rate having an 'A' rating from India Ratings, according to bond dealers.

The bond dealers are sticking with pre-determined coupon rates, due to high volatility of yield, leading the yield to move up & down by 25-50 basis points in a week.

The issuers will only take up higher amounts only if the yield (coupon rates) are near the pre-determined level.
takeaways:
• The bond issuers are taking up a conservative approach for bond issuance, dealing with small-tickets fundraise due to yield volatility.

  •  Raising of capital through bonds will aid companies like HBD Financial services, LIC etc. in fulfilling capital requirements & saving on tax in the form of interest payments.

  • This implies increased overall profitability & growth prospects for the company.
takeaways_snippet:
The bond issuers are taking up a conservative approach for bond issuance, dealing with small-tickets fundraise due to yield volatility.

 Raising of capital through bonds will aid companies like HBD Financial services, LIC etc. in fulfilling capital requirements & saving on tax in the form of interest payments.

This implies increased overall profitability & growth prospects for the company.

title: CSKCL to borrow Rs 350 cr for expansion of business and working capital requirement
content:
Chennai Super Kings Cricket Ltd, the Franchise owner of IPL team Chennai Super Kings, set to borrow INR 350 Cr for business expansion.

  • The company proposes to borrow the loan in the form of term loans, debentures, bonds & other instruments in India & foreign currencies for business expansion & working capital requirements.

  • The company is seeking approval from their shareholders to increase their borrowing limit from INR 250 Cr to INR 350 Cr, according to its Annual Report for FY 2022-23.

  • The company recently acquired ownership of franchises Jo burg Super Kings in Cricket South Africa (CSA) T20 League and Texas Super Kings in Major League Cricket in The USA

  • It also introduced ‘Talent Management’ as a new line of business, owing to the commercial interest of talented professionals of Cricket.

  • The company reported a 16.2% decline in its total revenue at INR 292.34 crore for FY23 from INR 349.14 crore in the previous fiscal. 

  • The net profit for FY23 stood at Rs 52.17 crore, up from Rs 31.54 crore in the last fiscal, with a 65.4% increase. The company’s cost of operations stood at Rs 201. 61 crore, compared to Rs 281.49 crore.
content_snippet:
Chennai Super Kings Cricket Ltd, the Franchise owner of IPL team Chennai Super Kings, set to borrow INR 350 Cr for business expansion.

The company proposes to borrow the loan in the form of term loans, debentures, bonds & other instruments in India & foreign currencies for business expansion & working capital requirements.

The company is seeking approval from their shareholders to increase their borrowing limit from INR 250 Cr to INR 350 Cr, according to its Annual Report for FY 2022-23.

The company recently acquired ownership of franchises Jo burg Super Kings in Cricket South Africa (CSA) T20 League and Texas Super Kings in Major League Cricket in The USA

It also introduced ‘Talent Management’ as a new line of business, owing to the commercial interest of talented professionals of Cricket.

The company reported a 16.2% decline in its total revenue at INR 292.34 crore for FY23 from INR 349.14 crore in the previous fiscal. 

The net profit for FY23 stood at Rs 52.17 crore, up from Rs 31.54 crore in the last fiscal, with a 65.4% increase. The company’s cost of operations stood at Rs 201. 61 crore, compared to Rs 281.49 crore.
takeaways:
• The borrowed fund will fulfil immediate working capital requirements of the company ensuring smooth day-to-day operations of the company, along with company saving amount on account of tax payments.

  • Smooth operations increase growth prospects & profitability in the future.

  • The company’s team captained by M.S Dhoni has one of the highest brand values amongst all IPL Teams, which makes the company an attractive investment.
